AI Memory Systems — Landscape

Comprehensive list of memory layers, framework-embedded memory, platform features, research systems, retrieval-as-memory hybrids, file-backed paradigms, knowledge-graph platforms, vector-DB infrastructure, and enterprise-search adjacencies. The largest section is at the bottom — recent method papers without distinct products, organised by memory category. Sourced from arxiv.org/abs/2512.13564 (Memory in the Age of AI Agents survey), the Agent-Memory-Paper-List, Awesome-GraphMemory, and the LongMemEval / LoCoMo / ConvoMem leaderboards.
T1 Battle-tested — production at scale, multiple commercial customers T2 Established — mature OSS framework with adoption, or major platform feature T3 Peer-reviewed — published at NeurIPS / EMNLP / ACL / ICLR / CHI etc., often with implementation T4 Preprint — arxiv-only, no peer review yet, may have repo T5 Theoretical / informal — blog post, tweet, talk, RFC, no paper
System Memory model Storage Retrieval Persistence Update Unit Governance Conflict res. What it does & what's distinct Claims / benefits Modalities Created Latest release License GitHub Mindshare Citations Funding Customers / scale Pricing Compliance Data handling Deployment HQ Founders / pedigree Memory volume / scale Performance Reproducibility Code/weights release Pros Cons Project & sources
Dedicated memory layers
Standalone services whose entire product is memory for AI agents. They sit between your agent code and durable storage — handling extraction, governance, contradiction resolution, and recall via API. The "database vendor" analogue for the memory tier: you bring an agent, they bring durability.
Mem0 Vector + graph + KV (hybrid) vectorgraphkv similaritygraph-traversalextraction-pull long-term extraction factepisode opaque LLM-arbitrate ↗ Universal memory layer for AI agents. Three concurrent stores (vector + graph + KV); LLM-extracted facts; concurrent retrieval via ThreadPoolExecutor. ~51k★. $24M Series A (Oct 2025) at $150M. Exclusive memory provider for AWS Agent SDK. Reports 26% improvement over OpenAI on LOCOMO; 91% lower p95 latency vs full-context. text ↗ 2023-06 openclaw-v1.0.11 2026-04-29 ↗ Apache-2.0 ↗ 54.9k★ +1.6k/mo↗ Python 12 inbound 57 HN · 4 press↗ searched not found ↗ $24M total $150M val Series A · 2025-10↗ 1-10 ppl AWS (exclusive memory provider for Agent SDK), 80k+ developer signups, enterprise unnamed↗ Free + paid ↗ SOC 2 Type II, HIPAA-ready ↗ Trains on de-identified/aggregated data (see privacy policy); enterprise controls not publicly detailed ↗ Both ↗ US ↗ YC S24; co-founders Taranjeet Singh (built first GPT app store to 1M+ users) and Deshraj Yadav … ↗ 1B+ memories (vendor blog claims; 80k+ dev signups) ↗ 30 ConvoMem
91.6 LoCoMo ⚠
~26% imp LMES ⚠↗		 searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ Hybrid (vector + graph + KV) gives the most architectural flexibility of any memory layer; AWS Agent SDK exclusivity and 51k★ make it the field's de-facto reference. LOCOMO benchmark numbers were publicly disputed by Zep in counter-analysis; LLM-extraction approach risks dropping facts that don't fit the prompt. GitHub mem0ai/mem0 arxiv 2504.19413 Graph Memory docs research page AWS architecture
Letta / MemGPT Tiered: core / recall / archival kvvector similarityinjectionagentic long-term agent-controlled episodesummaryfact inspectable LLM-arbitrate ↗ LLM-as-Operating-System paradigm — model manages its own memory, paging between core context, recall buffer, and archival store. UC Berkeley origin; transitioning to letta_v1_agent. 21.8k★. $10M seed at $70M (Sep 2024). Reports ~83.2% on LongMemEval; ~74.0% on LoCoMo with gpt-4o-mini using files-as-memory. text ↗ 2023-10 0.16.7 2026-03-31 ↗ Apache-2.0 ↗ 22.5k★ +151/mo↗ Python searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic $10M total $70M val Seed · 2024-09↗ $1M ARR 11-50 ppl No named enterprise customers disclosed↗ Free + paid ↗ no data ↗ searched not found ↗ Both ↗ US ↗ UC Berkeley Sky Computing Lab spin-off; co-founders Charles Packer and Sarah Wooders (both Berk… ↗ no scale claim ↗ 74.0% LoCoMo
83.2% LMES↗		 not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Most architecturally principled — explicit OS-style tiering makes capacity behavior predictable; UC Berkeley origin + active v1 maturity. Tier configuration adds operational complexity vs flat vector memory; latency is sensitive to paging decisions on long histories. GitHub letta-ai/letta Letta docs v1 agent blog agent memory blog benchmarking
Zep & Graphiti Bi-temporal knowledge graph graph graph-traversalsimilarity long-term append-only episodefact auditable bi-temporal versioning ↗ Bi-temporal knowledge graph (event time + ingestion time). Strong on chronological reasoning and contradiction tracking. Graphiti is the open-source core. Graphiti at 24.3k★. Published counter-analysis disputing Mem0's LOCOMO results. $2.3M raised Apr 2024. text ↗ 2024-08 v0.29.0 2026-04-27 ↗ Apache-2.0 ↗ 25.7k★ +137/mo↗ Python 3 inbound searched not found ↗ $3M total Seed (additional) · 2024-04↗ 11-50 ppl No named customers disclosed, 30x infra scale in summer 2025↗ Free + paid ↗ SOC 2 Type II, HIPAA (BAA available on Enterprise) ↗ Never trains on customer data; BYOK supported (BYOM/BYOC/BYOC-VPC options available) ↗ Managed-only ↗ US ↗ YC W24; founder Daniel Chalef (serial entrepreneur; previously founded KnowledgeTree; ex-SparkP… ↗ no scale claim ↗ 84% LoCoMo ⚠↗ searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ Bi-temporal graph captures event time + ingestion time, making contradiction tracking and chronological reasoning correct by construction. KG storage is heavier than vector for the same data volume; smaller funding base than Mem0 ($2.3M vs $24M). GitHub getzep/graphiti arxiv 2501.13956 Graphiti OSS Neo4j writeup 20k stars / MCP
Cognee Knowledge graph + ECL pipeline graph graph-traversalsimilarity long-term extraction factdocument inspectable LLM-arbitrate ↗ "Extract–Cognify–Load" pipeline that turns raw input into a typed, queryable knowledge graph for agent recall. 14.7k★. €7.5M seed Feb 2026. Reports #1 on LongMemEval, LoCoMo, and ConvoMem (self-reported, late 2025). 76.69% LMES (Temporal Reasoning)↗		 not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Simple universal API surface — wraps memory in a single SDK call without forcing extraction/retrieval choices; YC-backed. Architectural opacity is the cost of simplicity — limited control over structure or eviction; small team. GitHub supermemoryai/supermemory research page org repos (26) SMFS (agent filesystem)
Memvid Single-file append-only Smart Frames file similarity long-term append-only chunkdocument inspectable append (no resolution) ↗ Single-file memory layer (one .mv2 file). No DB, no server. Append-only sequence of immutable Smart Frames with timestamps + checksums. Native Rust core (rewritten from Python). Sub-millisecond ops (0.025ms P50, 0.075ms P99). Reports +35% on LoCoMo. 10–100× speedup vs the prior Python implementation. text ↗ 2025-05 v2.0.139 2026-03-13 ↗ Apache-2.0 ↗ 15.3k★ +149/mo↗ Rust no data — PyPI pkg not found i 1 press n/a (open-sourc jobs ↗ not applicable — not academic searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ OSS (Apache-2.0); free — no commercial pricing found ↗ not applicable — not commercial ↗ not applicable — not commercial (all data stays in user's .mv2 file) ↗ Self-hosted only (single .mv2 file; no cloud service) ↗ searched not found ↗ co-founders include Mohamed Omar (CEO) and one unnamed co-founder; team is anonymous on GitHub org ↗ fits in single .mv2 file (no upper bound claimed) ↗ +35% ove LoCoMo↗ not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Single-file .mv2 store you can git commit — memory becomes a first-class artifact, not a database; sub-ms reads from a Rust core. Single-file format means scale ceilings; non-standard format limits ecosystem interop. GitHub memvid/memvid docs claude-brain (Claude Code plugin)
Memobase User profile + event timeline relationalvector injectionsimilarity long-term extractionappend-only profileepisode inspectable none specified ↗ User-profile-based long-term memory. Per-user profile + event timeline. API + Python/Node/Go SDKs + MCP server. Profile-centric rather than fact-centric. Sub-100ms retrieval. Cross-tool memory passport for Claude / Cursor / MCP-compatible agents. text ↗ 2024-09 no releases (no tagged releases on GitHub as of research date) ↗ Apache-2.0 ↗ 2.7k★ +10/mo↗ Python 1 inbound not applicable — not academic searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ Free + paid (Memobase Cloud free tier for testing; pay-as-you-go for production) ↗ searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ Both (self-hosted Docker + Memobase Cloud managed) ↗ searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ no scale claim ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper User-profile-centric model is closer to how product teams think about user memory than fact-extraction approaches; cross-tool memory passport via MCP. OpenMemory Cognitive memory engine vectorgraph similaritygraph-traversal long-term extraction fact user-controllable none specified ↗ Open-source cognitive memory engine for LLMs and agents. text ↗ 2025-10 v1.2.3 2025-12-12 ↗ Apache-2.0 ↗ 4.1k★ +165/mo↗ TypeScript 4.1k GitHub stars; +165/mo; below detection threshold for HN/press ↗ not applicable — not academic searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ OSS (Apache-2.0 self-hosted); self-hosted cost ~$5-8/mo for 100k memories vs $60-120 for SaaS ↗ searched not found ↗ not applicable — self-hosted OSS (all data stays on user's infrastructure) ↗ Self-hosted (local + Docker; no cloud service found) ↗ searched not found ↗ Contributors include Morven, Muhammad Fiaz, Peter Chung, Brett Ammeson, Joseph Goksu; no named corporate founders ↗ no scale claim ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Cross-tool memory protocol — designed to make memory portable between agents that today silo it. SuperLocalMemory Local-geometric / privacy-first / no LLM scoring vectorgraph similaritygraph-traversal long-term extraction factepisode user-controllable none specified ↗ Cloud-free agent memory using information-geometric retrieval (Fisher-Rao distance + BM25 + entity graph + temporal fusion via 4-channel RRF) instead of LLM calls for scoring and lifecycle. Mode A is the only system to cross 70% LoCoMo with zero API dependency. 74.8% LoCoMo (Mode A, zero LLM), 87.7% (Mode C). AGPL v3. EU AI Act compliance framing. arxiv 2603.14588. text ↗ searched not found ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ EU AI Act compliant framing (explicitly stated) ↗ All data stays on user's machine; no cloud; no API dependency ↗ Local-only (no Docker; no graph DB; no API keys; CPU-only; Mode A zero-LLM) ↗ searched not found ↗ Varun Pratap Bhardwaj (creator; researcher; Qualixar initiative) ↗ no scale claim ↗ 74.8% LoCoMo (Mode A — zero LLM)
87.7% LoCoMo (Mode C — LLM-assisted)↗		 not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Local-first by default keeps PII out of the cloud; lightweight footprint suits on-device agents. Local-only constraint limits scale and cross-device sync; smaller ecosystem than cloud-first peers. superlocalmemory.com
VEKTOR Memory Local-SQLite + AUDN-loop + TypeScript-native relationalvector similarity cross-session extraction factepisode inspectable LLM-arbitrate ↗ Node.js/TypeScript-native memory layer. SQLite + local Transformers.js embeddings (~80MB model via WebAssembly). AUDN loop (Add/Update/Delete/None) resolves contradictions before write rather than at retrieval — read-after-write consistency. Only production-grade memory library targeting Node.js agents specifically with no cloud dependency. Python port on roadmap. text ↗ searched not found ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic searched not found ↗ unknown ppl No identifiable standalone memory-layer startup found↗ $9/month subscription OR one-time $159 commercial license (activates on up to 3 machines) ↗ not applicable — local-only (no cloud; data stays on user's server) ↗ searched not found ↗ Local-only (Node.js; Transformers.js via WebAssembly; SQLite; no cloud infrastructure) ↗ unknown ↗ searched not found ↗ no scale claim ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Vector-native memory framework with built-in embedding pipeline; minimal setup for new agents. Vector-only — no graph or KV layer; smaller ecosystem. DEV writeup
Memory Store Cross-agent shared episodic + preference memory vector similarity cross-session extraction episodefact opaque none specified ↗ YC P26 (Spring 2026). Unified memory layer so all agents on a team share one persistent context — meetings, Claude sessions, Slack threads all become queryable. Solves the "goldfish brain" problem of every session starting from zero. YC P26. Very early stage. text ↗ searched not found ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ no scale claim ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Lightweight key-value memory abstraction; minimal complexity for simple agents. Interloom Enterprise tacit-knowledge memory tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description long-term tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description none specified ↗ German startup. Captures how real work is performed across enterprise systems and teams; converts process knowledge into a persistent memory layer agents use to automate complex workflows. Targets the tacit-knowledge gap that agents currently lack. $16.5M seed (March 2026, DN Capital). Customers: Zurich Insurance, JLL, Fiege, Commerzbank, Volkswagen. Processing millions of enterprise cases. text ↗ 2026-03 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo 2 press ~15 (seed-stage jobs ↗ not applicable — not academic $16M total Seed · 2026-03↗ 11-50 ppl Zurich Insurance, JLL, Fiege, Commerzbank, Volkswagen↗ Enterprise pricing; no setup fees; fixed-price per interaction (contact sales) ↗ ISO 27001:2022, ISO 42001, TISAX certification roadmap in progress; runs on Azure (inherits Azure compliance) ↗ Data protected in transit and at rest; single-tenant isolation available; Azure infrastructure ↗ Three modes: managed multi-tenant cloud; dedicated single-tenant; BYOM (bring your own model/endpoints) ↗ Germany (Munich) German startup; founder Fabian Jakobi (serial entrepreneur; previously founded Boxplot acquired… ↗ processing millions of enterprise cases (vendor claim) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Memory-as-context-orchestration positioning — focuses on what to inject when, not just what to store. Newer entrant with limited track record; orchestration framing may overlap with what LangGraph and similar tools already address. interloom.com Fortune
Nyne Human-profile / people-graph memory graph graph-traversalinjection long-term extraction profilefact opaque none specified ↗ Deploys agents across the web to build structured profiles of individuals from public digital footprint, then feeds the people graph into AI agents as contextual memory so they understand users before any conversation starts. $5.3M seed (March 2026, Wischoff Ventures + South Park Commons). MemWal (Walrus) Decentralised / verifiable on-chain agent memory proprietary exact-match lifelong append-only documentepisode auditable append (no resolution) ↗ Walrus is a Sui-blockchain decentralised storage protocol; MemWal is its AI-facing SDK that stores encrypted agent memory on-chain — portable, verifiable, shareable across agent systems with no single-vendor lock-in. Targets production agents needing auditability. $140M private token sale ($WAL, 2025) at ~$2B protocol valuation. Plugins for OpenClaw and NemoClaw. text ↗ 2025-01 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic $140M total $2.0B val Private Token Sale · 2025-01↗ searched not found ↗ Comparable to centralized models (per Mysten Labs; $WAL token-based usage) ↗ Append-only verifiable on-chain storage; auditability by design; no traditional compliance certs found ↗ Encrypted agent memory stored on Sui blockchain; portable and verifiable; user controls keys ↗ Decentralised blockchain storage (Sui blockchain via Walrus protocol; SDK in beta) ↗ US (Palo Alto — Mysten Labs HQ) ↗ Mysten Labs: Evan Cheng (CEO) + Sam Blackshear (CTO) + Adeniyi Abiodun + George Danezis + Kostas Chalkias (ex-Faceb… ↗ no scale claim (blockchain-backed; no upper limit stated) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Walrus decentralized-storage backed memory — first memory layer with on-chain durability. Jamba (AI21 Labs) SSM-Transformer hybrid + 256K KV-bypass context kv-cacheparametric attentioncache-lookup session read-only kv-token opaque none specified ↗ Israeli production model. Interleaves Mamba SSM layers with Transformer attention (1:7 ratio) + MoE blocks every 2 layers. SSM layers maintain fixed-size recurrent hidden state — compressed long-context memory bypassing growing KV cache. 256K context on single 8-GPU node. 3× throughput vs Mixtral 8x7B on long contexts. Jamba Reasoning 3B runs 250K-context inference on a laptop. text ↗ 2020-11 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic $608M total Series D · 2025-05↗ $58M ARR 201-500 ppl Boston Consulting Group, Bain & Company, Moderna, Amgen, Gates Foundation…↗ Free trial ($10 credit, no CC required); pay-as-you-go per token; enterprise custom pricing with VPC/on-prem ↗ SOC 2; ISO 27001/17/18 certified; GDPR; enterprise deployment enables GLBA + HIPAA-ready ↗ Zero data visibility option via private VPC or on-prem; Microsoft Azure does not share prompt/output data with AI21 ↗ Both (AI21 Studio cloud; Azure/AWS Bedrock/GCP Vertex AI; private VPC; on-premises) ↗ Israel (Tel Aviv) Tel Aviv-based; co-founders Yoav Shoham (Stanford AI professor emeritus) and Amnon Shashua (Heb… ↗ 256K context on single 8-GPU node; Reasoning 3B fits 25… ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Hybrid Transformer/Mamba architecture means memory cost grows sub-quadratically — long-context inference is genuinely cheaper. Closed product; primarily Chinese-market; documentation in Chinese. project page Open-AutoGLM
Framework-embedded memory
Memory subsystems shipped inside a broader agent orchestration framework. Memory isn't the product — the framework is — and the memory module is one component alongside tool-calling, planning, and routing. Composition over best-in-class: convenient when you've already chosen the framework, limiting when you outgrow what's built in.

LangGraph Persistence Checkpoints + Store System relationalkv exact-matchinjection cross-session overwriteappend-only episodefact inspectable overwrite ↗ Distinct from LangMem. Built-in checkpointer saves graph state per super-step (short-term, thread-scoped). Store System adds long-term hierarchical key-value memory across threads with optional vector search + TTL. Postgres / Mongo / Redis stores for production. text ↗ 2024-01 (LangGraph v0.1 released Jan 2024; long-term memory persistence added Oct 2024) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo 5 inbound not applicable — not academic $160M total (LangChain parent); $1.2B valuation; Series B Oct 2025 ↗ searched not found ↗ OSS free (MIT); LangGraph Platform (now LangSmith Deployment as of Oct 2025): Free Developer plan 100k nodes/mo; Pl… ↗ SOC 2 Type II; GDPR; HIPAA (inherits LangChain/LangSmith compliance posture) ↗ Same as LangSmith: encrypted in transit/at rest; EU and US regions; enterprise self-hosted option ↗ Both (LangGraph OSS self-hosted; LangSmith Deployment cloud managed or self-hosted) ↗ US (San Francisco, CA) ↗ LangChain; Harrison Chase (Harvard CS; ex-Robust Intelligence) ↗ no scale claim ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Persistent state for graph agents — checkpointing and resumability are first-class, not bolted on. LangGraph-shaped; persistence model is general but most idiomatic when paired with LangChain memory. LangGraph persistence docs Store System langgraph-checkpoint
smolagents Memory In-process Python list (no persistence) kv injection session append-only episode inspectable append (no resolution) ↗ Hugging Face barebones agent framework. Memory lives as a Python list; no built-in persistence. Step callbacks let you mutate memory between steps. Persistence is left to the user — community proposals exist for "memory bank"-style additions. text ↗ 2024-12 v1.24.0 2026-01-16 ↗ Apache-2.0 ↗ 27.1k★ +104/mo↗ Python 582984/mo PyPI (smolagents pkg ↗ not applicable — not academic Hugging Face (parent) raised $400M total across 8 rounds from 34 investors ↗ searched not found ↗ OSS free (Apache-2.0); Hugging Face Enterprise Hub starts at $20/user/mo for managed services ↗ Hugging Face Enterprise: SOC 2 Type II; GDPR premium coverage; continuous security scanning via VirusTotal ↗ OSS self-hosted: data handling left to deployer; Hugging Face Inference Endpoints: standard HF data processing term… ↗ Both (OSS self-hosted; Hugging Face Inference Endpoints / Enterprise Hub managed) ↗ US (New York, NY — Hugging Face HQ) ↗ Hugging Face; Hugging Face open-source AI team (Clement Delangue CEO; Thomas Wolf CSO) ↗ no scale claim ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper HuggingFace's minimal-agent framework; tiny dependency surface; useful for embedded / edge deployments. Memory features are intentionally minimal; not the right fit for sophisticated long-term memory needs. GitHub huggingface/smolagents memory tutorial memory bank proposal #1121

Haystack Memory (deepset) Pipeline-native ChatMessageStore kv exact-matchinjection session append-only document inspectable append (no resolution) ↗ Haystack 2.x: InMemoryChatMessageStore (experimental) + ChatMessageRetriever + ChatMessageWriter components in declarative pipelines. Legacy: ConversationSummaryMemory with LLM-compressed rolling summaries. Spring AI ChatMemory Advisor-pattern Java-native memory kv injection session append-only document inspectable append (no resolution) ↗ Wraps conversation memory behind an Advisor abstraction. MessageWindowChatMemory default 20-msg sliding window (preserves system messages); pluggable ChatMemoryRepository backends (in-memory, JDBC, Cassandra, Neo4j). Memory injected as cross-cutting concern at the ChatClient level. Spring AI 1.x stable. Auto-configured by default. text ↗ 2023-09 (announced at SpringOne Sept 2023; 0.8.0 released Feb 2024; 1.0 GA May 2025) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched not applicable — not academic Part of Spring / VMware Tanzu (now Broadcom); no independent funding — corporate Broadcom product ↗ Nine of the top 10 Fortune 500 committed to VMware Cloud Foundation (Broadcom umbrella); no Spring AI-specific cust… ↗ OSS free (Apache-2.0); Tanzu Spring Enterprise subscription available for commercial support ↗ Inherits Broadcom/VMware compliance posture; Spring AI compliance templates for HIPAA, SOX, PCI-DSS available in fr… ↗ Library runs in-process; data handling entirely under deployer control; Spring framework adds no telemetry by default ↗ Both (on-prem, any cloud, or VMware Tanzu managed platform) ↗ US (San Jose, CA — Broadcom HQ) ↗ Mark Pollack (Spring AI creator; Broadcom/VMware Spring team); part of the broader Spring open-source community und… ↗ no scale claim ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Brings Java enterprise stacks into modern AI memory without leaving Spring. DSPy History Signature-field / prompt-injection contract kv injection session read-only document inspectable not applicable ↗ dspy.History primitive — typed field holding messages: list[dict] that slots into any Signature. No persistent memory of its own; purely a structured context-injection contract. DSPy's optimisation loop (MIPRO, BootstrapFewShot) treats historical turns as trainable few-shot structure. Open source. Inngest AgentKit — Network State Durable-network-state / event-driven kv exact-match session overwrite kv-token inspectable overwrite (KV store, last write wins) ↗ Memory as Network State — shared key-value store owned by the multi-agent network, readable by router / agents / tools. Persisted durably via Inngest infrastructure. Memory writes decoupled from response latency: agent responds immediately, background Inngest function handles DB write durably with retries. Open source. TypeScript. Rivet Memory Node (Ironclad) Visual-graph first-class memory node kv exact-match session overwrite kv-token inspectable overwrite (stable-ID overwrite on save) ↗ Open-source visual AI programming environment. Memory is a first-class graph node: stores text block, supports stable IDs (saving same ID overwrites enabling updates), injects stored text into downstream nodes when relevant. Memory is designed and debugged visually. AGiXT Adaptive Memory Adaptive provider-spanning + plugin storage vector similarity long-term extractionoverwrite episodefact inspectable LLM-arbitrate ↗ Open-source AI automation platform. Routes between short-term and long-term memory adaptively across any LLM provider; plugin system for storage backends. Memory managed at the instruction-management layer — task context, instruction state, conversation history as unified agent state. Supports 20+ LLM providers. Voiceflow Memory Sliding-window buffer (vf_memory) kv injection session overwrite fact user-controllable last-write-wins for vf_memory string variable; no published conflict resolution policy ↗ Chatbot platform. Tracks last 25 turns (configurable up to 100) as labelled assistant/user transcript stored in vf_memory string variable. Optional indefinite chat persistence across browser sessions. Flowise Memory Buffer + Buffer-Window + Conversation-Summary nodes vectorkv similarityinjection session append-onlyconsolidation episodesummary inspectable append-only for Buffer/Buffer-Window; Summary Buffer consolidates older turns via LLM compression; no explicit conf… ↗ Visual LangChain canvas. LangChain-native memory nodes (Buffer, Buffer Window, Conversation Summary Buffer) backed by internal chat_message DB table. Buffer Window limits to last K interactions; Summary Buffer compresses older turns while keeping recent verbatim. Open source. Closed ecosystem; lock-in risk is highest of the framework-embedded options. memory docs
Vapi (voice agents) Structured workflow state during call kv injection session overwrite fact inspectable overwrite (structured variables) ↗ Voice agents. Externalises memory from LLM into structured call state — variables rather than model recall — reducing hallucinations. Squad mode passes context across multi-agent assistant hand-offs in same call. Usage-based (per call minute). Call-scoped only. voice + text ↗ 2023-01 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo 2 press ~50 (small star jobs ↗ not applicable — not academic $22M total $130M val Series A · 2024-12↗ $8M ARR 51-200 ppl No named customers disclosed, ~30 per early tracking↗ Pay-per-use ↗ SOC 2 Type II; HIPAA (hipaaEnabled flag; only HIPAA-compliant services used when enabled); PCI DSS (pciEnabled flag… ↗ PHI processed but not stored when HIPAA mode enabled; PCI-compliant masking of payment data; recording consent plan… ↗ Both ↗ US ↗ Vapi AI (Nikhil Gupta & Jordan Dearsley, co-founders; YC W23) ↗ no scale claim — state lives in call variables ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Voice-first agent platform with memory tuned for telephony / IVR; mature voice infrastructure. Voice-only positioning; not useful for non-voice agents. vapi.ai

Stack AI Enterprise-KB RAG (no agent memory store) vector similarity long-term overwrite document auditable not applicable (read-only KB; no agent memory write) ↗ Connects enterprise knowledge sources (SharePoint, Confluence, Notion, Drive, DBs) as memory layer with access controls, versioning, citations. Agents query at runtime; no separate session/cross-session store. Free tier; Enterprise custom. text ↗ 2022 (founded 2022 by Bernard Aceituno and Toni Rosinol; YC S23) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo 1 press ~20 (small star jobs ↗ not applicable — not academic $16.6M total; Series A 2025 from Lobby Capital; YC W23 ↗ HP, SmartAsset, Red Bull, IBM among named customers; 200+ enterprise customers in first 6 months; finance, insuranc… ↗ Free + paid ↗ SOC 2 Type II; HIPAA; GDPR-compliant ↗ SOC 2 Type II; HIPAA; GDPR; on-premise deploy available on Enterprise tier; enterprise dedicated infrastructure ↗ Both ↗ US ↗ Stack AI (Bernardo Aceituno & Antoni Rosinol, co-founders; MIT / CMU; YC W23) ↗ no scale claim — enterprise SaaS KB; undisclosed ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper No-code agent platform with memory integrated; fastest path for non-engineers. Memory abstractions are simple; sophisticated agents hit ceilings quickly. stackai.com
Platform-provider memory
First-party memory features built into a foundation-model provider's chat product or cloud platform. The provider owns user, model, and memory together — tight integration with their product, but no portability across vendors and limited control for builders. The "memory feature in ChatGPT / Claude / Gemini / Copilot" tier.
Mistral Agents API Platform-managed stateful conversation memory kv injection cross-session append-only episode opaque none specified ↗ Mistral's Agents API provides persistent conversation state across turns and sessions; runtime maintains branching conversation trees. Memory is implicit in the stateful session model rather than a separate store — agents can branch, continue prior discussions, and accumulate tool-call history without manual tracking. Vendor-reported seamless multi-step workflow support; agents handle complex task sequences across multiple sessions. text ↗ 2023 (Mistral AI founded April 2023; Agents API launched May 27 2025) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched not applicable — not academic $2.71B total raised; Series C €1.7B (ASML lead) Sep 2025; $830M debt financing Mar 2026 ↗ searched not found ↗ Free tier (la Plateforme developer API); API pay-per-token (e.g. Mistral Large $2/$6 per 1M tokens); enterprise custom ↗ ISO 27001; ISO 27701; SOC 2 Type II; GDPR-compliant (EU jurisdiction) ↗ GDPR-compliant; on-prem/self-hosted options available for data sovereignty; EU-based infrastructure ↗ Managed cloud (la Plateforme) + self-hosted Docker on-prem + Mistral Forge enterprise ↗ France (Paris) ↗ Mistral AI; co-founders Arthur Mensch (ex-Google DeepMind) Guillaume Lample and Timothée Lacroi… ↗ no scale claim ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Fully managed — developers need no external state store for session continuity; branching conversations allow parallel reasoning paths. Closed infrastructure; no published architecture detail on how memory is stored/retrieved/evicted; no peer-reviewed evaluation of memory accuracy or long-term drift. Agents API docs launch announcement
Anthropic Managed Agents — Memory Filesystem-backed cross-session persistent memory file extraction-pull cross-session agent-controlled file inspectable none specified ↗ Mounts memory as files on a managed filesystem; Claude reads and writes via the same bash/code-execution tools used for agentic tasks. Unit of storage is a file — inspectable, editable, exportable by developers. Full audit log per session. Memory beta launched April 23, 2026, two weeks after Managed Agents itself launched April 8. Rakuten reported 97% fewer first-pass errors, 27% lower cost, 34% lower latency in early-adopter usage. text ↗ 2026-04-08 (Managed Agents GA); 2026-04-23 (Memory beta added) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched not applicable — not academic Anthropic $12.4B+ raised; $40B valuation ↗ Rakuten (97% fewer first-pass errors, 27% lower cost, 34% lower latency in early use) ↗ Claude API token rates + $0.08 per session-hour for Managed Agents ↗ SOC 2 Type I, SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, ISO 42001, HIPAA (with BAA); FedRAMP in progress ↗ Enterprise/API: never trains on customer data; FedRAMP in progress; opt-out toggle for consumer plans ↗ Managed-only (hosted Anthropic infrastructure) ↗ San Francisco, CA, US ↗ Anthropic; Dario Amodei (CEO; ex-OpenAI VP Research), Daniela Amodei (President; ex-OpenAI VP Operations) ↗ no scale claim ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Filesystem metaphor aligns with Claude's existing tool-use primitives; no separate embedding pipeline; human-auditable without special tooling. Memory granularity and eviction policy not yet documented publicly; filesystem-as-memory has known scalability limits at large knowledge volumes; beta status means API surface may change. Managed Agents docs Techzine TestingCatalog
Anthropic Claude Memory File-backed + tool-driven + Auto Dream file extraction-pullinjection long-term consolidationagent-controlled factsummary user-controllable manual ↗ Memory tool API + consumer "Memory" feature + Auto Dream consolidation. Document-as-memory rather than extracted-fact-as-memory. text ↗ 2024-09 (Enterprise/Team rollout); 2026-03-02 (all users incl. free) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo 19 HN · 3 press↗ not applicable — not academic Anthropic total $12.4B+ raised; $40B valuation (Series E+ 2025) ↗ 501-1000 ppl Rakuten (reported 97% fewer errors, 27% lower cost, 34% lower latency)↗ Free + paid ↗ SOC 2 Type I, SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, ISO 42001, HIPAA (Claude for Work/API with BAA) ↗ Opt-out available (consumer Free/Pro/Max: opt-out toggle; Enterprise/API/Bedrock/Vertex: never trains on customer data; FedRAMP in progress) ↗ Managed-only ↗ US ↗ Anthropic; founded by Dario Amodei (ex-OpenAI VP Research) and Daniela Amodei (ex-OpenAI VP Ope… ↗ no scale claim ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Three-tier model (memory tool API + consumer Memory + Auto Dream) covers developer, user, and system layers; document-as-memory unit aligns with how humans organize information. Three-tier model means developers and users see different abstractions; Auto Dream consolidation is not user-controllable. memory tool docs Auto Dream guide memory import tool Wiegold guide
OpenAI ChatGPT Memory User-scoped persistent facts kv injection long-term extraction fact user-controllable LLM-arbitrate ↗ First-party memory in ChatGPT; auto-extracts persistent facts about the user across sessions. Apr 2025 expansion to chat-history referencing. text ↗ 2024-02-13 (limited rollout); GA broader rollout mid-2024 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo 26 HN · 4 press↗ not applicable — not academic OpenAI total $57B+ raised; $300B+ valuation (2025) ↗ 1000+ ppl Largest installed base, 100M+ MAU, enterprise customers unnamed↗ Free + paid ↗ SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, ISO 27017, ISO 27018, ISO 27701, HIPAA (ChatGPT for Healthcare with BAA) ↗ Opt-out available (consumer: trains by default; opt-out available; Enterprise/Team/Edu/API: never trains on customer data by default) ↗ Managed-only ↗ US ↗ OpenAI; founded by Sam Altman Greg Brockman Ilya Sutskever Wojciech Zaremba and John Schulman ↗ internal — undisclosed ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Largest installed base of memory-using AI users by orders of magnitude; cross-conversation context reads chat history automatically since Sept 2025. Memory is opaque — no inspect/edit primitive beyond the settings UI; cross-thread leakage has been a recurring privacy concern. launch announcement memory FAQ how it works (EmbraceTheRed)
Google Gemini Memory Personal Context + Personal Intelligence kv injection long-term extraction factprofile user-controllable LLM-arbitrate ↗ Personal Context setting + Personal Intelligence packing. Memory import from ChatGPT/Claude (Mar 2026). text ↗ 2024-11-20 (initial rollout to Gemini Advanced subscribers) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo 1 HN · 1 press↗ not applicable — not academic Google/Alphabet public (GOOGL); no separate Gemini funding ↗ 1000+ ppl Consumer product, Workspace enterprise customers unnamed↗ Free + paid ↗ SOC 2 Type II; ISO 27001; ISO 27017; ISO 27018; FedRAMP (Google Cloud); HIPAA; GDPR-compliant ↗ Opt-out available (consumer: trains by default; Workspace/API: never trains on customer data by default) ↗ Managed-only ↗ US ↗ Google DeepMind; corporate product led by Demis Hassabis (CEO Google DeepMind) ↗ internal — undisclosed ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Tightest integration of any memory product with personal data sources (Gmail, Drive, Calendar) where users have meaningful history. Cross-product data sharing is a trust liability; persistence model has been less clearly communicated than ChatGPT's. DataStudios writeup enterprise personalization privacy controls blog
Microsoft Copilot Memory Org / tenant-scoped (Exchange-backed) relational injection long-term extraction fact auditable overwrite ↗ Microsoft 365 Copilot memory stored in user's Exchange mailbox with compliance alignment. LinkedIn Cognitive Memory Agent Episodic + semantic + procedural (shared) vectorgraph similaritygraph-traversal long-term extraction episodefactskill opaque none specified ↗ Production memory infrastructure powering LinkedIn's Hiring Assistant and other GenAI apps. Perplexity Memory Auto-distillation across model swaps kv injection long-term extraction fact user-controllable LLM-arbitrate ↗ Auto-infers facts from conversations and surfaces them in future responses (Pro subscribers). Primarily helps repeat-search scenarios; less useful as general assistive memory than competitors. help center
Grok Memory (xAI) Preference-distillation + user-deletable kv injection long-term extraction fact user-controllable LLM-arbitrate ↗ Extracts and persists facts from past conversations; applies them to future responses. Per-memory deletion UI; user can also clear all. Beta on grok.com + iOS/Android. Not available in EU or UK. GDPR data-handling policy. Meta AI Memory Cross-platform via Accounts Center kv injection long-term extraction fact user-controllable LLM-arbitrate ↗ Stores facts from one-on-one chats and shares them across Instagram, WhatsApp, Messenger, Facebook when accounts are linked via Accounts Center. The widest cross-surface memory scope of any consumer product — propagates context across entirely separate social apps, not just devices/browsers. User-deletable memories. Non-developer surface only; no API; cross-product data flow remains a privacy concern. help center
Coding-agent memory
Memory features baked directly into AI coding products — Devin, Replit Agent, Codex CLI, v0, Bolt.new, Lovable. The memory is part of the agent UX rather than an SDK or framework you compose with. Distinct from file-backed paradigms because here the agent is autonomous; distinct from platform-provider memory because the product is the coding workflow itself.
Devin (Cognition) Server-side Knowledge + auto-Repo Knowledge kvfile injectionextraction-pull cross-session agent-controlled factdocument inspectable none specified ↗ Structured Knowledge items stored server-side. Three-tier scope: repo-pinned (always injected), global (recall by relevance), org/enterprise (visible to org members). Auto-generates Repo Knowledge from repo structure each session; reads past session trajectories for self-improvement; writes notes for recurring scheduled tasks. $175M Series B at ~$2B (2024). Devin 2.0 announced Mar 2025. text ↗ 2024-03 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched not applicable — not academic $1.1B total $10.2B val Series E · 2025-09↗ $150M ARR 51-200 ppl Goldman Sachs, Citi, Dell, Cisco, Ramp…↗ Free + paid ↗ Encryption in transit and at rest; no customer data used for training by default (Enterprise: never trains); Trust … ↗ By default no training on customer data; Enterprise: contractual prohibition on training; data retained only during… ↗ Both ↗ US ↗ Founders Fund-backed; co-founders Scott Wu (Harvard CS; 3× IOI gold medalist) Steven Hao and Wa… ↗ no scale claim ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Most aggressive memory model in the coding-agent space — explicit knowledge base + file-backed task notes + persistent terminal sessions. Memory features only available inside the Devin product; not portable to other agents. devin.ai Knowledge docs

OpenAI Codex (cloud agent) File-backed ~/.codex/memories + Chronicle file injectionextraction-pull long-term consolidationappend-only summaryepisodefact inspectable none specified ↗ Memory under ~/.codex/memories/: summaries, durable entries, recent-input logs, supporting evidence — generated in background after sessions go idle. Chronicle (opt-in macOS preview) uses screen-capture to seed memory. Off by default; opt-in via config.toml. User-scoped across sessions on the same machine. Aider Per-repo chat history file + YAML config file injection cross-session append-only episode user-controllable none specified ↗ Appends conversation turns to .aider.chat.history.md in project root. restore_chat_history defaults to false — no automatic replay into new sessions. Community /session save command for JSON state snapshots. .aider.conf.yml carries static preferences. No vector store, no server-side persistence — file-only. text ↗ 2023-05 v0.86.0 2025-08-09 Apache-2.0 ↗ 44.4k★ +1.7k/mo↗ Python 5.3M+ cumulative installs (aid 2 press n/a (open-sourc jobs ↗ not applicable — not academic no public funding ↗ 1-10 ppl No named enterprise customers, OSS CLI tool↗ OSS ↗ searched not found ↗ Local-only; no server-side data retention; opt-in anonymous analytics with no personal info ↗ Self-hostable ↗ US ↗ Paul Gauthier (solo founder; ex-founding CTO Inktomi 1996-2000; ex-Google) ↗ no scale claim ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Most established CLI coding agent; explicit project-level conventions file + repo map keeps memory inspectable. Continue.dev Memory MCP KG-backed memory via MCP graph graph-traversal long-term extraction factdocument inspectable none specified ↗ Official Memory MCP (Mar 2025) backed by a knowledge graph. Browser-runtime-only; not applicable to native or backend-heavy projects. bolt.new GitHub
Tabnine Org-level Context Engine + Tabnine Agents graphvector graph-traversalsimilarity long-term extraction documentfact opaque none specified ↗ Enterprise Context Engine builds a multi-dimensional knowledge graph from repos + CI/CD + PRs + tickets + docs at the org level. Individual completions stay session-scoped. Tabnine Agents (Nov 2025) adds team-shared agent context. Cross-session memory only on enterprise tier (org-level). text ↗ 2023-11 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo n/a (IDE plugin) 1 press ~50 (Tabnine co jobs ↗ not applicable — not academic $25M total Series B · 2023-11↗ $27M ARR 51-200 ppl no data (1M+ monthly users, thousands of orgs)↗ Free + paid ↗ SOC 2 Type II; ISO 27001; GDPR-compliant ↗ Never trains on customer data (Enterprise: fully isolated; never uses code to train shared models) ↗ Both ↗ IL ↗ Founded as Codota 2013 by Dror Weiss (CEO) and Eran Yahav (CTO; Technion professor); acquired TabNine from Jacob Ja… ↗ internal — undisclosed ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Long-running enterprise coding assistant with on-prem option; memory respects enterprise data boundaries. Slower model adoption than newer entrants; on-prem deployment is heavyweight. Context Engine docs
Replit Agent Project-files-as-memory + Temporal orchestration file injection cross-session overwrite document inspectable none specified ↗ No documented built-in persistent semantic memory store. Session duration expanded to 200 min (Agent 3); Agent 4 shifts toward persistent runtimes. Temporal used for orchestration durability, not semantic memory. Cross-session memory relies on filesystem + agent-written notes. Product adoption T1; memory-layer maturity T2 (immature). text ↗ 2018-01 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched not applicable — not academic $852M total $9.0B val Series D · 2026-03↗ $253M ARR 201-500 ppl Coinbase, Zillow, Mercedes-Benz, 85% Fortune 500 use Replit platform↗ Free + paid ↗ SOC 2 Type II ↗ Never trains on customer data for Teams/Enterprise (only public Repls used, anonymized; commercial agreement prohibits training on Customer Content) ↗ Managed-only ↗ US ↗ a16z / Coatue-backed; founders Amjad Masad (ex-Facebook) and Haya Odeh; $400M raised at $9B val… ↗ no scale claim ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Built into the Replit IDE so memory + execution + deployment are one product — agent memory is grounded in actual workspace state. Tied to Replit's hosted environment; not usable for projects living in a normal local repo. Replit Agent docs
Sweep AI GitHub-thread-as-implicit-memory file extraction-pull session read-only episode inspectable none specified ↗ No dedicated persistent memory layer. Operates as a GitHub App — reads issue + codebase fresh on each trigger. Some context implicit in the PR/issue threads it created previously. Pivoted to JetBrains plugin (2025); no cross-session memory added. text ↗ 2023 (GitHub app launched); pivoted to JetBrains plugin 2025 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo n/a (GitHub app) n/a (YC-backed jobs ↗ not applicable — not academic $500K Seed · 2023-01↗ 1-10 ppl JetBrains IDE users↗ Free (community); Pro and Team tiers; LLM cost + 5% markup model for agent usage ↗ searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ Both (GitHub App managed + JetBrains plugin local) ↗ US (San Francisco) ↗ Kevin Lu and William Zeng (co-founders); YC S23; both previously at Roblox as ML engineers ↗ no scale claim ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper GitHub-issue-to-PR agent with memory of past PRs and code-review feedback. GitHub-PR scope; less useful for interactive coding sessions. sweep.dev docs
Browser-agent memory
Browser-as-agent products where memory persists across browsing sessions and the agent operates on the user's browser context. Distinct from coding-agent memory because the environment is web pages and tabs; distinct from personal AI because the corpus is browsing history and tab content rather than notes.
Perplexity Comet Conversation across pages + browser-history memory filevector similarityextraction-pull long-term append-only episodedocument opaque none specified ↗ AI-native Chromium-based browser. Assistant operates across tabs and sessions; persistent memory of preferences; multi-step agentic tasks without leaving the browser. Memory is opt-in and server-side; enterprise memory tier separately available. Perplexity raised $500M at $14B (May 2025), then $100M at $18B (late 2025). ARR ~$200M as of Feb 2026. Comet went free across all platforms March 2026. text ↗ 2025-07-09 (limited launch Windows/macOS); 2025-10-02 (public release free worldwide) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched not applicable — not academic Perplexity $1.4B+ total raised; $21.2B valuation (Series E-6 early 2026) ↗ searched not found ↗ Free + paid ↗ SOC 2 Type II; GDPR-compliant; HIPAA (Enterprise Pro/Max with BAA) ↗ Opt-out available (Enterprise: never trains; free/non-enterprise: trains by default; opt-out available) ↗ Managed-only ↗ US ↗ Perplexity AI; founders Aravind Srinivas (IIT Madras + UC Berkeley PhD; ex-OpenAI/Google DeepMi… ↗ internal — undisclosed ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper First search-engine-native browser agent — memory of past searches and tabs is part of the product premise. Limited to what Perplexity sees; cross-browser portability is not on the roadmap. comet

History/chats/bookmarks encrypted local-first. $128M raised pre-acquisition; $610M Atlassian acquisition. Personal AI / PKM / lifelogging memory
Consumer-facing memory products centered on the individual user's life or notes — screen recordings, conversations, networked-thought note apps, second-brain tools. The agent layer is secondary; the primary product is the personal corpus and the UX of recalling from it. Where memory becomes a consumer-product category.
Tencent ima.copilot Four-module self-evolving personal memory vectorgraph similaritygraph-traversal long-term extraction documentfactepisode opaque none specified ↗ Tencent's knowledge-management productivity AI, powered by Hunyuan. Copilot mode adds a four-module persistent memory architecture: Soul (agent persona), User (profile), Memory (long-term declarative), Agent (learned skills/task experience). Memory persists across devices and sessions; context awareness reads active web pages and files automatically. ima.copilot 2.0 (Oct 2025) introduced Task Mode with report and podcast generation; high-accuracy recall of prior work context across sessions. text ↗ searched not found ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo n/a (Tencent product; China-on 2 press n/a (Tencent su jobs ↗ not applicable — not academic parent is public ↗ 1000+ ppl Chinese market consumers and enterprise via WeChat/QQ ecosystem, names not disclosed↗ Free ↗ Subject to China PIPL (Personal Information Protection Law) and MIIT oversight; no Western certifications disclosed ↗ searched not found ↗ Managed-only ↗ CN ↗ Tencent corporate product (Pony Ma / Ma Huateng, CEO and co-founder of Tencent) ↗ internal — undisclosed ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Deeply integrated with the Chinese ecosystem (WeChat, QQ, Tencent cloud); the four-module decomposition cleanly separates persona, user model, episodic memory, and procedural memory — most products treat these as one bag. Primarily Chinese-market product; Hunyuan model and memory infrastructure are closed; no peer-reviewed benchmark results; limited English-language documentation. ima.qq.com HKMU guide (EN) Phemex coverage
Notion AI Workspace-scoped context + custom instructions vectorrelational similarityinjection long-term overwrite document user-controllable not applicable ↗ Notion's first-party AI. Reads up to 50 pages per query (as of Jan 2026); Custom Instructions persist brand voice / team structure / preferences. Context follows you across model swaps. Notably not persistent cross-session learning — it organises and summarises what's in Notion rather than learning from interactions. text ↗ 2018-01 not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo 34 HN · 3 press↗ not applicable — not academic $335M total $10.0B val Series C · 2021-10↗ $600M ARR 501-1000 ppl Kaiser Permanente, Mitsubishi Heavy Industries, Nvidia, Volvo Cars, 75% Fortune 500↗ Free + paid ↗ SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, ISO 27701, ISO 27017, ISO 27018, HIPAA (Enterprise with BAA) ↗ Never trains on customer data (Enterprise: zero-retention APIs, contractual prohibition; non-Enterprise LLM providers retain ≤30 days; opt-in LEAP program available) ↗ Managed-only ↗ US ↗ Ivan Zhao (CEO; ex-Inkling; cognitive science & fine arts UBC) and Simon Last (CTO; CS University of Maryland; ex-S… ↗ up to 50 pages per query (workspace-scoped) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Memory is grounded in workspace pages — what AI knows is exactly what's in your Notion, not a hidden vector store. Not persistent learning — does not remember interactions, only summarizes existing pages; 50-page-per-query ceiling at typical workspace size. Notion AI page 3.2 release notes
Obsidian Smart Connections Local-first semantic search over notes vectorfile similarity long-term overwrite documentchunk user-controllable overwrite ↗ Obsidian community plugin. Generates embeddings locally with a bundled model (TaylorAI/bge-micro-v2, 384-dim); chat with notes + see semantically related links. No API key required for local; supports 100+ cloud models optionally. Privacy-first. text ↗ 2022-12 4.5.0 2026-05-05 ↗ Custom ↗ 4.9k★ +144/mo↗ JavaScript 4.9k GitHub stars; no public HN count found ↗ not applicable — not academic searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ Free core plugin; Pro (all-access) subscription — 14-day free trial then paid; Founding Supporter one-time lifetime… ↗ not applicable — OSS plugin; no formal certifications ↗ searched not found ↗ Both (local-first plugin; optional cloud LLM APIs) ↗ US (Brian Petro / WFH Brian — US-based solo developer) ↗ Brian Petro (solo maintainer; US-based) ↗ local vault (no upper bound claimed) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Plugin to the most-extensible PKM tool — memory layered on top of your local markdown vault means full data ownership. Obsidian-tied; community plugin so quality depends on maintainer; setup more involved than first-party AI. GitHub brianpetro/obsidian-smart-connections smartconnections.app
Tana Supertag-based PKM with AI relationalgraph graph-traversalsimilarity long-term overwrite factdocument user-controllable overwrite ↗ Outliner + structured-data hybrid (Roam/Logseq + Notion-database influences). "Supertags" turn freeform nodes into queryable databases; Tana AI invokes on any node to summarise, generate action items, or extract structured fields (e.g. Meeting node with attendees + decisions + follow-ups). text ↗ 2024-05 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo n/a (SaaS app) 3 press ~15 (small star jobs ↗ not applicable — not academic $40M total Series B · 2025-02↗ 11-50 ppl no data (160K+ waitlist)↗ Free + paid ↗ GDPR-compliant; contractual prohibition on providers using customer data for model training ↗ Never trains on customer data (contractual obligation with all AI providers; GDPR-compliant data transfers) ↗ Managed-only ↗ NO ↗ Tarjei Munthe Vassbotn (CEO; ex-Google), Olav Sindre Kriken, Grim Iversen; Oslo Norway ↗ no scale claim ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Supertag + node graph model gives Tana a structurally richer memory substrate than flat-file notes. Heavyweight onboarding (model training); pricing tier limits casual adoption; closed system. personal.ai memory page
Voice-first / wearable AI memory
Hardware-tethered memory products that capture audio and ambient context continuously — pendants, recorders, meeting bots, AI-augmented note-takers. Memory is the primary product feature; the pitch is "remember everything you say and hear." Privacy, battery life, and always-on capture are the engineering frontier.
Era Computer User-sovereign pluggable memory layer for wearable OS proprietary agentic long-term agent-controlled file user-controllable none specified ↗ Software platform / OS layer for AI wearables (glasses, rings, pendants, speakers) that abstracts hardware from AI orchestration. Memory and model providers are pluggable and user-controlled rather than cloud-locked; supports 130+ LLMs from 14+ providers. Founded 2025 by ex-Humane team. $11M raised April 23, 2026 ($9M seed Abstract Ventures + BoxGroup; $2M pre-seed Topology + Betaworks). voice + text ↗ 2025-01 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo 5 HN · 1 press↗ ↗ $11M total Seed · 2026-04↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company ex-Humane team; CEO Liz Dorman (ex-Humane AI orchestration; ex-HP) CTO Alex Ollman (ex-HP agent… ↗ no scale claim ↗ not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Addresses vendor lock-in for wearable memory directly; neutral OS positioning means memory sovereignty is a first-class design constraint rather than an afterthought. No shipping product described; memory architecture is high-level positioning, not a published technical design; competing against Apple, Meta, and Google's proprietary stacks. Records in-person conversations and calls; AI summaries, action items, searchable transcripts. AES-256 + TLS 1.2 in transit; doesn't retain audio after transcription. Also sells Note (card-form recorder) and Note Pro. "World's No.1 AI note-taking brand." Hardware $169; free 300 min/month. ISO 27001, SOC II, HIPAA certified. Engagement scoring raises analytics-on-coworkers concerns; Ada digital-twin is opaque about what it stores. read.ai plans + pricing
Research / specialised systems
Memory systems with a paper and a reference implementation, often introducing distinctive architectural ideas — graph + temporal, OS-style hierarchies, three-phase consolidation, surprise-based gating. Not yet at production scale, but where new architectures are first tested. Many of the production systems above are direct descendants of entries here.
MemOS (MemTensor) "Memory operating system" + MemCubes hybrid similarityhybrid-rerank long-term consolidation chunk tagging agent could not infer from description searched not found ↗ Treats memory as an OS-managed resource with explicit allocation and process-like scoping. MemCubes unify parametric, activation, and plaintext memory. 7.9k★. v2.0 "Stardust" Dec 2025. searched not found ↗ 2025-07 v2.0.14 2026-04-23 ↗ Apache-2.0 ↗ 8.9k★ +113/mo↗ TypeScript searched not found ↗ 32 cites 32/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not a company MemTensor (Shanghai) Technology Co. + Shanghai Jiao Tong University + Renmin University + Zheji… ↗ searched not found ↗ no public benchmark scores found Too recent ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Most architecturally ambitious 'memory operating system' to date — explicit memory cycle and substrate. Chinese-market origin; less English-language adoption; production deployment evidence is light. GitHub MemTensor/MemOS arxiv 2505.22101 arxiv 2507.03724
A-MEM Atomic-note / Zettelkasten-style graph graph-traversalsimilarity long-term append-only fact tagging agent could not infer from description searched not found ↗ Treats memories as atomic linkable notes — explicit nod to Zettelkasten knowledge management. Dynamic linking; retroactive memory revision. searched not found ↗ 2025-01 no releases ↗ MIT ↗ 871★ +10/mo↗ Python searched not found ↗ 443 cites 443/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not a company no data — arxiv preprint only ↗ searched not found ↗ no public benchmark scores found Too recent ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Adaptive memory with explicit policy for what to remember vs forget. Research-only; no production deployment. GitHub WujiangXu/A-mem arxiv 2502.12110 NeurIPS 2025 poster
BAI-LAB MemoryOS Hierarchical: short / mid / long-term hybrid similarityhybrid-rerank long-term consolidation summary tagging agent could not infer from description searched not found ↗ Hierarchical "OS" with Storage / Updating / Retrieval / Generation modules. Short-term → mid-term via FIFO dialogue-chain; mid-term → long-term via segmented paging. EMNLP 2025 Oral. +48.36% F1 and +46.18% BLEU-1 over baselines on LoCoMo with GPT-4o-mini. 217★. searched not found ↗ 2025-05 V1.2 2025-07-18 ↗ Apache-2.0 ↗ 1.4k★ +40/mo↗ Python searched not found ↗ 41 cites 41/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not a company BAI Lab (Beijing Academy of AI affiliated); no individual authors confirmed ↗ searched not found ↗ +48.36% LoCoMo↗ Too recent ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ ICML 2025 venue — peer-reviewed memory-as-OS framework. Research scope; smaller community than commercial memory layers. GitHub BAI-LAB/MemoryOS ACL Anthology arxiv 2506.06326
Titans (Google) Neural fast/slow + surprise gating parametric attention session parametric-edit kv-token tagging agent could not infer from description searched not found ↗ Neural long-term memory module that learns to memorise at test time. Uses gradient-of-loss as "surprise" signal; adaptive weight-decay forgetting. Three variants: MAC (memory-as-context), MAG (memory-as-gate), MAL (memory-as-layer). Scales to 2M+ tokens. Reports outperforming Transformers + recent linear-recurrent models on language modeling, common-sense reasoning, genomics, and time series. searched not found ↗ 2025-01 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ 206 cites 103/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Google Research; Ali Behrouz (Cornell + Google) Peilin Zhong and Vahab Mirrokni ↗ searched not found ↗ no public benchmark scores found No reproductions found ↗ Paper only ↗ Google's response to Mamba — long-term memory module learns at test time; widely discussed in 2025. Closed weights; reproducibility limited; benchmark coverage narrower than published claims suggest. arxiv 2501.00663 Google Research blog Titans Revisited (analysis)
MemoRAG Global memory-enhanced RAG vector similarityhybrid-rerank long-term read-only chunk tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (read-only RAG retrieval) ↗ RAG framework on top of a long-context memory model. Builds global memory once, generates contextual clues at query time. TheWebConf 2025. Up to 1M-token contexts (with beacon_ratio=16). 30× pre-fill speedup via caching. Encoding+chunking+indexing 200K-token context: ~35s cold, 1.5s from cache. text ↗ 2024-09 v0.1.5 2024-09-24 ↗ Apache-2.0 ↗ 2.2k★↗ Python searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not a company Qian-Hui Qin et al. — Renmin University of China; TheWebConf 2025 ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ no public benchmark scores found Independently reproduced ↗ Code public + weights public ↗ Extends RAG with explicit memory layer — bridges retrieval and stored facts. Position between RAG and memory layer is muddled — neither owns the use case decisively. GitHub qhjqhj00/MemoRAG arxiv 2409.05591

MemPalace Verbatim chunks + spatial metaphor vector similarity long-term append-only chunk inspectable append (no resolution) ↗ Spatial-metaphor memory (wings / rooms / halls) on top of verbatim chunked storage. Independent analysis suggests the score is driven by verbatim storage + ChromaDB defaults rather than the palace structure. Reports 96.6% Recall@5 on LongMemEval (later updated to 98.4% on held-out data after benchmark-tuning controversy). 47k★ in two weeks at launch. text ↗ 2026-04 v3.3.4 2026-05-01 ↗ MIT ↗ 51.3k★ +49.6k/mo↗ Python searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not a company no data — searched mempalace.net; no author or affiliation listed ↗ no scale claim — research / open-source ↗ 96.6% LMES ⚠↗ searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ Three-layer corpus model (raw / governed / substrate) with explicit governance phases — most architecturally principled OSS memory framework. OSS framework; less polished than commercial memory layers; smaller user base. GitHub MemPalace/mempalace mempalace.net arxiv 2604.21284 (critical analysis) Vectorize benchmark debunk

76.88% LMES↗		 Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ Time-aware memory model — explicit temporal indexing of stored facts. Preprint; eval coverage narrow. arxiv 2601.02845 GitHub TiMEM-AI/timem
LiCoMemory CogniGraph + temporal/hierarchy-aware retrieval graph graph-traversalsimilarity long-term extraction episode tagging agent could not infer from description LLM-arbitrate (incremental graph updates) ↗ Lightweight hierarchical graph (CogniGraph) with entities and relations as semantic indexing layers. Incremental graph construction, fast updates, low-latency inference. Nov 2025. Beats Zep by ~9.0% accuracy and ~9.7% recall on LongMemEval with Llama-3.1-70B. 73.8% accuracy / 76.6% recall on LongMemEval with GPT-4o-mini. 10–40% lower latency than Mem0/Zep/A-MEM/LoCoMo-RAG. text ↗ 2025-11 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ 20 cites 20/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2511.01448; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ 73.8% LME (GPT-4o-mini)
73.8% LME (Llama-3.1-70B)↗		 Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ Lifelong continuous memory with explicit consolidation phases. Preprint; scaling and stability properties not fully characterized. arxiv 2511.01448
Recent method papers — theorized, no distinct product
Recent papers (mostly 2024–2026) proposing novel memory approaches with no shipped product or widely-used implementation yet. The frontier — most won't pan out, some will define the next generation. Read this section as ideas to test, not tools to adopt. Sub-grouped below by the kind of memory the paper targets.
— Factual & long-term memory (token-level)
Methods for storing and retrieving discrete facts — entities, events, observations — typically as tokenisable text passed in and out of the LLM. The dominant paradigm; this is where most memory research currently lives.

ReadAgent Gist-episodic compression + on-demand lookup kv similarityextraction-pull session read-only summary tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (read-only gist compression) ↗ Google Research / DeepMind. Prompts LLM to segment long documents into "pages," compresses each to a short gist stored as episodic memory, re-reads original pages on demand at answer time. Pure prompting, no fine-tuning. 3–20× effective context extension. Outperforms baselines on QuALITY, NarrativeQA, QMSum. text ↗ 2024-02 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold ↗ 105 cites 52/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Google Research / Google DeepMind; arxiv 2402.09727 ↗ no scale claim — research (prompt-only, no persistent store) ↗ no public benchmark scores found No reproductions found ↗ Paper only ↗ Read-then-act memory — agent reads documents, summarizes, acts. Preprint; summarization quality bounds downstream action quality. arxiv 2402.09727
GAM (General Agentic Memory) JIT runtime research over immutable page-store file agenticextraction-pull long-term read-only document tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (immutable page-store) ↗ Separates offline memory (lightweight Memorizer highlighting key facts) from online runtime (Researcher agent that queries the full raw page-store at inference). Inverts usual approach — keep full fidelity offline, pay complexity at query time. >90% accuracy on RULER Multi-Hop Tracing vs competing methods below 60%. Nov 2025. text ↗ 2025-11 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold ↗ 19 cites 19/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2511.18423; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ >90% RULER (Multi-Hop Tracing)↗ Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ General memory framework spanning multiple agent types. Preprint; generality vs specialization tradeoff unclear. arxiv 2511.18423
TierMem Provenance-linked summary→raw escalation hybrid similarityextraction-pull long-term append-only summary auditable not applicable (immutable raw-log; summaries derived) ↗ Two-tier: fast compressed summary index (Tier 1) + immutable raw-log store (Tier 2), with explicit provenance pointers linking summary units to source pages. Runtime sufficiency router escalates to raw log only when summary inadequate. Prevents "write-before-query barrier" — addresses unverifiable omission risk. Feb 2026. text ↗ 2026-02 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold ↗ 2 cites 2/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2602.17913; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ no public benchmark scores found Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ Tiered memory with explicit hot/warm/cold layers. MemInsight Autonomous semantic augmentation vector similarity long-term extraction summary tagging agent could not infer from description LLM-arbitrate (autonomous augmentation) ↗ Autonomously augments semantic representations of historical interactions to improve retrieval quality without manual curation. Validated on conversational recommendation, QA, and event summarisation. EMNLP 2025 Main. Boosts LoCoMo retrieval recall by 34% over a RAG baseline. text ↗ 2025-03 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold ↗ 48 cites 48/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2503.21760; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ +34% ret LoCoMo↗ Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ Insight-oriented memory aggregation — surfaces patterns rather than raw recall.
