Engram (DeepSeek) Conditional-memory N-gram lookup module parametric parametric-recall parametric-permanent read-only chunk opaque not applicable (architectural parametric sparsity — N-gram embedding; no runtime user write) ↗ DeepSeek + PKU. Introduces "conditional memory" alongside MoE's conditional compute. 27B-param N-gram lookup store offloads static knowledge from the transformer; O(1) lookup; prefetched from host RAM at near-zero runtime overhead. MMLU +3.4, CMMLU +4.0, BBH +5.0, ARC-Challenge +3.7. Multi-Query NIAH 84.2 → 97.0 vs iso-FLOPs MoE baseline. text (LM training — O(1) N-gram conditional lookup) ↗ 2026-01 no releases ↗ Apache-2.0 ↗ 4.4k★ +153/mo↗ Python Medium — several ML community blogs + LinkedIn posts about DeepSeek V4 architecture ↗ 28 cites (28/yr) ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not a company DeepSeek (High-Flyer Capital spin-off); founder Liang Wenfeng; Chinese AI lab ↗ Scaled to 27B parameters; MoE baseline comparison ↗ +3.7 poi ARC-Challenge
+5.0 poi BBH
+4.0 poi CMMLU
+3.4 poi MMLU
84.2 NIAH (Multi-Query)↗		 Too recent — Jan 2026 preprint; no independent reproductions ↗ Code public + demo code ↗ DeepSeek-quality model with explicit memory architecture — open weights, strong on Chinese benchmarks; novel agentic memory design. Documentation primarily in Chinese; smaller English-language adoption; eval coverage on Western benchmarks is limited. arxiv 2601.07372 GitHub deepseek-ai/Engram
MemoryBench (Tsinghua THUIR) Declarative + procedural continual-learning bench tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description read-only tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (evaluation benchmark) ↗ Tsinghua THUIR. First benchmark covering both declarative and procedural memory for LLM systems. User-feedback simulation framework tests adaptive improvement. Distinct from Renmin's MemBench. Existing memory systems fail badly on procedural knowledge. text ↗ 2025-10 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold ↗ 21 cites 21/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Tsinghua THUIR; arxiv 2510.17281 ↗ no scale claim — benchmark ↗ no public benchmark scores found Too recent ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Tsinghua's structured memory benchmark — multiple memory operation types in one harness. Smaller adoption than LongMemEval / LoCoMo; tasks oriented toward Chinese language. arxiv 2510.17281 HF dataset
MemR³ Closed-loop retrieve / reflect / answer router vector similarityagentic long-term read-only chunk tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (retrieval controller, no write) ↗ Closed-loop retrieval controller replacing single-shot retrieve-then-answer. Iterative router chooses between retrieve, reflect, answer; global evidence-gap tracker makes retrieval reasoning explicit and auditable. Outperforms strong baselines on LoCoMo (LLM-as-Judge). Explicit evidence-gap tracking enables post-hoc explainability. text ↗ 2025-12 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold ↗ 8 cites 8/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2512.20237; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ outperfo LoCoMo↗ Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ Triple-R memory: retrieve, reason, refresh. Preprint; refresh step adds latency. arxiv 2512.20237
— Experiential & procedural memory (agent learning from experience)
Methods focused on remembering how to do things — successful trajectories, failed attempts, learned skills, plan templates. The unit of memory is a procedure or an experience, not a fact. The natural home of agent self-improvement work.
MemMachine (paper) Ground-truth-preserving episodic + cluster-expansion retrieval vector similarity long-term append-only episode tagging agent could not infer from description append (no resolution — raw episodic store) ↗ Stores raw conversational episodes without LLM-driven extraction, indexing at sentence level; retrieval expands nucleus matches into neighboring-context clusters to address the embedding-dissimilarity problem in dialogue data. A Retrieval Agent routes queries across direct, parallel-decomposition, or iterative chain-of-query strategies. Distinct from the MemMachine framework already listed above (Dedicated memory layers). 0.9169 on LoCoMo (gpt-4.1-mini) — above Mem0, Zep, Memobase, LangMem. 93.0% on LongMemEvalS. ~80% fewer input tokens than Mem0 in matched comparisons. text ↗ 2026-04 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched 2 cites 2/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2604.04853; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ no public benchmark scores found Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ Ground-truth preservation avoids the factual hallucination risk of extraction-based condensation; cluster-expansion retrieval directly targets the embedding-dissimilarity gap in conversation data. Storing raw episodes grows storage linearly with conversation length; no published evaluation on months-scale interactions. arxiv 2604.04853
MIA — Memory Intelligence Agent Manager-Planner-Executor with bidirectional parametric/non-parametric loop hybrid parametric-recallsimilarity long-term consolidation trajectory tagging agent could not infer from description LLM-arbitrate (Planner parametric + Manager non-parametric) ↗ Manager stores compressed non-parametric search trajectories; Planner is a parametric memory agent producing search plans; Executor acts on plans. Planner evolves via test-time learning (on-the-fly weight updates during inference), creating a bidirectional conversion loop between parametric and non-parametric memory. Superior performance across 11 benchmarks for deep research agents (DRAs); alternating RL paradigm enhances Planner-Executor cooperation. April 2026. text ↗ 2026-04 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold ↗ searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2604.04503; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ no public benchmark scores found Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ Test-time parametric learning means the Planner genuinely improves within a session without fine-tuning infrastructure; the bidirectional loop is novel vs read-only RAG or write-only episodic stores. Test-time weight updates raise inference cost significantly; evaluation is on research-oriented tasks — generalization to conversational or tool-use agents not yet demonstrated. arxiv 2604.04503

MoT (Memory-of-Thought) Pre-test self-reflection cache vector similarity session append-only chunk tagging agent could not infer from description LLM-arbitrate (high-confidence pre-thought retrieval — not write-conflict path) ↗ Before test time, LLM reasons over unlabeled data and stores high-confidence intermediate thoughts as external memory. At test time, retrieves relevant stored thoughts to augment reasoning. No annotated data, no parameter updates. Significant gains on arithmetic, commonsense, factual, NLI reasoning with ChatGPT. Operates at utterance / turn / session scopes. >10% accuracy improvement over no-memory baseline on LongMemEval. — Working memory & context management
Methods for managing what's in the model's context window during a single task — compression, eviction, recurrent state, segment-level recurrence. The "active" tier of memory, analogous to RAM in cognitive-science framings. Closely related to long-context architectures.

MemoBrain Dependency-aware working-context compaction kv agentic session consolidation chunk tagging agent could not infer from description LLM-arbitrate (dependency-aware compaction) ↗ Co-pilot alongside reasoning agent. Builds dependency graph over reasoning steps; prunes invalid steps, folds completed sub-trajectories, preserves compact high-salience backbone under fixed context budget — without blocking execution. Consistent improvements on GAIA, WebWalker, BrowseComp-Plus. Recurrent Memory Transformer (RMT) Segment-level recurrence + memory tokens kv-cache attention session overwrite kv-token tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (architectural recurrent memory tokens — no user write path) ↗ Bulatov / Kuratov / Burtsev. Adds memory tokens passed between segments — no architectural change to the Transformer. Scaling variant (arxiv 2304.11062) handles up to 2M-token sequences with high retrieval accuracy. On par with Transformer-XL for small memory; outperforms it for long-sequence tasks. text (language modelling + algorithmic tasks — copy reverse associative retrieval) ↗ 2022-06 no releases ↗ Unlicensed ↗ 779★↗ Jupyter Notebook below detection threshold ↗ 177 cites 44/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not a company Moscow Institute of Physics and Technology + AIRI + DeepPavlov.ai; Aydar Bulatov Yuri Kuratov M… ↗ no scale claim — research (WikiText-103 enwik8) ↗ no public benchmark scores found Independently reproduced ↗ Code public + weights public ↗ Defined recurrent memory tokens in transformer attention — foundational for segment-recurrent architectures. Training instability at scale; superseded for production by attention-window techniques. arxiv 2207.06881 scaling-to-1M arxiv 2304.11062 GitHub booydar/RMT
ARMT (Associative RMT) Hopfield-style energy basins kv-cache attentionsimilarity session overwrite kv-token tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (architectural memory — no user write path) ↗ Associative Recurrent Memory Transformer. Stores tokens in Hopfield-style energy basins for constant-time pattern completion. Pattern completion claimed over 50M tokens. text ↗ 2023-08 no-release no-release Apache-2.0 ↗ 64★↗ Jupyter Notebook below detection threshold ↗ 13 cites 6/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2407.04841; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ no public benchmark scores found No reproductions found ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Associative memory built into Recurrent Memory Transformer. Infini-attention Compressive memory + linear attention kv-cache attention session consolidation kv-token tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (architectural; compression evicts old tokens) ↗ Google. "Leave No Context Behind." Compressive memory inside the attention block + masked local + long-term linear attention. Bounded memory footprint, streaming inference, infinite context in principle. Minimal additional bounded-memory parameters. text ↗ 2024-04 no releases ↗ Unlicensed ↗ 55★↗ Python below detection threshold ↗ 192 cites 96/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not a company Google; arxiv 2404.07143 ↗ infinite via streaming (compressive; no hard cap) ↗ no public benchmark scores found No reproductions found ↗ Paper only ↗ Infinite-context attention with compressed historical memory. Compressive Transformer Recent full-res + compressed older kv-cache attention session consolidation kv-token tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (architectural two-level compressed memory — no user write path) ↗ Maintains recent states in full resolution while compressing older memories with learned compression functions. TransformerFAM Feedback Attention Memory kv-cache attention session overwrite kv-token tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (architectural; no user write path) ↗ Google. Feedback attention loop enables the model to attend to its own latent representations — emerges as working memory. No extra weights; integrates with pre-trained models. Validated on 1B / 8B / 24B model scales. Significantly improves Transformer performance on long-context tasks. text ↗ 2024-04 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold ↗ 18 cites 9/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Google; arxiv 2404.09173 ↗ no scale claim — research (working memory architectural) ↗ no public benchmark scores found No reproductions found ↗ Paper only ↗ Feedback Attention Memory — recurrent memory loop in transformer attention. ATLAS Omega rule + polynomial features + Muon parametric attention session parametric-edit kv-token tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (architectural memory module) ↗ Long-term memory module that learns to memorise the context at test time. Omega rule (sliding-window update over all past tokens), polynomial feature mappings, Muon optimiser. Generalises to "DeepTransformers" family. May 2025. +80% accuracy at 10M context length on BABILong. Surpasses Transformers + recent linear-recurrent models on language modeling, common-sense reasoning, recall-intensive, and long-context understanding. text ↗ 2025-05 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold ↗ 42 cites 42/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2505.23735; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ +80% acc BABILong↗ Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ Long-context atlas of attention patterns. LongMem Trainable SideNet over memory bank kv-cache similarityattention long-term append-only kv-token tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (architectural SideNet memory bank) ↗ Trainable SideNet module selectively retrieves relevant cached key-value pairs from memory banks during decoding. text ↗ 2023-06 no-release no-release Apache-2.0 ↗ 826★↗ Python below detection threshold ↗ 172 cites 57/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2306.07174; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ no public benchmark scores found No reproductions found ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Long-term memory module for transformer LMs. Preprint; memory module pretraining cost. arxiv 2306.07174

EM-LLM Bayesian-surprise episodic partition kv-cache similarityattention long-term consolidation episode tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (architectural KV partition) ↗ Uses Bayesian surprise detection to partition the cache into meaningful episodes for coherent retrieval. text ↗ 2024-10 no-release no-release MIT ↗ 272★↗ Python below detection threshold ↗ 52 cites 26/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2407.09450; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ no public benchmark scores found No reproductions found ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Episodic-memory LLM with explicit episode boundaries. Preprint; boundary detection quality varies. arxiv 2407.09450
— Parametric & latent memory (memory in weights / KV / hidden states)
Methods that put memory into the model's parameters or latent representations rather than discrete text. Includes memory-augmented architectures, KV-cache compression, and knowledge editing. Where "memory" stops being external storage and becomes part of the model's internal state.
NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Hybrid Mamba + sparse KV-cache, 1M-token context kv-cacheparametric attentioncache-lookup session read-only kv-token opaque not applicable (architectural hybrid; no user write path) ↗ Interleaves sparse MoE layers with Mamba-2 layers; Mamba layers maintain a fixed-size recurrent state (constant memory during generation) rather than a growing KV cache. Attention layers use GQA with only 2 KV heads. Two parallel memory systems: explicit small KV cache (attention layers only) and implicit compressed history in the Mamba state. Post-trained via multi-environment RL for multi-step tool use. Nemotron 3 Nano (31.6B total, 3.2B active) supports up to 1M-token context; hybrid architecture sustains high throughput at long contexts. text ↗ 2025-12 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched Too recent — paper submitted December 24 2025; 69 citing models on HuggingFace noted; no S2 count confirmed ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company NVIDIA Research; arxiv 2512.20856 ↗ 1M-token context (vendor claim) ↗ no public benchmark scores found Too recent (weights public; training recipes released at github.com/NVIDIA-NeMo/Nemotron) ↗ Code public + weights public (model weights on HuggingFace; training recipes at NVIDIA-NeMo/Nemotron) ↗ Constant-memory Mamba state eliminates KV-cache explosion for the majority of layers, enabling genuinely long-context inference without proportional memory growth. Mamba state is a lossy compressed summary — information not captured is lost; memory-relevant properties not benchmarked against dedicated memory-augmented LLMs on QA or agent tasks. arxiv 2512.20856 NVIDIA page HF model card
Sakana CTM Per-neuron history + neural synchronization as latent parametric parametric-recallattention session read-only kv-token opaque not applicable (neural architecture; no external memory write path) ↗ Continuous Thought Machines. Each neuron maintains its own history of incoming activations via private per-neuron weight parameters; the model operates along a self-generated "thought step" timeline rather than the input sequence timeline. Neural synchronization across neurons is used directly as a latent representation. Distinct from Sakana NAMM (evolutionary attention memory) — CTM is an architectural rethink of the recurrent state itself. Strong performance on ImageNet-1K, 2D maze solving, sorting, parity, QA, RL tasks without domain-specific tuning. text + image (classification and reasoning benchmarks) ↗ 2025-05 no releases ↗ Apache-2.0 ↗ 1.8k★↗ Python below detection threshold or not yet researched Too recent — submitted May 9 2025; no S2 count confirmed ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not a company Sakana AI (Tokyo); founder David Ha (ex-Google Brain Research Director; ex-Stability AI CSO) an… ↗ not applicable — research paper — no scale claim no public benchmark scores found Too recent (code public at SakanaAI/continuous-thought-machines with checkpoints) ↗ Code public + weights public (SakanaAI/continuous-thought-machines GitHub; model checkpoints released) ↗ Architecture-agnostic over data modality — can apply sequential "thought" to static inputs (images, graphs) that lack inherent sequence. Research prototype; no production deployment; eval set is narrow and does not include long-document or multi-session memory tasks comparable to RAG or KV-cache work. arxiv 2505.05522 project page GitHub SakanaAI/CTM
NAVER Provence + PISCO RAG context pruning + soft compression kv-cache cache-lookupattention session evict-oldest kv-token opaque not applicable (read-only retrieval augmentation; no user write path) ↗ Two complementary NAVER Labs Europe papers (Jan 2025). Provence fine-tunes DeBERTa as a sequence labeler to dynamically detect needed pruning amount per query and mask irrelevant context spans before generation, unifying pruning with reranking in one pass. PISCO is a distillation-only approach (no annotated data, no pretraining) that trains an LLM to decode from a 16x-compressed document representation. PISCO: 16x document compression with 0–3% accuracy drop on RAG-QA; 5.7x inference speed-up; fine-tuning under 48h on a single A100. text ↗ 2025-01 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company NAVER Labs Europe; corporate research ↗ not applicable — research paper — no scale claim no public benchmark scores found Too recent ↗ searched not found ↗ PISCO requires no labeled data or pretraining — distillation from open-ended questions only, cheap to adapt to new domains. Provence relies on a separately-trained DeBERTa ranker (extra pipeline component); PISCO requires fine-tuning the target LLM, so does not apply zero-shot to arbitrary models. Provence 2501.16214 PISCO 2501.16075
ShadowKV (ByteDance) Low-rank CPU-offloaded KV cache + sparse reconstruction kv-cache cache-lookupattention session evict-oldest kv-token opaque not applicable (KV cache compression; no user write path) ↗ During prefill, value caches are offloaded to CPU and only low-rank pre-RoPE keys plus compressed landmarks and outliers are kept on GPU. During decoding, landmarks select chunk indices for key reconstruction and value fetching from CPU. Effectively unlimited context without GPU memory blowup. ICML 2025 Spotlight. Up to 6× larger batch sizes and 3.04× throughput on A100 without accuracy loss; validated on RULER, LongBench, NIAH across Llama-3.1, GLM-4, Yi, Phi-3, Qwen2. text ↗ 2024-10 no releases ↗ Apache-2.0 ↗ 297★↗ Python below detection threshold or not yet researched Too recent — ICML 2025 Spotlight (accepted 2024); no S2 count confirmed ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not a company ByteDance Seed; arxiv 2410.21465 ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ no accur LongBench
no accur NIAH
no accur RULER↗		 Independently reproduced (ICML 2025 Spotlight acceptance implies community review) ↗ Code public + weights closed (ByteDance-Seed/ShadowKV GitHub) ↗ Validated on six diverse model families with no accuracy degradation; ICML 2025 Spotlight; open-source. Requires CPU-GPU data transfer during decoding (PCIe-bandwidth-sensitive latency); low-rank key approximation may miss precise token-level distinctions in adversarial retrieval. arxiv 2410.21465 GitHub bytedance/ShadowKV
IBM Meta-Tokens Lossless context compression via learned placeholders parametrickv-cache attentioncache-lookup parametric-permanent parametric-edit kv-tokenweight opaque not applicable (context compression; no user write path) ↗ Meta-tokens are learned vocabulary entries that act as placeholders for recurring token subsequences — the model is fine-tuned to understand compressed input where meta-tokens stand in for their corresponding subsequences without any information loss. Mathematically lossless and reversible, unlike soft-compression approaches. 27% and 18% average sequence-length reduction on two evaluation tasks; 47% and 33% less encoding compute due to quadratic attention cost. text ↗ 2025-06 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched Too recent — submitted Sep 2025; no S2 count confirmed ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company IBM Research; arxiv 2506.00307 ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ no public benchmark scores found Too recent (ICML 2025 paper; no independent reproductions found) ↗ Paper only — no public code repo found ↗ True losslessness — no semantic degradation by construction; compression decision can be made at inference time with no model change. Compression ratio is modest compared to soft methods (16x for PISCO vs ~1.2–1.4x here); requires fine-tuning on the specific compression format. arxiv 2506.00307
MemoryLLM (original) Fixed-size in-weights memory pool with online self-update parametric parametric-recallattention cross-session parametric-edit weight opaque not applicable (architectural in-weights pool) ↗ Augments a transformer with a fixed-size 1B-parameter memory pool distributed across all layers; new knowledge is injected by updating the pool parameters during inference, not via context extension. Distinct from M+ (already in landscape) which extends with scalable long-term storage — this is the original fixed-capacity pool. Effective on model editing benchmarks and long-context tests; stable after ~1M sequential updates. text ↗ 2024-02 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched 31 cites (15/yr); ICML 2024 ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2402.04624; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ no scale claim — research (1B-parameter memory pool) ↗ no public benchmark scores found Independently reproduced (ICML 2024 published; community replications confirmed) ↗ Code public + weights public (wangyu-ustc/MemoryLLM GitHub; YuWangX/memoryllm-8b on HuggingFace) ↗ No external retrieval store needed — memory is entirely parametric and intrinsic to the model. Fixed pool capacity creates a hard ceiling on storable knowledge; no dynamic growth path without the M+ extension; pool update is sequential, limiting batched knowledge injection speed. arxiv 2402.04624
SELF-PARAM KL-distillation experience internalization parametric parametric-recall parametric-permanent parametric-edit weight opaque not applicable (KL-distillation into parameters) ↗ Encodes new experiences directly into the LLM's existing parameters by minimizing KL divergence between a teacher model (with context access) and a student model (without). Generates diverse QA pairs from new knowledge as the distillation signal. No new memory pool or external store added. Outperforms existing continual learning and memory injection methods on QA and conversational recommendation, even vs methods with nonzero storage cost. text ↗ 2024-10 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched Too recent — submitted Oct 2024; no S2 count confirmed ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2410.00487; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ no public benchmark scores found Too recent ↗ Code public + weights closed (XinshuangL/SELF-PARAM GitHub) ↗ Zero-parameter overhead — no new layers, stores, or pools added; computationally accessible (fine-tuning only). Internalization is a form of fine-tuning and risks catastrophic forgetting of earlier knowledge; updating is slow vs fast in-context or retrieval-based methods. arxiv 2410.00487
Anthropic Circuit Tracing Mechanistic interpretability of parametric storage parametric parametric-recall parametric-permanent read-only weight auditable not applicable (interpretability research; no external memory write path) ↗ Replaces MLP layers with sparse cross-layer transcoders to produce "replacement models" where activations correspond to interpretable features; traces attribution graphs showing which features causally influenced which outputs. Includes case studies on factual recall, revealing how facts are stored and retrieved from weights at the circuit level. Open-source tools run on any open-weights model. Successfully traced computational circuits for factual recall, planning, and multi-step reasoning in Claude 3.5 Haiku; open-source library released. text (Claude 3.5 Haiku; multi-lingual cross-modal features noted) ↗ 2025-03-27 (published March 27 2025 on transformer-circuits.pub) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched Too recent — published March 2025; not on arxiv; no S2 count available ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Anthropic; mechanistic interpretability team (Chris Olah and team) ↗ not applicable — research paper — no scale claim no public benchmark scores found Original team only (Anthropic; open-source tools released June 2025; community replication pending) ↗ Code public + weights closed (open-sourced circuit-tracing library; Neuronpedia frontend; no model weights released) ↗ Only published tool that makes parametric (weight-level) memory storage mechanistically interpretable rather than behaviorally probed; attribution graphs are auditable per-prompt. Not a memory system — it studies memory; findings are descriptive, not prescriptive; scalability to full production Claude models is limited (primary validation on 18-layer models and Haiku). methods paper Anthropic blog open-source release
MemLoRA Distilled expert adapters parametric parametric-recall parametric-permanent parametric-edit skill tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (distilled adapters; parametric) ↗ Distils expert adapters for on-device memory systems. text ↗ 2025-12 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched 1 cites 1/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2512.04763; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ no public benchmark scores found Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ LoRA-shaped memory adapter — memory injection via low-rank update. Larimar Distributed hippocampal episodic memory for editing parametric parametric-recall parametric-permanent parametric-edit episode tagging agent could not infer from description overwrite (one-shot knowledge update; selective forgetting) ↗ IBM Research. Attaches a fast-learning distributed episodic memory (hippocampus-inspired) to a frozen LLM, enabling one-shot knowledge updates without retraining. Supports selective forgetting + information-leakage prevention. Accuracy on par with leading fact-editing baselines; 8–10× speed-up over gradient-based editing. Generalises beyond trained context lengths. EMAT Sub-ms KV memory for QA pairs kv-cache similarity long-term read-only kv-token tagging agent could not infer from description not yet researched — read-only memory (encode millions of QA pairs as KV; no runtime write) Efficient Memory-Augmented Transformer. Encodes millions of QA pairs into key-value memory; MIPS search returns hits in sub-millisecond latency. EMNLP 2022. T5-base augmented with EMAT: 25.8 → 44.3 EM on Natural Questions. ~1000 queries/sec throughput. Outperforms retrieval-augmented baselines on WoW and ELI5. text 2022-10 no releases ↗ Unlicensed ↗ 35★↗ Python below detection threshold 58 cites 14/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not a company Facebook AI Research (FAIR); Patrick S. MemReasoner Iterative bidirectional-GRU re-read kv-cache attention session overwrite kv-token tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (architectural latent GRU memory; trained) ↗ IBM Research. Memory-augmented LLM architecture for multi-hop reasoning. Iteratively re-reads a temporal latent memory with bidirectional GRUs until readout stabilises. NeurIPS 2024. +18% on multi-hop QA. Generalises better than off-the-shelf LLMs and existing recurrent models on tests where required facts are scattered up to 128k tokens. text ↗ 2024-10-04 (submitted to NeurIPS 2024 OpenReview Oct 4 2024; published Dec 10 2024) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company IBM Research; openreview.net/forum?id=ODcMy97cVZ ↗ no scale claim — research (NeurIPS 2024) ↗ no public benchmark scores found Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ Reasoning over memory store as a discrete inference step. NAMMs Evolution-trained KV-cache memory kv-cache attention session evict-oldest kv-token tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (evolution-trained KV; architectural) ↗ Sakana AI. "An Evolved Universal Transformer Memory." Neural Attention Memory Models trained via evolution (overcomes non-differentiability of token-keep/discard). Universally applicable — train on language tasks, transfer zero-shot to other transformers and modalities. Substantial gains on long-context benchmarks while compressing context to a fraction of original size. text ↗ 2024-10 no releases ↗ Unlicensed ↗ 357★↗ Python below detection threshold 5 cites 2/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not a company Sakana AI; arxiv 2410.13166 ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ no public benchmark scores found No reproductions found ↗ Code public + weights public ↗ Sakana NAMM — neural attention memory model with evolutionary tuning. Research-stage; less popular than implicit context-window approaches. PLOS One PMC mirror
— World-model & imagination memory
Architectures that learn an internal model of the environment and use it as a memory of "how the world works" — agents plan and learn by imagining rollouts inside this model rather than recalling discrete facts. Adjacent to LeCun's JEPA programme and to RL world-model lines (Dreamer, Genie). Memory here is a learned dynamics function, not a database.
I-JEPA Joint-embedding predictive arch (image) parametric parametric-recall parametric-permanent read-only scene-graph tagging agent could not infer from description not yet researched — self-supervised image learning; no conflict path (read-only world model) Meta. Self-supervised image learning by predicting target-block representations from a context block in latent space — no hand-crafted augmentations or pixel reconstruction. Foundation of LeCun's hierarchical-JEPA programme. CVPR 2023. ViT-Huge/14 trains on ImageNet using 16 A100 GPUs in under 72 hours. image (ImageNet; ViT-Huge) 2023-06 no releases ↗ CC-BY-NC-4.0 ↗ 3.4k★↗ Python below detection threshold 773 cites 257/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not a company Meta FAIR; Mahmoud Assran Quentin Duval Ishan Misra Piotr Bojanowski Pascal Vincent Michael Rab… ↗ ViT-Huge/14 trains on ImageNet in under 72h on 16 A100 GPUs no public benchmark scores found Independently reproduced ↗ Code public + weights public ↗ Meta's joint embedding predictive architecture — backbone of LeCun's JEPA programme; non-generative world modeling. Image-prediction scope in initial paper; pre-training corpus is small vs LLM standards. arxiv 2301.08243 GitHub facebookresearch/ijepa

DreamerV3 Model-based RL with imagination parametric parametric-recall parametric-permanent parametric-edit trajectory tagging agent could not infer from description not yet researched — world-model RL; no external write path Hafner et al. General world-model agent. Symlog observation, KL balance, RMSNorm, Block GRU. Single hyperparameter set across 150+ tasks. First algorithm to collect diamonds in Minecraft from scratch without human data or curricula. Published in Nature 2025. image + sensor (Atari / DMControl / Minecraft / BSuite — 150+ tasks) 2023-01 no releases ↗ MIT ↗ 3.2k★↗ Python 1 inbound 1.0k cites 343/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not a company Google DeepMind; Danijar Hafner Jurgis Pasukonis Jimmy Ba Timothy Lillicrap ↗ single hyperparameter set across 150+ tasks; first algorithm to collect Minecraft diamonds without human data no public benchmark scores found Independently reproduced ↗ Code public + weights public ↗ World-model RL state-of-the-art across diverse tasks without per-task tuning. Genie Foundation world model from video parametric parametric-recall parametric-permanent read-only scene-graph tagging agent could not infer from description not yet researched — generative world model; no external write path DeepMind. Foundation world model trained unsupervised on internet gaming video. Generates frame-by-frame controllable virtual worlds from text / image / sketch prompts. Spatiotemporal video tokenizer + autoregressive dynamics + latent action model. 11B parameters. 200k+ hours of training video. Frame-by-frame controllability without any ground-truth action labels. video + image (internet gaming video) 2024-05 no-release no-release MIT ↗ 273★↗ Python below detection threshold 511 cites 255/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not a company Google DeepMind + University of British Columbia; 25 authors including Jake Bruce Michael Denni… ↗ 11B parameters; trained on 200k+ hours of internet gaming video; unsupervised controllable world generation no public benchmark scores found No reproductions found ↗ Paper only ↗ DeepMind's interactive world model — generates playable game-like environments from prompts. Closed Google; gameplay scope; production utility outside game / sim is unclear. arxiv 2402.15391 open-genie (community impl)
V-JEPA (original) Non-generative video world model parametric parametric-recall parametric-permanent read-only trajectory tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (self-supervised world model; no write path) ↗ Meta AI. Predicts masked spatio-temporal video patches in latent representation space rather than pixel space. Implicit world model without generative decoding. Released for self-supervised video representation learning from VideoMix2M. Strong on motion understanding and action anticipation. Predecessor to V-JEPA 2. video + image ↗ 2024-02 no releases ↗ CC-BY-NC-4.0 ↗ 3.8k★↗ Python below detection threshold 259 cites 129/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not a company Meta AI; arxiv 2404.08471 ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ no public benchmark scores found Independently reproduced ↗ Code public + weights public ↗ Original V-JEPA paper — established the JEPA-for-video approach. DIAMOND Diffusion world model for RL parametric parametric-recall parametric-permanent parametric-edit trajectory tagging agent could not infer from description not yet researched — diffusion world model RL; no external write path DIffusion As a Model Of eNvironment Dreams. Trains an RL agent inside a diffusion-based world model — denoising diffusion generates high-fidelity environment rollouts rather than discrete latents. Addresses visual-detail loss in prior discrete-token world models. Mean human-normalised score 1.46 on Atari 100k — best for agents trained entirely in a world model. NeurIPS 2024 Spotlight. image 2024-05 no-release no-release MIT ↗ 2.0k★↗ Python below detection threshold 209 cites 104/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not a company Ecole Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne (EPFL); no lead author confirmed from search ↗ mean human-normalized score 1.46 on Atari 100k — best for world-model-trained agents no public benchmark scores found Independently reproduced ↗ Code public + weights public ↗ Diffusion-based world models — first to apply diffusion to learned dynamics. Research-stage; sampling cost is high; production deployment limited. arxiv 2405.12399
PWM (Policy Learning with Large World Models) Multi-task world-model + first-order gradient policies parametric parametric-recall parametric-permanent parametric-edit trajectory tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (world-model RL; no external write path) ↗ Pre-trains a large multi-task world model on offline data across many continuous-control tasks, then extracts task-specific policies by differentiating through the model with first-order gradient optimisation. World model serves as compressed environment memory enabling policy transfer. Strong continuous-control results. Efficient policy extraction without environment interaction. image + sensor ↗ 2024-07 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold 11 cites 5/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2407.02466; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ no public benchmark scores found No reproductions found ↗ Paper only ↗ Policy learning grounded in large pretrained world models. Preprint; world-model scale-policy-learning interaction unclear. arxiv 2407.02466
— Continual learning & catastrophic forgetting
Methods that prevent a model from forgetting earlier tasks when trained on new ones. Distinct from "memory" in the agent sense — the model itself is the memory, and the question is how to update its weights without losing prior capabilities. Tightly coupled to neural-architecture work; relevant whenever you fine-tune sequentially.
RIKEN Tensor Decomposition Incremental Learning Tensor-decomposed exemplar compression parametric parametric-recall parametric-permanent parametric-edit episode tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (continual learning compression; no external write path) ↗ Uses tensor decomposition to exploit low intrinsic dimensionality and pixel correlation of stored exemplar images, achieving high compression while preserving discriminative information for class-incremental learning. Compresses the replay buffer used in catastrophic-forgetting prevention. RIKEN AIP + Guangdong U. of Technology. ICML 2025 poster; compression without proportional accuracy loss vs full-exemplar replay. image ↗ 2025 (ICML 2025 poster; exact arxiv submission date not confirmed) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company RIKEN Center for Advanced Intelligence Project (AIP); ICML 2025 ↗ no scale claim — research (ICML 2025) ↗ no public benchmark scores found Too recent ↗ searched not found ↗ Principled mathematical basis (tensor low-rank structure) rather than heuristic pruning; applies to any replay-based continual learning setup. Targets image classification exemplar buffers specifically; applicability to language model continual learning is indirect and not demonstrated in this paper. ICML 2025 page
EWC (Elastic Weight Consolidation) Quadratic constraint per weight parametric parametric-recall parametric-permanent parametric-edit weight tagging agent could not infer from description not yet researched — quadratic weight constraint per-parameter; no external memory write Kirkpatrick et al. Soft quadratic constraint pulls each weight back toward old values, scaled by importance for prior tasks. PNAS 2017. Foundational continual-learning paper. Analogous to synaptic consolidation in biological neural systems. image + text (supervised classification + RL Atari) 2018-01 no-release no-release no-license ↗ 260★↗ Jupyter Notebook below detection threshold 9.6k cites 964/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not a company Google DeepMind; lead author James Kirkpatrick with Razvan Pascanu Neil Rabinowitz Demis Hassab… ↗ no scale claim — research (PNAS 2017 — tested on supervised tasks and Atari RL) no public benchmark scores found Independently reproduced ↗ Code public + weights public ↗ Kirkpatrick et al. — foundational PNAS 2017 paper; analogous to synaptic consolidation in biology. Best on small task sequences; degrades when task count grows; LLM-scale applicability limited. arxiv 1612.00796 PNAS

FOREVER Forgetting-curve memory replay vector similarity long-term consolidation episode tagging agent could not infer from description LLM-arbitrate (replay + regularisation) ↗ Replay scheduler inspired by the Ebbinghaus forgetting curve, plus intensity-aware regularisation to control replay strength. Mitigates catastrophic forgetting in LLM continual learning. text ↗ 2026-01 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold 2 cites 2/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2601.03938; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ no public benchmark scores found Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ Continual-learning framework with explicit forgetting prevention. SERS Self-evolving pseudo-rehearsal parametric parametric-recall parametric-permanent parametric-edit episode tagging agent could not infer from description LLM-arbitrate (Wasserstein-distance regulariser) ↗ Dynamic regulariser driven by Wasserstein distance between task distributions; automatically relaxes or strengthens constraints in proportion to task similarity. text ↗ 2025-09-18 (submitted to NeurIPS 2025 Sept 18 2025; Jun Wang / Liang Ding et al.) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company no data — searched openreview.net/forum?id=jR1lvwexLt; author affiliations not found … ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ no public benchmark scores found Too recent ↗ searched not found ↗ Self-evolving rehearsal-scheduling for continual learning. Preprint; eval scope limited. OpenReview
— Memory-augmented reinforcement learning
RL agents that maintain explicit episodic memory of past trajectories — the agent recalls similar past states/actions to make better decisions now. Distinct from policy-only RL or world-model RL: memory is the policy mechanism. Where neuroscience-inspired memory work most directly meets RL.
Neural Episodic Control Differentiable episodic dictionary kv similarity long-term overwrite episode tagging agent could not infer from description not yet researched — differentiable episodic dictionary RL; no concurrent external write Pritzel et al. Agent stores past state-action-value tuples in a differentiable dictionary; recall via approximate nearest neighbour. ICML 2017. Landmark. Established episodic-memory RL as a paradigm. Fast learning from sparse rewards. image (Atari games) 2017-05 no-release no-release no-license ↗ 59★↗ Python below detection threshold 392 cites 43/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not a company Google DeepMind; Alexander Pritzel Benigno Uria Oriol Vinyals Demis Hassabis Daan Wierstra Char… ↗ met or surpassed human performance on ~25% of Atari games within 10M frames (46 hours) no public benchmark scores found Independently reproduced ↗ Paper only ↗ DeepMind paper — defined episodic-memory-augmented RL; widely cited. Small-scale benchmark scope; doesn't generalize to LLM agents directly. arxiv 1703.01988
EMU (Efficient Episodic Memory Utilization) Coherent-memory MARL vector similarity long-term consolidation episode tagging agent could not infer from description LLM-arbitrate (selective promotion) ↗ Multi-agent RL with semantically coherent episodic buffer; selectively promotes desirable transitions to avoid local convergence. ICLR 2024 Oral. text ↗ 2024-03 no-release no-release Apache-2.0 ↗ 56★↗ Python below detection threshold 14 cites 7/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2403.01112; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ no public benchmark scores found No reproductions found ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Efficient episodic memory in RL with explicit retrieval policy. Research-only; eval primarily on game tasks. arxiv 2403.01112 OpenReview (ICLR 2024 Oral)
HAMI Hippocampus-inspired RL framework hybrid graph-traversalsimilarity long-term consolidation episode tagging agent could not infer from description LLM-arbitrate (hierarchical refinement) ↗ Symbolic indexing + hierarchical memory refinement + structured episodic retrieval, inspired by hippocampal mechanisms. Nature Scientific Reports 2025. Improves learning efficiency and adaptability in contextual / sequential decision making. text ↗ 2025-07-12 (received March 1 2025; accepted July 4 2025; published July 12 2025 in Nature Scientific Reports) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Nature Scientific Reports 2025; no affiliation found at nature.com/articles/s41598-02… ↗ no scale claim — research (Nature Scientific Reports 2025) ↗ no public benchmark scores found Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ Hierarchical memory-augmented agents with explicit abstraction levels. Research-stage; hierarchy design is task-specific. Nature Sci Reports
— Cognitive-architecture-inspired memory
Approaches that import structures from classical cognitive architectures (Soar, ACT-R, complementary learning systems) into LLM-based agents. Bridge between cognitive science and modern AI memory.
LLM-ACTR / Cognitive LLMs ACT-R latent representations injected into LLM parametric parametric-recallsimilarity parametric-permanent read-only chunk tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (ACT-R buffers injected; no external write) ↗ Neuro-symbolic architecture for manufacturing decision-making. Extracts ACT-R's procedural / declarative / goal buffers as latent neural representations, then injects them into trainable LLM adapter layers via knowledge transfer. Outperforms chain-of-thought baselines on Design for Manufacturing tasks. Journal version SAGE AI 2025. text ↗ 2024-11 no releases ↗ Unlicensed ↗ 5★↗ Jupyter Notebook below detection threshold 8 cites 4/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2408.09176; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ no public benchmark scores found No reproductions found ↗ Paper only ↗ Bridges ACT-R cognitive architecture with LLM agent reasoning. Research-stage; ACT-R fluency required to interpret; small adoption. arxiv 2408.09176 GitHub SiyuWu528/LLM-ACTR
Human-Like Remembering and Forgetting ACT-R Base-Level Activation over vector store vector similarity long-term consolidation chunk tagging agent could not infer from description LLM-arbitrate (ACT-R base-level activation scoring) ↗ Re-implements ACT-R's retrieval probability model — temporal decay, prior-access frequency, stochastic noise — as scoring on top of a vector store. Spreading activation over semantic similarity adds associative recall. Forgetting becomes an explicit function of recency and use-count, not eviction heuristics. Evaluated on social-robot conversation. ACM HAI 2025. text ↗ 2025-11-10 (ACM HAI 2025; published online November 10 2025) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company ACM CHI 2025; no affiliation found at dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3765766.3765803 in preview ↗ no scale claim — research (ACM CHI 2025) ↗ no public benchmark scores found Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ Cognitive-psychology-grounded memory model that matches human recall / forgetting curves. Descriptive model rather than production system; benchmark-only. ACM DL
SYNAPSE Spreading-activation graph + episodic-semantic split graph graph-traversal long-term extraction episode tagging agent could not infer from description LLM-arbitrate (spreading-activation graph) ↗ Memory is a dynamic graph; retrieval fires spreading activation across edges rather than vector similarity. Lateral inhibition suppresses competing activations; temporal decay modulates edge weights. Episodic and semantic stores maintained separately. +7.2 F1 on LoCoMo with 95% fewer tokens than full-context methods. Jan 2026. text ↗ 2026-01 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold 3 cites 3/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2601.02744; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ +7.2 F1 LoCoMo↗ Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ Synaptic-consolidation-inspired LLM memory. Preprint; biological-inspiration is interpretive. arxiv 2601.02744
HeLa-Mem Hebbian distillation + hippocampal-cortical consolidation graph graph-traversal long-term consolidation episode tagging agent could not infer from description LLM-arbitrate (Hebbian distillation + consolidation) ↗ Co-activation strengthens associative edges (Hebbian Distillation); separate consolidation pass periodically extracts semantic summaries from the episodic graph — directly modelling slow-wave hippocampal-to-cortical consolidation. Dual-path retrieval over both stores. Outperforms SOTA on temporal and multi-session reasoning. Apr 2026. text ↗ 2026-04 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold not yet researched (too new — Apr 2026) ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2604.16839; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ no public benchmark scores found Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ Hebbian-learning memory adapted for LLMs. Preprint; Hebbian rules applied speculatively. arxiv 2604.16839
NL2GenSym Soar production-rule generation + Execution-Grounded Critic kv exact-match long-term extraction skill deterministic LLM-arbitrate (production-rule generation) ↗ First end-to-end pipeline translating natural language into executable Soar rules. Imports the full Soar production-rule cycle: working memory, operator proposal/application, chunking. LLM generator proposes new productions; critic validates via live Soar execution. >86% rule-generation success across Gemini and Qwen families. Oct 2025. text ↗ 2025-10 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold not yet researched (Oct 2025 preprint) ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2510.09355; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ no public benchmark scores found Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ Natural-language-to-generative-symbolic memory mapping. Preprint; mapping quality varies. arxiv 2510.09355
AriGraph CLS dual-layer episodic + semantic KG graph graph-traversalsimilarity long-term extraction episode tagging agent could not infer from description LLM-arbitrate (dual-layer KG) ↗ Episodic observations as timestamped vertices; LLM extracts relational triplets into a semantic knowledge graph; episodic edges connect semantic nodes — structural equivalent of hippocampal index binding cortical representations. Dual-layer world model for interactive text agents. Outperforms vector retrieval and RL baselines on multi-hop planning. IJCAI 2025. text ↗ 2024-07 no-release no-release MIT ↗ 166★↗ Inform 7 below detection threshold 63 cites 31/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — research paper not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2407.04363; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ no public benchmark scores found No reproductions found ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Graph-based reasoning memory — explicit knowledge structure during reasoning. Research-stage; graph build cost limits real-time use. arxiv 2407.04363
Dual-Process Agent (DPA) Kahneman System 1 / System 2 vector similarity long-term consolidation summary tagging agent could not infer from description LLM-arbitrate (System 2 consensus write) ↗ System 1: fast retrieval path that pulls compressed, relevant memories and conditions generation. System 2: slow reflection pass that evaluates outcomes and writes curated updates back through a conservator gate. No model-weight changes. Outperforms vanilla prompting on six benchmarks with both GPT and Llama backbones. MDPI Electronics 2025. text ↗ 2026-03-16 (MDPI Electronics 15(6) 1232; published March 16 2026) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company MDPI Electronics 2025; no affiliation found at mdpi.com/2079-9292/15/6/1232 in preview ↗ no scale claim — research (MDPI Electronics 2025) ↗ no public benchmark scores found Too recent ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ System-1 / System-2 dual-process model applied to LLM agents. Dual-process framing is descriptive; production tradeoffs against single-loop agents are unclear. MDPI Electronics
Episodic Memory Is the Missing Piece CLS framework as design requirement tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (position paper; no system implementation) ↗ Pink et al. — position paper. Uses CLS theory to identify five structural properties of episodic memory absent from current LLM agents: single-shot acquisition, temporal context, source binding, flexible recombination, selective consolidation. Maps each to a research-roadmap requirement. Most rigorous theoretical bridging paper in the space — not an implementation, but a roadmap. Feb 2025. text ↗ 2025-02 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold 35 cites 35/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Pink et al. — no affiliation found; searched arxiv.org/abs/2502.06975 ↗ no scale claim — position paper ↗ no public benchmark scores found Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ Position paper arguing episodic memory is the gap in current LLM agents. Position paper; no method or empirical evaluation. arxiv 2502.06975
Retrieval-as-memory hybrids
Retrieval techniques (RAG variants) used as the recall mechanism for a memory system. The retrieval method is the memory architecture's read path. Where the line between "RAG" and "memory" blurs — both store knowledge externally and pull it on demand, but these techniques specifically address the memory-shaped retrieval problem.
RAPTOR Recursive abstractive summary tree vector similarityhybrid-rerank tagging agent could not infer from description read-only chunk tagging agent could not infer from description searched not found ↗ Recursively embeds, clusters, and summarises chunks into a tree of abstractions. Retrieval traverses the tree at query time. ICLR 2024. +20% absolute accuracy on QuALITY benchmark with GPT-4. Multi-step-reasoning state of the art on QA tasks at publication. searched not found ↗ 2024-02 no releases ↗ MIT ↗ 1.7k★↗ Python below detection threshold or not yet researched 452 cites 226/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not a company Stanford NLP; Parth Sarthi Salman Abdullah Aditi Tuli Shubh Khanna Anna Goldie and Christopher … ↗ searched not found ↗ no public benchmark scores found Independently reproduced ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Hierarchical clustering retrieval — multi-resolution recall over long documents; widely adopted in 2024 RAG stacks. Indexing cost grows with corpus; cluster quality depends on embedding choice. GitHub parthsarthi03/raptor arxiv 2401.18059 OpenReview (ICLR)
GraphRAG (Microsoft) Community summary + Leiden detection graphvector graph-traversalsimilarity long-term extraction documentsummary inspectable searched not found ↗ Modular graph-based RAG. Leiden community detection over knowledge graph; community summaries at multiple levels. DRIFT Search hybrid (Oct 2024). v1.0 GA; PyPI distributed. Indexing cost reported at $50–200 per 500 pages. searched not found ↗ 2024-03 3.0.9 2026-04-13 ↗ MIT ↗ 32.8k★ +189/mo↗ Python 1 inbound searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not a company Microsoft Research; lead authors Darren Edge (Sr Director) Ha Trinh (Sr Data Scientist) Jonatha… ↗ searched not found ↗ no public benchmark scores found searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ Most cited RAG-as-memory pattern of 2024 — graph extraction + community summaries gives it macro-level semantic recall most flat-RAG misses. Indexing cost is high (extraction LLM passes per chunk); operational complexity exceeds vanilla RAG significantly. GitHub microsoft/graphrag docs site project page Azure accelerator
LazyGraphRAG Deferred-indexing GraphRAG variant graph graph-traversalhybrid-rerank tagging agent could not infer from description read-only chunk tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (read-only retrieval; no write path) ↗ Performs minimal up-front indexing; defers LLM use until query time. Being integrated into the main GraphRAG library. Lower indexing cost than GraphRAG (claimed milestone for the GraphRAG repo). text ↗ 2024-03 3.0.9 2026-04-13 ↗ MIT ↗ 32.8k★ +189/mo↗ Python searched not found ↗ ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not a company Microsoft Research (GraphRAG team) ↗ no scale claim — research / Microsoft GraphRAG variant ↗ no public benchmark scores found ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Lazy / on-demand graph build for RAG — significantly cheaper than GraphRAG indexing. Query latency tradeoff vs pre-built graphs; quality varies by query complexity. integration discussion #1490 GitHub microsoft/graphrag
HippoRAG / HippoRAG2 Personalised PageRank over KG graph graph-traversalsimilarity tagging agent could not infer from description read-only chunk tagging agent could not infer from description searched not found ↗ Hippocampus-inspired RAG. LLMs + knowledge graphs + Personalized PageRank to mimic neocortex/hippocampus roles. NeurIPS 2024. Up to +20% on multi-hop QA over SOTA. Single-step retrieval ≥ iterative IRCoT at 10–30× lower cost and 6–13× faster. v2 (Feb 2025) outperforms GraphRAG / RAPTOR / LightRAG. searched not found ↗ 2024-05 v1.0.0 2025-02-27 ↗ MIT ↗ 3.5k★↗ Python searched not found ↗ 103 cites 51/yr ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not a company Ohio State University NLP Group (OSU-NLP); Bernal Jiménez Gutiérrez Yiheng Shu Yu Gu Michihiro … ↗ searched not found ↗ no public benchmark scores found Independently reproduced ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Hippocampus-inspired single-pass retrieval — strong on multi-hop questions where multi-pass retrieval struggles. Index build cost is high; eval primarily on synthetic multi-hop tasks. GitHub OSU-NLP-Group/HippoRAG arxiv 2405.14831 NeurIPS 2024 PDF
LightRAG Dual-level graph retrieval graph graph-traversalsimilarity tagging agent could not infer from description read-only chunk tagging agent could not infer from description searched not found ↗ Entity-relationship graph + broader-theme retrieval. Incremental update support. Integrates with RAG-Anything for multimodal docs. EMNLP 2025 Findings. PathRAG Path-pruned graph retrieval graph graph-traversalhybrid-rerank tagging agent could not infer from description read-only chunk tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (read-only path retrieval) ↗ Retrieves key relational paths from knowledge graph; flow-based pruning + reliability scoring; path-based prompting. HyDE Hypothetical document embeddings vector similarity tagging agent could not infer from description read-only document tagging agent could not infer from description searched not found ↗ LLM generates a hypothetical answer document; embeds that instead of the query. Closes the query-document semantic gap for retrieval. arxiv 2212.10496. searched not found ↗ 2022-12 no releases ↗ Unlicensed ↗ 578★↗ Jupyter Notebook below detection threshold or not yet researched 667 cites 166/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not a company Carnegie Mellon University; Luyu Gao Xueguang Ma Jimmy Lin Jamie Callan; SIGIR 2023 ↗ searched not found ↗ no public benchmark scores found Independently reproduced ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Hypothetical-document embedding — embed the answer-shape rather than the query directly; widely adopted. Adaptive-RAG Query-complexity routing vector similarityhybrid-rerank tagging agent could not infer from description read-only chunk tagging agent could not infer from description searched not found ↗ Smaller classifier LM predicts query complexity, then routes to no-retrieval / single-step / iterative retrieval as appropriate. RouteRAG RL-routed text + graph + hybrid hybrid similaritygraph-traversalhybrid-rerank tagging agent could not infer from description read-only chunk tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (RL-routed retrieval; no persistent write) ↗ End-to-end RL-trained RAG. Token-level policy chooses when to retrieve and which mode (text / KG / hybrid via RRF). Two-stage Group-Relative Policy Optimisation (GRPO). No public repo at time of writing. +7.7 F1 over baselines with only 10k RL training samples. Reduces retrieval turns while maintaining performance. text ↗ 2025-12 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched 2 cites 2/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2512.09487; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ no public benchmark scores found Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ Routes queries across multiple retrievers based on question type. Beyond RAG for Agent Memory Decoupled-aggregate memory retrieval hybrid similarityhybrid-rerank tagging agent could not infer from description read-only chunk tagging agent could not infer from description LLM-arbitrate (semantic unit decomposition) ↗ Argues standard RAG breaks on bounded agent conversation streams (highly correlated, redundant spans). Decomposes the stream into semantic units, organises them into themes, then inverts that structure for retrieval. Consistent gains on LoCoMo and PerLTQA across diverse LLMs. Feb 2026. text ↗ 2026-02 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched 2 cites 2/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2602.02007; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ no public benchmark scores found Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ Position paper arguing RAG is insufficient for agent memory. Position paper; no concrete method. arxiv 2602.02007
Generative Semantic Workspace (GSW) Episodic memory for RAG vector similarity tagging agent could not infer from description read-only episode tagging agent could not infer from description LLM-arbitrate (Reconciler integration) ↗ Replaces chunk-retrieval with neuro-inspired generative memory. Operator maps observations to intermediate semantic structures; Reconciler integrates them into a persistent workspace with temporal/spatial/logical coherence. Targets 100k–1M-token corpora. +20% over RAG baselines on EpBench; 51% reduction in query-time context tokens. AAAI 2025. text ↗ 2025-11 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched 3 cites 3/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2511.07587; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ no public benchmark scores found Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ Generative semantic workspace as memory substrate; novel architecture. TC-RAG Turing-complete state-variable RAG hybrid similarityhybrid-rerank tagging agent could not infer from description read-only chunk tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (Turing-complete state variables; read-only) ↗ Frames RAG as Turing-complete with explicit monitored state variables, enabling adaptive retrieval halting and convergence control. Medical-LLM case study. Adds latency; clarification step has its own failure modes. arxiv 2408.09199
KAG KG-grounded hybrid reasoning graph graph-traversalhybrid-rerank tagging agent could not infer from description read-only chunk tagging agent could not infer from description searched not found ↗ Knowledge-Augmented Generation. LLM-friendly KG representation + mutual indexing between KGs and raw chunks + logical-form-guided hybrid reasoning. Handles numerical / temporal / multi-hop chains that vector similarity misses. +19.6% F1 on 2WikiMultiHopQA, +33.5% on HotpotQA (relative). Deployed at Ant Group. +33.5% F HotpotQA↗		 No reproductions found ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Knowledge-augmented generation — extends RAG with explicit knowledge graph layer. Operational complexity higher than vanilla RAG. arxiv 2409.13731
Speculative RAG Small-model draft, large-model verify vector similarityhybrid-rerank tagging agent could not infer from description read-only chunk tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (read-only retrieval + verification) ↗ Smaller distilled specialist generates multiple RAG drafts in parallel from distinct document subsets. Larger generalist verifies in a single pass. Subsets formed by content-similarity clustering to minimise redundancy. +12.97% accuracy on PubHealth; 51% latency reduction vs conventional RAG. SOTA on TriviaQA, MuSiQue, ARC-Challenge. text ↗ 2024-07 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched 97 cites 48/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Google DeepMind; arxiv 2407.08223 ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ +12.97% PubHealth
SOTA (sc TriviaQA↗		 No reproductions found ↗ Paper only ↗ Generates candidate answers in parallel from retrieval, picks best. Preprint; parallel-generation cost. arxiv 2407.08223
CRAG Retrieval self-correction with web fallback vector similarityhybrid-rerank tagging agent could not infer from description read-only chunk tagging agent could not infer from description searched not found ↗ Lightweight retrieval evaluator scores docs and triggers different actions: use, supplement with web search, or discard. Decompose-then-recompose algorithm filters irrelevant content from retrieved passages. Consistently improves RAG and Self-RAG baselines across short- and long-form generation. Jan 2024. searched not found ↗ 2024-01 no-release no-release no-license ↗ 455★↗ Python below detection threshold or not yet researched 194 cites 97/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not a company Meta AI Research; Xiao Yang et al.; competitive RAG benchmark ↗ searched not found ↗ no public benchmark scores found Independently reproduced ↗ Code public + weights public ↗ Corrective RAG — model checks retrieved content and re-retrieves if wrong. Self-RAG Reflection-token self-critique vector similarity tagging agent could not infer from description read-only chunk tagging agent could not infer from description searched not found ↗ Trains an LLM end-to-end to emit reflection tokens that decide whether to retrieve, assess passage relevance, and critique output quality — all within a single model. Outperforms ChatGPT and retrieval-augmented Llama2-chat on open-domain QA, reasoning, fact verification.
