FLARE Confidence-triggered forward retrieval vector similarity tagging agent could not infer from description read-only chunk tagging agent could not infer from description searched not found ↗ Forward-Looking Active Retrieval. Uses model's low-confidence token predictions as signal to anticipate future information needs and retrieve proactively. Retrieves only when token probability falls below threshold; generator stays frozen. Superior or competitive on all four long-form knowledge-intensive tasks tested. Xu Graham Neu… ↗ searched not found ↗ no public benchmark scores found Independently reproduced ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Forward-looking active retrieval — model decides when to retrieve mid-generation. Extra LLM calls per generation; retrieval-decision quality is variable. arxiv 2305.06983
ITER-RETGEN Output-conditioned iterative retrieval vector similarity tagging agent could not infer from description read-only chunk tagging agent could not infer from description searched not found ↗ Uses model's generated output as a rich context signal for the next retrieval round rather than just the original query. Processes all retrieved knowledge as a whole, preserving generation flexibility. Superior or competitive on multi-hop QA, fact verification, commonsense reasoning. EMNLP 2023 Findings. searched not found ↗ 2021-05 no-release no-release no-license ↗ 116★↗ Python below detection threshold or not yet researched 472 cites 157/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not a company National University of Singapore; no individual authors confirmed ↗ searched not found ↗ no public benchmark scores found No reproductions found ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Iterative retrieve+generate cycle — explicitly improves over single-shot retrieval. Latency multiplied by iterations; convergence not guaranteed. arxiv 2305.15294
StructRAG Task-adaptive structurisation hybrid hybrid-rerank tagging agent could not infer from description read-only chunk tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (read-only structured retrieval) ↗ Identifies optimal structure (table, catalog, algorithm, graph) per task; converts source documents into that structure at inference time and reasons over the structured form. Motivated by cognitive theories of human knowledge conversion. SOTA on knowledge-intensive reasoning; strongest on multi-document scatter scenarios. HiRAG Hierarchical KG indexing + retrieval graph graph-traversalsimilarity tagging agent could not infer from description read-only chunk tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (read-only hierarchical KG retrieval) ↗ Multi-layer knowledge graph: higher layers hold coarse summary entities improving connectivity between similar lower-layer entities (HiIndex). HiRetrieval traverses across granularity levels. Outperforms baselines on QFS and multi-hop QA across all evaluated datasets. EMNLP 2025 Findings. text ↗ 2025-03 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched 27 cites 27/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2503.10150; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ no public benchmark scores found Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ Hierarchical RAG with explicit document-section / community structure. Extra retrieval cost; index build complexity. arxiv 2503.10150
CAG (Cache-Augmented Generation) KV-cache preloading replaces retrieval kv-cache cache-lookup session read-only document tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (KV-cache preload; no user write or conflict path — static preloaded documents) ↗ "Don't Do RAG." Preloads all relevant documents into context, caches the resulting KV state; at inference the model answers from cache with no retrieval step — eliminates retrieval latency + retrieval-error failure modes. Matches or beats RAG accuracy on knowledge tasks while removing retrieval overhead. Best for bounded, static corpora. ACM Web Conf 2025. searched not found ↗ 2024-12 no-release no-release MIT ↗ 1.5k★ +5/mo↗ Python below detection threshold or not yet researched 52 cites 26/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not a company National Taiwan University; no individual authors confirmed ↗ searched not found ↗ no public benchmark scores found Independently reproduced ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Prefill-cache reuse — when the corpus fits in context, retrieval can be replaced with cache lookup. Requires the corpus to fit in context; doesn't scale to large knowledge bases. arxiv 2412.15605
LongRAG Long retrieval-unit grouping vector similarity tagging agent could not infer from description read-only document tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (read-only retrieval) ↗ Groups related Wikipedia documents into 4K-token units. Reduces NQ corpus 22M→600K units, HotpotQA 5M→500K. Hands longer passages to a long-context reader (GPT-4o). Recall@1 NQ 52→71%; Recall@2 HotpotQA 47→72%. EM 62.7% NQ, 64.3% HotpotQA. Recall@1 NaturalQuestions↗		 No reproductions found ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Long-document RAG with explicit segment-level indexing. Preprint; segment-quality bounds recall. arxiv 2406.15319
Multi-Head RAG (MHRAG) Attention-head multi-aspect embeddings vector similarityattention tagging agent could not infer from description read-only chunk tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (read-only multi-aspect retrieval) ↗ Uses transformer multi-head attention activations (not decoder-layer embeddings) as retrieval keys, yielding multi-aspect embeddings that independently capture different semantic facets for heterogeneous queries. +20% retrieval success ratio over 18 RAG baselines on real-world use cases; same latency as standard RAG. text ↗ 2024-05 no-release no-release ↗ 242★↗ Python below detection threshold or not yet researched 29 cites 14/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2406.05085; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ no scale claim — research ↗ no public benchmark scores found No reproductions found ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Multi-head retrieval — different attention heads route to different retrievers. Preprint; head-attribution is interpretive. arxiv 2406.05085
BGE-M3 Unified dense-sparse-multi-vector embedding vector similarityhybrid-rerank tagging agent could not infer from description read-only chunk tagging agent could not infer from description searched not found ↗ Single embedding model supporting dense, sparse, and multi-vector (ColBERT-style) retrieval simultaneously. 100+ languages, inputs up to 8192 tokens. Self-knowledge distillation across the three modes. +5.1 points on BEIR/MTEB over dense-only. SOTA on multilingual + long-document retrieval. searched not found ↗ 2023-08 v1.4.0 2026-04-22 MIT ↗ 11.6k★ +142/mo↗ Python below detection threshold or not yet researched 1.2k cites 620/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not a company Beijing Academy of AI (BAAI); Jianlv Chen et al.; dense/sparse/multi-vector retrieval ↗ searched not found ↗ no public benchmark scores found Independently reproduced ↗ Code public + weights public ↗ Multi-lingual, multi-functionality, multi-granularity embedding model. Preprint; embedding-model rather than full RAG architecture. arxiv 2402.03216
ChunkRAG LLM-scored chunk-level filtering vector similarityhybrid-rerank tagging agent could not infer from description read-only chunk tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (read-only chunk-level filtering) ↗ Semantic chunking divides documents into coherent sections; LLM-based relevance scorer filters each chunk against the query before generation, pruning irrelevant chunks and reducing hallucination. Higher accuracy on fact-checking and multi-hop reasoning vs existing RAG; reduces hallucination. Validated on ARAGOG (423 papers, 107 QAs). Strong CRAG benchmark challenge submissions. REALM Retrieval-augmented LM pre-training vector similarity parametric-permanent parametric-edit document tagging agent could not infer from description searched not found ↗ Foundational paper. Augments LM pre-training with a latent knowledge retriever — model retrieves and attends over Wikipedia-scale corpus during pre-training, fine-tuning, and inference. Backprop through retrieval over millions of documents. RETRO Trillion-token retrieval transformer vector similarity long-term read-only chunk tagging agent could not infer from description searched not found ↗ DeepMind. Retrieval-Enhanced Transformer with chunked cross-attention over a 2T-token retrieval database. Frozen BERT retriever + differentiable encoder. 7.5B-param RETRO outperforms 175B Jurassic-1 on 10/16 datasets and 280B Gopher on 9/16 — equivalent quality at 25× fewer parameters. Quality continues improving up to 2T retrieval tokens. searched not found ↗ 2022-01 0.3.9 2023-10-30 ↗ Apache-2.0 ↗ 880★↗ Python below detection threshold or not yet researched 1.6k cites 324/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not a company DeepMind (London); 28 authors including Sebastian Borgeaud Arthur Mensch Jordan Hoffmann Lauren… ↗ searched not found ↗ no public benchmark scores found Original team only ↗ Paper only ↗ DeepMind retrieval augmentation — model trained with retrieval as a first-class component. Closed weights; not reproducible; superseded by simpler RAG approaches in practice. arxiv 2112.04426 DeepMind blog PyTorch impl
Atlas Few-shot retrieval-augmented LM vector similarity long-term parametric-edit document tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (retriever + LM fine-tuning; no persistent write at inference) ↗ Meta AI. Jointly fine-tunes retriever + LM for few-shot tasks. Product-quantised compressed index achieves quality comparable to uncompressed at 5× lower memory footprint. text ↗ 2022-11 no-release no-release ↗ 558★↗ Python below detection threshold ↗ 1.2k cites 296/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial searched not found ↗ not applicable — not a company Meta AI; arxiv 2208.03299 ↗ 26.8M Wikipedia documents (research corpus) ↗ +80% acc BABILong↗ Independently reproduced ↗ Code public + weights public ↗ Meta retrieval architecture for few-shot learning. Research scope; less production utility than fine-tuned RAG. arxiv 2208.03299
File-backed / editor paradigms
Memory implemented as plain-text files under version control, injected at session start. No database, no extraction LLM, no vector index — just human-readable rules and notes. Optimal when memory is curated, low-volume, and benefits from human review (CLAUDE.md, Cursor rules, Windsurf rules). The opposite philosophical pole from extraction-and-storage memory layers.
CLAUDE.md Git-backed persistent instructions file injection cross-session overwrite document user-controllable manual (git-versioned; human edits) ↗ Markdown file injected at session start; reloaded post-compaction. Document-as-memory: human-readable, version-controlled, no extraction or vector store. Anthropic-canonical pattern; equivalent emerges in Cursor / Windsurf. text ↗ 2024-10 (first appeared in Claude Code public release; Claude Code beta May 2024; GA Oct 2024) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Anthropic (CLAUDE.md pattern) ↗ no scale claim — single markdown file per project ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Plain-text + git-versioned + human-reviewable — the highest-trust memory format with zero infra; works in any editor or terminal. Manual curation required; doesn't scale beyond ~10–20 KB before context bloat; no semantic recall, only injection-by-default. Claude Code memory docs
Cursor Rules IDE-embedded code memory file injection cross-session overwrite document user-controllable manual (file-based; human edits) ↗ .cursorrules + project rules. Semantic chunking + Merkle-tree incremental re-indexing over the codebase. Turbopuffer-backed retrieval at IDE scale. text ↗ 2023-03 (Cursor launched March 2023; .cursorrules file supported from initial release) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Anysphere Inc. (Cursor) — ex-OpenAI / MIT founders; a16z-backed ↗ no scale claim — files at IDE scale (Turbopuffer-backed) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Rule-file format integrated directly into the IDE that 35M+ developers use; specific selectors (file globs) make rules precise. Cursor-only — no portability to Claude Code or other agents; rules can quietly conflict and produce confusing agent behavior. Cursor docs — Rules cursor.com
Windsurf Rules & Memories Codebase semantic + auto-memory filevector injectionsimilarity cross-session overwriteextraction documentfact user-controllable LLM-arbitrate (Cascade auto-generated memories) ↗ Rules files plus Cascade auto-generated memories. "Fast Context" RAG using SWE-grep models; ~100K-token effective context. text ↗ 2024-11 (Windsurf Editor launched November 2024; Cascade Memories feature included at launch) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Codeium (Varun Mohan & Douglas Chen, co-founders; ex-NVIDIA; General Catalyst-backed) ↗ ~100K-token effective context (vendor claim) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Two-tier model — explicit rules + implicit auto-memory — covers both curated and emergent guidance; Codeium ecosystem maturity. Auto-memory layer is opaque (no inspect UI); rules + memories together can compound into context bloat. Windsurf Memories docs windsurf.com
AGENTS.md Cross-tool open standard file injection cross-session overwrite document user-controllable manual (git-versioned; human edits) ↗ AGENTS.md at repo root (and ~/.codex/AGENTS.md for global); hierarchical concatenation from root to cwd. Persists build/test commands, conventions, architecture overview, security constraints, git workflow. Donated to Linux Foundation's AAIF (Dec 2025). Adopted by 20,000+ repos. Read by Codex CLI, GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Claude Code, Factory.ai. text ↗ 2025-05 (introduced with OpenAI Codex CLI research preview May 16 2025; donated to Linux Foundation AAIF Dec 2025) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Open standard (community-driven); no single founding org ↗ no scale claim — hierarchical markdown files ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Cross-vendor convention — the closest thing to a universal agent memory file format; works across Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex CLI. Adoption is informal (no spec authority); agent-by-agent interpretation differences mean behavior isn't fully portable. agents.md GitHub Blog
GitHub Copilot custom instructions .github/copilot-instructions.md + path-scoped file injection cross-session overwrite document user-controllable manual (git-versioned; human edits) ↗ .github/copilot-instructions.md repo-wide; .github/instructions/<name>.instructions.md for path-scoped rules with YAML frontmatter (applyTo glob). Persists style, test conventions, language preferences. Vendor-supported across VS Code, JetBrains, GitHub.com chat. text ↗ no data no data — repo … no data — no data — repo unreachable ↗ 0★↗ no data — repo unreachable searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — file pattern included in GitHub Copilot subscription ($10/mo individual; $19/mo business; $39/mo e… ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — GitHub-hosted; no separate deployment ↗ not applicable — not a company GitHub / Microsoft ↗ no scale claim — markdown files in repo ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Reaches the largest installed base of AI-using developers; integrated where developers already work; backed by GitHub's persistence. Repository-scoped instructions only — no persistent learning; less expressive than Cursor Rules' selector model. docs
Aider CONVENTIONS.md / .aider.conf.yml Multi-file convention pattern file injection cross-session overwrite document user-controllable manual (file-based; human edits) ↗ CONVENTIONS.md (any name, loaded via --read or read: in config); .aider.conf.yml (home / git-root / cwd, last wins); .aiderignore (gitignore-syntax). Mature, widely used. text ↗ 2023-06 (Aider launched June 2023 by Paul Gauthier; CONVENTIONS.md pattern included from early versions) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Paul Gauthier (founder; ex-Google SWE); open-source / indie ↗ no scale claim — markdown / YAML config files ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Plain-text + git-versioned conventions file — the most CLAUDE.md-like memory pattern outside Anthropic's tools. Aider-only; manual curation required; no semantic recall. conventions docs YAML config
Cline .clinerules Version-controlled, AI-editable rules file injection cross-session overwrite document user-controllable manual (git-versioned; AI-editable) ↗ .clinerules single file or .clinerules/ directory of numbered .md files (e.g. 01-style.md). Global at ~/.cline/rules/. Files version-controlled and AI-editable — Cline can write back. Toggleable per-session via UI. Vendor-supported (Cline VS Code extension). text ↗ 2024-07 (Cline launched as Claude Dev July 2024; rebranded Cline Oct 2024; .clinerules added post-launch) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo 1 inbound not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Cline (Saoud Rizwan, founder; open-source; ex-indie developer; VS Code extension) ↗ no scale claim — markdown files in repo ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Cline IDE plugin's rule file — git-versioned, IDE-aware; auto-loaded per project. Cline-only; rule file maintenance burden; no auto-learning. Cline blog docs
Cline Memory Bank Session-spanning state pattern file injection cross-session overwrite document user-controllable manual (human-curated markdown) ↗ memory-bank/ directory of Markdown: projectBrief.md, productContext.md, activeContext.md, systemPatterns.md, techContext.md, progress.md. Loaded in full at start of every task. Compensates for context-window reset between sessions. Convention loaded via .clinerules; also an MCP server variant. Pattern, not built-in. text ↗ 2024-11 (community pattern formalized after Cline's October 2024 rebrand; adopted ~Nov-Dec 2024) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Cline (Saoud Rizwan, founder; open-source) ↗ no scale claim — markdown directory (~6 standard files) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Structured memory bank pattern (multiple files) — explicit decomposition of project context across 6 canonical docs. Cline-only convention; community pattern, not a tool — discipline-dependent. Memory Bank docs
Roo Code .roorules Per-mode rules + workspace overrides file injection cross-session overwrite document user-controllable manual (file-based; human edits) ↗ .roo/rules/ (recursive .md/.txt alphabetic load); .roorules single-file fallback. Mode-specific via .roo/rules-{modeSlug}/. Global at ~/.roo/rules/. Multi-tenant mode customisation. VS Code extension (fork of Cline). text ↗ 2024-12 (Roo Code forked from Cline in late 2024; .roorules added as distinct pattern post-fork) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Roo Code (open-source VS Code extension; community-maintained) ↗ no scale claim — markdown files ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Roo Code's rule file — git-versioned conventions distinct from Cline's despite forking heritage. Roo-only; rule format diverges from Cline so cross-tool portability isn't trivial. custom instructions docs
Continue.dev .continue/rules Path-glob-scoped rules file injection cross-session overwrite document user-controllable manual (file-based; lexicographic load) ↗ .continue/rules/*.md at workspace root. YAML frontmatter: name, globs (file-pattern trigger), alwaysApply, description. Loaded lexicographically. Path-scoped rules apply only when matching files in context. text ↗ 2023-07 (Continue.dev YC S23 launched July 2023; rules feature added in later iterations) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Continue.dev (Ty Dunn & Nate Sesti, co-founders; ex-Palantir; YC S23) ↗ no scale claim — markdown files ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Open-source IDE extension's rule file format; matches the OSS ethos of the broader Continue.dev project. Continue.dev-only; rule format is its own; no cross-tool standard. rules docs
Zed .rules Reads competitors as fallback file injection cross-session overwrite document user-controllable manual (file-based; human edits) ↗ .rules at project root. Falls back to reading .cursorrules, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md (first match wins). Plain Markdown, no frontmatter schema. Injected as system prompt for all Agent Panel interactions. Notable: only editor that explicitly reads competitors' files as aliases. text ↗ 2024-08 (Zed AI launched August 22 2024 with Anthropic collaboration; .rules pattern came with or shortly after) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Zed Industries (Nathan Sobo & Antonio Scandurra, co-founders; ex-GitHub Atom; a16z-ba… ↗ no scale claim — single markdown file ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Zed editor's native rules file; benefits from Zed's high-performance Rust-built editor base. Zed-only; smaller user base than Cursor / VSCode-derived editors. Zed rules docs
Replit replit.md Project-level Replit Agent context file injection cross-session overwrite document user-controllable manual (file-based; human edits) ↗ replit.md at project root. Persists project overview, tech stack, architectural decisions, deployment process, external resources. Scoped to Agent conversations only. Cloud-IDE context only. text ↗ 2024-09 (Replit Agent launched September 2024; replit.md memory file part of Agent feature) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Replit (Amjad Masad & Haya Odeh, co-founders; Y Combinator; a16z / Andreessen-backed) ↗ no scale claim — single markdown file per project ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Replit's project-level memory file — automatic context for the agent inside Replit's hosted environment. Replit-only; tied to Replit's hosted workspace model. replit.md docs
Sourcegraph Cody — cody.json Custom command templates file injection cross-session overwrite document user-controllable manual (file-based; human edits) ↗ .vscode/cody.json (workspace-scoped). JSON defining slash-commands with prompt text, context options (currentFile, openTabs, terminal), and mode (ask/edit/insert). Reusable prompt templates for project-specific workflows; checked in for team sharing. Commands rather than freeform instructions. text ↗ 2023-09 (Cody custom commands experimental in VS Code v0.10 Sep 3 2023; GA Dec 2023) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Sourcegraph (Quinn Slack & Beyang Liu, co-founders; a16z-backed) ↗ no scale claim — JSON file per workspace ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Cody's project config file with explicit tool / context definitions; benefits from Sourcegraph's code intelligence. Cody-only; Sourcegraph deployment expected for full benefit; enterprise positioning. custom commands
Claude Code memory mechanisms
The specific memory features available inside Claude Code — what this CLI session uses to remember things across runs. Distinct from the "Anthropic Claude Memory" entry above because this is the CLI / IDE-integration tier (auto-memory directory, MCP plugins, MEMORY.md indices), not the consumer chat product.
Claude Code auto-memory Per-project file directory file injectionextraction-pull cross-session append-onlyoverwrite factdocument user-controllable append (no resolution — files accumulate; human can edit) ↗ Persistent file-based memory at ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/ with a MEMORY.md index plus typed memory files (user / feedback / project / reference). Loaded into context across sessions. text ↗ 2025-03 (Claude Code v1.0 launched Feb/Mar 2025; auto-memory feature in subsequent releases; v2.1.59+ required) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Anthropic ↗ no scale claim — markdown files per project ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Per-project automatic memory built into the runtime — no plugin install, no external store, no extra prompts. Single-vendor lock-in to Claude Code; no inspect/edit UI for what's been auto-saved; opaque to other tools. Claude Code memory docs
Superpowers episodic-memory plugin MCP semantic search over conversations vector similarity cross-session append-only episode inspectable not applicable (read-only search over past sessions) ↗ Claude Code plugin that indexes prior conversations at session end and exposes search/show tools via MCP. Distributed via obra/superpowers-marketplace; install with /plugin install episodic-memory@superpowers-marketplace. text ↗ 2025-10 v1.2.0 2026-05-04 ↗ MIT ↗ 372★ +10/mo↗ TypeScript 372 GitHub stars; niche community signal ↗ not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial free / open source (MIT); no paid tier ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial local — runs as Claude Code plugin via superpowers marketplace; no cloud component ↗ not applicable — not a company obra (Jeremy Penner / open-source community) ↗ no scale claim — per-project SQLite index ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper OSS Claude Code plugin that adds explicit episodic memory across sessions — user surfaced this in their own settings. Plugin maintenance dependent on author; less polished than first-party auto-memory. GitHub obra/episodic-memory superpowers-marketplace obra/superpowers framework launch post
claude-mem Episodic / session-log compression file injection cross-session consolidation summaryepisode inspectable append (no resolution — compression logs) ↗ Hooks into Claude Code's session lifecycle; compresses tool outputs into ~500-token observations via Claude's agent SDK. SQLite + FTS5 with optional Chroma vector. "Endless Mode" compresses in-flight to extend session length. Captures what the agent did, not just what the user typed. 46k★, 223 releases, 92 contributors. Distributed via Claude Code plugin marketplace. text ↗ 2025-09 v12.7.0 2026-05-06 ↗ AGPL-3.0 ↗ 72.7k★ +9.1k/mo↗ TypeScript 72.7k GitHub stars (+9.1k/mo); highest-starred Claude Code memory plugin ↗ not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial free / open source (AGPL-3.0); no paid tier ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial local — SQLite + FTS5 per project; optional Chroma vector; no cloud ↗ not applicable — not a company thedotmack (open-source; no institutional affiliation) ↗ no scale claim — SQLite + FTS5 per project ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper OSS plugin that adds auto-memory + indexing without sending data to Anthropic's cloud — strongest privacy posture for Claude Code memory. Plugin install + maintenance burden; less polished than first-party auto-memory. GitHub thedotmack/claude-mem
Mem0 MCP (official) Hybrid vector + graph via cloud API vectorgraph similaritygraph-traversal long-term extraction factepisode opaque LLM-arbitrate ↗ Mem0's managed cloud API exposed as MCP tools. Semantic search + CRUD on memories scoped by user / agent / session. Cloud-hosted MCP endpoint — no local infrastructure required. v0.2.1 (Dec 2025). PyPI: mem0-mcp-server. text ↗ 2025-02 no releases ↗ Apache-2.0 ↗ 650★ +10/mo↗ Python 650 GitHub stars (mem0-mcp repo); mem0 main repo 21.8k stars ↗ not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial free tier (Hobby: 10K memories 1K retrieval/mo); Starter $19/mo; Pro $249/mo (graph memory included); Enterprise custom ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial cloud API (managed by Mem0); MCP local install pointing to Mem0 cloud ↗ not applicable — not a company Mem0 (YC S24; Taranjeet Singh & Deshraj Yadav) ↗ cloud-managed; no published per-user cap ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper MCP server that brings the Mem0 layer into any MCP-compatible agent — including Claude Code — without rewriting code. Inherits Mem0's tradeoffs (extraction approach, opaque eviction); MCP surface adds one more network hop on every memory call. GitHub mem0ai/mem0-mcp docs
Graphiti MCP Server (Zep) Temporal KG with bi-temporal edges graph graph-traversal long-term append-only episodefact auditable bi-temporal versioning ↗ Tracks how facts change over time; maintains provenance; supports prescribed and learned ontology. Bi-temporal edges (when known vs when valid). Only server that distinguishes "fact learned" from "fact true." 20k★. v1.0 shipped Nov 2025. text ↗ 2024-08 v0.29.0 2026-04-27 ↗ Apache-2.0 ↗ 25.7k★ +137/mo↗ Python 25.7k GitHub stars (graphiti repo); strong adoption signal ↗ not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial free tier: 2500 messages/mo + 2.5MB graph data; paid: $1.25/1000 messages + $2.50/MB extra; Enterprise custom ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial cloud (Zep managed) or self-hosted via Graphiti OSS + Neo4j/local graph DB ↗ not applicable — not a company Zep AI (YC W24; Daniel Chalef) ↗ no scale claim — local graph (no upper bound stated) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper MCP exposure of Graphiti's bi-temporal KG gives any agent contradiction-aware memory; first-party vendor support. KG memory is heavier than vector for the same data; MCP setup more involved than installing an embedded SDK. GitHub getzep/graphiti Zep KG MCP
mcp-memory-service (doobidoo) KG + vector hybrid + autonomous consolidation graphvector graph-traversalsimilarity long-term extractionconsolidation factepisode inspectable LLM-arbitrate (autonomous consolidation) ↗ SQLite-Vec default, Cloudflare hybrid for production. Typed edges (causes, fixes, contradicts). Autonomous consolidation compresses old memories. SSE real-time notifications + D3.js visualisation dashboard. Agent-pipeline focused (LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen). Self-compresses aging memories to address context-window pressure. text ↗ 2024-12 v10.49.4 2026-05-05 ↗ Apache-2.0 ↗ 1.8k★ +25/mo↗ Python 1.8k GitHub stars; niche developer tooling ↗ not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial free / open source (Apache-2.0); no paid tier ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial local — SQLite-Vec default; Cloudflare hybrid for production; no managed cloud ↗ not applicable — not a company doobidoo (open-source; no institutional affiliation) ↗ no scale claim — SQLite-Vec / Cloudflare Vectorize ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper OSS MCP memory server with vector + KV backends; flexible deployment options. Author-maintained; documentation uneven; not actively heavily adopted. GitHub doobidoo
OpenMemory MCP Local Postgres + Qdrant cross-tool memory vectorrelational similarity long-term extraction fact user-controllable LLM-arbitrate ↗ Local-first self-hosted Mem0 variant. Shared memory layer bridges Claude Desktop, Cursor, and Windsurf in one session. All data stays on-machine. Docker Compose self-host. Cross-tool memory passport: write in Claude Desktop, read in Cursor. text ↗ 2025-03 (Mem0 launched OpenMemory in early 2025; Docker Compose self-host) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Mem0 (YC S24; Taranjeet Singh & Deshraj Yadav) ↗ no scale claim — local Postgres + Qdrant ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper MCP exposure of the OpenMemory protocol — cross-tool memory portability vision in MCP form. Protocol still emerging; ecosystem support thin outside Mem0. mem0.ai/openmemory
Cognee MCP Multi-layer KG with rule extraction graph graph-traversal long-term extraction fact inspectable LLM-arbitrate ↗ "Cognify" extracts entities and relationships into a structured graph. Saves interaction patterns as coding rules; uses both vector and graph DBs. Bundled in Cognee v0.3.5+. Benchmarks claim superiority over Mem0, LightRAG, Graphiti on multi-hop QA. text ↗ 2023-08 v1.0.8 2026-05-06 ↗ Apache-2.0 ↗ 17.1k★ +152/mo↗ Python 17.1k GitHub stars (cognee repo); strong signal for OSS KG memory ↗ not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial free / open source (Apache-2.0) for self-hosted; cloud tier pricing not published ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial self-hosted (local vector + graph DB); cloud option exists (topoteretes managed) ↗ not applicable — not a company Cognee (EU startup; Vasilije Markovic & Boris Arzentar) ↗ no scale claim — local vector + graph DB ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper MCP server that brings Cognee's ECL pipeline into any MCP-compatible agent. Setup more involved than embedded SDK; inherits Cognee's tradeoffs. GitHub topoteretes/cognee
Hindsight MCP Four-network episodic memory graph graph-traversal long-term extraction factepisode inspectable LLM-arbitrate ↗ World (facts), Experiences (agent actions), Opinion (beliefs with confidence scores), Observation (mental models). Entity resolution ("Alice" and "my coworker Alice" same node). Mimics human recall topology. Claude Code hooks for automatic cross-session memory. text ↗ 2025-04 (Hindsight by Vectorize; Claude Code hooks referenced in late 2025) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Vectorize / Hindsight (True Ventures; Chris Latimer & Chris Bartholomew) ↗ no scale claim — local vector store ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper MCP server for Hindsight memory layer; gives any MCP agent access to Vectorize-tuned retrieval. Newer; smaller adoption than Mem0 MCP / Graphiti MCP. Hindsight MCP docs
Memobase MCP Profile + timeline injected into prompt relational injection long-term extraction profileepisode inspectable LLM-arbitrate ↗ Structured JSON user profile + chronological event timeline. Postgres + Redis. Optimised profile/timeline split reduces LLM calls by 40-50% in v0.0.40. Profile-orientation for chatbot personalisation rather than coding agents. FastAPI server; PyPI distributed. text ↗ 2024-09 no releases ↗ Apache-2.0 ↗ 2.7k★ +10/mo↗ Python 2.7k GitHub stars (memobase repo); niche adoption ↗ not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial free / open source (Apache-2.0); no published cloud pricing ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial self-hosted — Postgres + Redis; Docker Compose; no managed cloud offering confirmed ↗ not applicable — not a company Memobase (open-source; no institutional affiliation found) ↗ no scale claim — Postgres + Redis ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper MCP server for Memobase; brings user-profile-centric memory to any MCP-compatible agent. claude-supermemory Team-scope memory plugin vector similarity long-term extraction factdocument opaque LLM-arbitrate ↗ Wraps Supermemory cloud API. mcp-memory (Puliczek) Cloudflare-native vector memory vector similarity long-term overwriteappend-only chunk inspectable LLM-arbitrate ↗ Embeddings via @cf/baai/bge-m3; Vectorize + D1; per-user namespace isolation. local-memory-mcp SQLite + FTS5 with decision-tracking relational exact-matchsimilarity long-term overwriteappend-only factdocument inspectable append (no resolution — decision-log accumulates) ↗ SQLite + FTS5 + BM25 ranking. Stores learnings, decisions with reasoning and alternatives, entities, observations. Loads context from last 3 sessions. Only server that captures why a decision was made + alternatives considered. Zero config, no cloud, no API keys. npx @studiomeyer/local-memory-mcp. text ↗ 2026-04 v1.0.7 2026-04-21 ↗ MIT ↗ 5★↗ TypeScript 5 GitHub stars; very early / below threshold ↗ not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial free / open source (MIT); no paid tier ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial local — SQLite + FTS5; zero cloud dependency; no API keys required ↗ not applicable — not a company studiomeyer-io (open-source; no institutional affiliation) ↗ no scale claim — SQLite + FTS5 per user ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Local-only MCP memory server — no cloud round-trip; fastest possible memory ops. Local-only; no cross-device sync; smaller community. GitHub studiomeyer-io
claude-brain Memvid → Claude Code plugin file similarity long-term append-only chunk inspectable append (no resolution — Memvid append-only) ↗ Claude Code plugin packaging of Memvid (see Dedicated memory layers). Wraps the Memvid .mv2 single-file store as a slash-command-installable plugin so per-project memory becomes a git-committable file inside the repo. Plugin distribution of an existing engine — claims inherit from Memvid (sub-ms reads, single-file portability). text ↗ 2025-12 v1.0.7 2026-01-05 ↗ MIT ↗ 489★↗ TypeScript below detection threshold or not yet researched not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial free / open source ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial local ↗ not applicable — not a company Memvid project (open-source; no institutional affiliation) ↗ single .mv2 file (no upper bound stated) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Claude Code plugin that bundles Memvid as the per-project memory store; git-committable single-file. Plugin layer on top of Memvid — adds another moving part for the same underlying engine. GitHub memvid/claude-brain
engram Static Go binary, agent-agnostic relational exact-match long-term agent-controlled factdocument user-controllable append (no resolution — agent decides what to save) ↗ SQLite + FTS5; agent decides what to save (not firehose capture). Single Go binary, no CGO, no Node/Python/Docker dependencies. HTTP API + MCP + CLI + TUI; optional cloud sync with local-source-of-truth. Works across Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Codex, Cursor, Windsurf with no runtime deps. text ↗ 2026-02 v1.15.8 2026-05-05 MIT ↗ 3.2k★ +1.1k/mo↗ Go below detection threshold or not yet researched not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial free / open source ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial local — command-line tool for Claude Code context ↗ not applicable — not a company Gentleman-Programming (open-source; no institutional affiliation) ↗ no scale claim — SQLite + FTS5 per user ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Static Go binary, agent-agnostic — works across Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Codex without runtime deps. Smaller community; SQLite + FTS5 backend lacks vector recall vs other options. GitHub Gentleman-Programming/engram

Official MCP Memory server KG-based persistent memory MCP graph graph-traversal cross-session overwriteappend-only fact inspectable LLM-arbitrate ↗ Knowledge-graph-based persistent memory MCP server — the canonical reference implementation. npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-memory; configurable via MEMORY_FILE_PATH. text ↗ 2024-11 2026.1.26 2026-01-27 ↗ Custom ↗ 85.1k★ +4.8k/mo↗ TypeScript 2 inbound not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial free / open source (MIT) ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial local (Node.js MCP server; local JSON file) ↗ not applicable — not a company Anthropic / Model Context Protocol (Anthropic open-source) ↗ no scale claim — local JSONL file ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Reference implementation in the MCP spec; smallest possible memory primitive — useful for testing and lightweight cases. Minimal feature set — no extraction, no governance, no retention policy; production deployments quickly outgrow it. GitHub source npm package official MCP registry
Knowledge-graph platforms
Graph databases (RDF, property-graph) used as the substrate for AI memory. Not memory products themselves, but where graph-shaped memory products like Zep/Graphiti, Cognee, and Neo4j-backed agents typically sit. Worth understanding the substrate even if you don't operate one directly — the choice of graph DB constrains what kinds of memory queries are cheap.
Stardog RDF semantic platform + Voicebox AI graph graph-traversalsimilarity long-term overwriteappend-only factdocument auditable none specified ↗ RDF-native semantic platform with SHACL validation, virtual graphs, and the Voicebox AI assistant. $32.5M funding. Pitches itself as a governed-knowledge layer. text ↗ 2010 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo 1 press ~100 (Stardog L jobs ↗ not applicable — not academic $32M Series C (last known) · 2022-01↗ 51-200 ppl Fortune 500 enterprises, pharma financial services government↗ Enterprise only ↗ SOC 2 Type II ↗ Does not sell customer data; grants data rights only for product operation and maintenance ↗ Both ↗ US ↗ Kendall Clark (CEO/co-founder), Michael Grove, Evren Sirin ↗ no scale claim ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Mature semantic graph + virtualization (data fabric over relational/cloud sources); strong inference rules engine. Enterprise-tier pricing and complexity; smaller community than Neo4j; license shifted to Stardog Studio commercial focus. stardog.com Voicebox
TopQuadrant (TopBraid EDG) Ontology-native governance graph graph-traversal long-term overwrite factdocument auditable none specified ↗ Ontology-native semantic governance suite. Heavy operational overhead; documentation in English lags Chinese resources. nebula-graph.io
Vector-database infrastructure
Vector databases used as the substrate for semantic-recall memory. Same role as the knowledge-graph platforms — substrate beneath the memory product. Most dedicated memory layers are built on one or more of these; choice of vector DB sets the cost / latency / scale envelope of the memory product above it.

Activeloop Deep Lake Multimodal vector + serverless Postgres vectorcolumn similarity long-term overwriteappend-only chunkdocument inspectable overwrite ↗ Deep Memory feature optimises embedding space per use-case (+22% retrieval accuracy). Deep Lake PG unifies serverless Postgres (agent short-term state) + billion-scale vector search (long-term memory). +22% retrieval accuracy claim. Reports 80% cheaper than comparable vector DBs. text + image ↗ 2018 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo n/a (no top PyPI pkg) 1 press ~30 (Activeloop jobs ↗ not applicable — not academic ~$20M total (Seed + $11M Series A) ↗ searched not found ↗ Free + paid ↗ SOC 2 Type II; SAML/RBAC features ↗ searched not found ↗ Both ↗ US ↗ Davit Buniatyan (founder/CEO; Princeton origin) ↗ no scale claim ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Multi-modal data lake (vectors + images + video) with version control — strong for ML data pipelines plus memory. Less developer-API-friendly than Pinecone / Weaviate; positioning straddles ML training and runtime memory. activeloop.ai

Converged document + vector store for agentic RAG. text ↗ 2016 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo 2 inbound not applicable — not academic $311M VC pre-IPO; IPO 2017 NASDAQ:MDB; revenue $2.46B FY2026 ↗ 65200+ total customers as of FY2026 ↗ Pay-per-use ↗ SOC 2 Type II; ISO 27001; ISO 27017; ISO 27018; HIPAA; FedRAMP (Atlas Gov); PCI-DSS; GDPR-compliant ↗ Never trains on customer data ↗ Managed-only ↗ US ↗ Dwight Merriman (co-founder), Kevin P. Ryan (co-founder), Eliot Horowitz (CTO/co-founder) ↗ no scale claim ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Vector search inside the database your app already uses — eliminates a separate vector DB for many MongoDB-shop architectures. Vector index quality lags dedicated engines on benchmark recall; pricing tied to Atlas tier. Atlas Vector Search
pgvector PostgreSQL vector extension vectorrelational similarityexact-match long-term overwriteappend-only chunk inspectable overwrite ↗ Stores embeddings alongside relational + full-text data. HNSW + IVFFlat ANN indexes. Used as agent conversation memory via LangChain + MCP. Foundation of Supabase AI and many self-hosted RAG stacks. PostgreSQL license. Most-deployed open-source vector store. text ↗ 2021-04 no releases ↗ Custom ↗ 21.1k★ +123/mo↗ C 3 inbound not applicable — not academic No external funding — open-source project by independent developer ↗ searched not found ↗ Free (PostgreSQL License open source) ↗ Inherits PostgreSQL security model; no standalone vendor compliance ↗ searched not found ↗ Self-hosted only (PostgreSQL extension; managed via Supabase/AWS RDS/etc.) ↗ searched not found ↗ Andrew Kane (sole maintainer/creator; first release April 2021) ↗ no scale claim ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Postgres extension — keeps vector data alongside transactional data with full ACID; minimal ops for shops already running Postgres. Performance plateau at very large vector counts; limited filter pushdown vs purpose-built engines. GitHub pgvector

Enterprise-search adjacencies
Enterprise search products that solve memory-shaped problems for organisations rather than for agents — KCS-style governance, per-user knowledge graphs, curated bibliographic databases. Worth knowing because they handle organisational scale and governance issues that agent memory products are still figuring out.
Glean Enterprise search + per-user KG vectorgraph similaritygraph-traversal long-term extraction documentfact auditable none specified ↗ Enterprise search with 100+ connectors. Personalised per-user knowledge graph. No governance layer. text ↗ 2019-01 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo 660 jobs 6 press↗ not applicable — not academic $765M total $7.2B val Series F · 2025-06↗ $208M ARR 501-1000 ppl Reddit, Instacart, Pinterest, Duolingo, Databricks…↗ Enterprise only ↗ SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, ISO 42001, HIPAA, GDPR-compliant ↗ Never trains on customer data (zero-retention agreements with all model providers) ↗ Both ↗ US ↗ Arvind Jain (CEO/co-founder; ex-Google distinguished engineer; ex-Rubrik co-founder), T.R. Vishwanath, Piyush Prahl… ↗ internal — undisclosed ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Most polished enterprise AI search product — connectors, governance, ranking, and conversational interface tightly integrated; high enterprise NPS. Indexed-content-volume pricing scales aggressively; less suited to large enterprise corpora than Glean. algolia.com
Vertical / domain-specific AI memory
AI products and platforms whose memory features are tuned for a specific vertical — healthcare, legal, science, gaming, robotics, customer support. Distinct from horizontal memory infrastructure because the memory model is shaped by domain semantics (causal biology graphs, matter-context, NPC personalities, scene graphs, ticket histories) rather than generic facts.
— Scientific / research AI memory
Tools where the memory layer is the architectural differentiator — persistent causal graphs, claim-to-sentence provenance, experiment-loop memory — rather than plain LLM-over-papers retrieval.
FutureHouse Cross-experiment + organism-scale factbase graphvector graph-traversalsimilarity long-term extraction factdocument opaque LLM-arbitrate ↗ Full autonomous-scientist loop. Owl agent surfaces prior work so downstream agents don't re-derive known results. WikiCrow produced cited factsheets for 15,616 previously undocumented human genes; Phoenix uses accumulated experiment context to plan chemistry work. Philanthropically funded (Eric Schmidt-backed). Autonomous gene-PCOS hypothesis claim. text ↗ 2023-11 (co-founded November 2023 by Sam Rodriques and Andrew White) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold ↗ not applicable — not academic Philanthropically funded (Eric Schmidt) + Edison spinout raised $70M seed at $250M valuation (Spark Capital / Triat… ↗ searched not found Enterprise only ↗ searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ Managed-only ↗ US ↗ Eric Schmidt-backed; founder Sam Rodriques (MIT Physics PhD; ex-Francis Crick Institute; neurot… ↗ no scale claim (philanthropically funded research; no u… ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Most aggressive agent-driven scientific research lab — memory of reading + experiment trajectories is central to the product premise. Research-positioned product — production deployment is for research teams, not general scientific software. futurehouse.org
Causaly Persistent causal knowledge graph graph graph-traversal long-term extractionoverwrite fact inspectable overwrite ↗ Drug discovery / target identification / causal mechanism tracing. The graph is the memory: 7 years of curated biomedical cause-effect relationships compounding with each new ingestion. Scientific RAG retrieves from a versioned causal substrate. 500M facts, 70M directional relationships. Major pharma partners. Drug-discovery scope only; commercial/subscription model; investor-facing pivots have introduced strategic uncertainty. benevolent.com
LabGenius Autonomous experiment memory (EVA platform) tagging agent could not infer from description agentic long-term extraction episodefact opaque not applicable (Design-Build-Test-Learn closed loop; no conflict resolution needed) ↗ Therapeutic-antibody discovery in a closed Design-Build-Test-Learn loop. EVA accumulates structured outcome data from each robotic experiment cycle and feeds it back to generative ML models directing the next design round. Genuine experiment-memory-as-training-signal. Robotic wet-lab integration. Niche to protein engineering / antibody discovery; not a memory product per se. labgenius.app
Elicit Session-persistent claim-to-sentence provenance relational exact-matchsimilarity cross-session extraction documentfact auditable append (no resolution) ↗ Systematic literature review + data extraction + meta-analysis. Every extracted claim anchored to its exact source sentence; Notebook persists paper collections across projects. Research Agents (Dec 2025) carry evidence forward across review stages. Scite.ai Intent-classified citation context index vectorrelational similarityexact-match long-term extraction documentfact inspectable append (no resolution) ↗ Stores surrounding textual context of every citation plus a deep-learning classification (supports / disputes / mentions). Persisted across the database, not computed on demand. MCP integration (2025) exposes the memory layer to Claude and ChatGPT. ResearchRabbit Iterative paper-graph "rabbit-hole" checkpoints graph graph-traversal cross-session append-only document user-controllable append (no resolution) ↗ Literature discovery + citation mapping. Oct 2025 revamp formalises checkpointed search paths as named "rabbit holes" — each checkpoint is a saved state of the citation graph the user can branch from. Navigational memory rather than stateless re-querying. Graph-checkpoint memory model. text ↗ 2021 (founded 2021 in Seattle; acquired by Litmaps May 2025) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold ↗ not applicable — not academic TA Ventures investor; acquired by Litmaps May 2025 ↗ searched not found ↗ Free (RR+ premium tier with country-parity pricing) ↗ searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ Managed-only; acquired by Litmaps May 2025 ↗ US (Seattle) ↗ searched not found ↗ no scale claim ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Visual citation graph navigation — memory is the mental model of related-work landscapes. Visual-discovery scope; less useful for direct Q&A or summarization. researchrabbit.ai
— Healthcare AI memory
Healthcare AI products with distinctive memory layers — longitudinal patient stores, EMR-filtered retrieval, ambient-plus-historical reconciliation. Excludes plain LLM-over-EHR products without a documented memory architecture.
Hippocratic AI Polaris Longitudinal multi-call patient memory store vector similarityinjection long-term append-only episodefact auditable append (no resolution) ↗ Patient-conversation / chronic-care staffing model. HIPAA-compliant Memory Store holds non-EHR salient facts from prior calls, persists across engagements, explicitly not used for model training. Shifts from transactional call-handling to longitudinal patient relationship. 4.2T-param constellation of 22 LLMs (Polaris 3.0). 99.38% clinical accuracy. $404M total raised; Series C $126M at $3.5B valuation. UHS deployments; KPMG collab. Call duration 5.5 → 9.5 min; patient willingness to confide 88.9% → 94.6%. voice + text ↗ 2024-03 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo 2 HN · 2 press↗ not applicable — not academic $320M total $3.5B val Series C · 2025-11↗ 51-200 ppl Cleveland Clinic, Northwestern Medicine, Ochsner Health, Moffitt Cancer Center, University Hospitals…↗ Enterprise only ↗ SOC 2 Type II, HITRUST e1, HIPAA ↗ Never trains on customer data (BAA governs all PHI; de-identified data only for model improvement per HIPAA standards) ↗ Managed-only ↗ US ↗ Stanford / Johns Hopkins / Google advisors; founder Munjal Shah (UCSD CS BS; Stanford CS MS; se… ↗ internal — undisclosed (HIPAA-governed; no upper limit … ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Patient-facing voice agent with healthcare-tuned safety constellation — memory of past patient interactions feeds longitudinal care. Closed product; deployment requires healthcare org partnership; clinical-evidence track record still emerging. Polaris 3.0 Memory Store
K Health EMR-filtered two-agent pipeline relationalvector similarityinjection long-term extraction episodefact auditable none specified ↗ Primary care / virtual physician intake. Two-agent pipeline: one extracts the relevant EMR subset, second answers grounded to that filtered history. Architecture explicitly diverges by patient condition (diabetic vs heart-failure get different responses to same intake). Matched physician decisions in 2/3 of real cases. Fine-tuned on Gemma 3 via Vertex AI. Live on Mayo Clinic Platform (May 2025); Northwell partnership; HBS case study. text ↗ 2016 (founded 2016 by Allon Bloch Ran Shaul et al.; New York) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic $50M $900M val Series F (approx) · 2024-07↗ 201-500 ppl Hospital systems, health systems, consumer telehealth↗ searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ US (New York) ↗ searched not found ↗ internal — undisclosed (proprietary EMR dataset) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Long-running clinical AI with memory grounded in millions of de-identified patient-symptom trajectories — actual ground-truth corpus. K Health's data is proprietary — memory benefits don't transfer to other products; primary care scope. khealth.com Knowledge Agent

Memory is single-encounter focused; cross-encounter clinical reasoning is less mature. nabla.com partnership
Suki AI Pre-visit summary + prior-note synthesis vectorrelational similarityinjection long-term extractionconsolidation episodedocument auditable none specified ↗ Doctor-assist ambient documentation. Pulls forward prior documentation into a pre-visit synthesis rather than waiting for the encounter. Patient summaries surface relevant insight from history before visit begins. Usage tripled in 2025; 400+ organizations; first ambient AI integrated with MEDITECH Expanse Document APIs; 80 languages. voice + text ↗ 2024-10 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic $70M total Series D · 2024-10↗ 51-200 ppl MedStar Health (strategic partner)↗ SaaS ↗ SOC 2 Type II; HIPAA; GDPR-compliant ↗ searched not found ↗ Managed-only ↗ US ↗ searched not found ↗ internal — undisclosed (400+ orgs) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Voice-first clinical assistant with strong EMR integration; memory is clinician-curated rather than auto-extracted. Workflow product more than memory product; cross-encounter or longitudinal patient memory is shallow. suki.ai
DeepScribe Longitudinal oncology pull-forward vectorrelational similarityinjection long-term extraction episodedocument auditable append (no resolution) ↗ Specialty-care ambient scribe (oncology focus). Pulls forward prior notes with precise dates; synthesises interval history (what changed since last visit); auto-populates disease-status and treatment-plan context. 5M oncology visits/year. 98.8 KLAS score (A+ all six categories, 2025 Emerging Company Spotlight). 4× monthly oncology visits YoY. Randomised clinical trial published. voice + text ↗ 2022-01 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic $30M total Series A · 2022-01↗ 51-200 ppl↗ Enterprise only ↗ SOC 2 Type II; HIPAA; GDPR-compliant ↗ Trains on de-identified clinical data only with BAA in place; PHI never used without customer consent ↗ Managed-only ↗ US ↗ searched not found ↗ 5M oncology visits/year (vendor claim) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Clinical documentation platform with explicit longitudinal patient profile across visits; large enterprise health-system deployments. Enterprise-scope only; closed product; minimal developer-API exposure. deepscribe.ai
Abridge Grounded-transcript provenance filevector similarityextraction-pull long-term append-only episodedocument auditable append (no resolution) ↗ Clinician-assist ambient documentation. Source mapping: every AI-generated summary element traced back to the source utterance. Audit-and-trust layer over episodic memory. Built on proprietary 1.5M+ medical-encounter dataset. Deployed at major academic health systems. Inpatient + outpatient tools launched 2025. OpenEvidence (DeepConsult + Visits) Encounter-grounded evidence synthesis vector similarity session read-only document inspectable none specified ↗ Clinical decision support. Visits (Aug 2025) connects evidence layer to the live encounter — patient-specific context binding distinct from pure literature RAG. System learns each clinician's preferred note structure, phrasing patterns, symptom-outlining conventions — applies persistently across sessions. — Legal AI memory
Legal AI products with persistent matter / firm / institutional memory. T1 entries store decisions, accepted positions, and prior precedent that actively influence future outputs — not just CRM-style document lookup.
Harvey Memory 4-tier matter / firm / client-institution memory vectorgraph similaritygraph-traversalinjection long-term extraction episodedocumentfact auditable none specified ↗ Personal lawyer memory + matter memory + institutional firm memory + client-institution memory. Opt-in at user level with admin kill-switches and ethical-wall controls. Announced Jan 2026. $190M ARR, 1,000+ customers at announcement. text ↗ 2024-07 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic $1.1B total $11.0B val Growth · 2026-03↗ $195M ARR 201-500 ppl A&O Shearman, Macfarlanes, Mori Hamada & Matsumoto, A&L Goodbody, Paul Weiss…↗ Enterprise only ↗ SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, GDPR-compliant, EU-US DPF ↗ Never trains on customer data (contractual commitment to all AI providers: no retention, no human access, no training on customer data) ↗ Both ↗ US ↗ searched not found ↗ internal — undisclosed (1000+ enterprise clients) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Most established legal AI brand — Harvey Memory adds firm + matter + drafted-document recall on top of mature legal model. Luminance Institutional Memory Persistent negotiation history + accepted-risk registry vectorrelational similarityinjection long-term extractionoverwrite episodedocumentfact auditable overwrite ↗ Jan 2026 platform relaunch. Persists negotiation history, prior decisions, accepted risk positions across all contracts; new terms align with what the org has already agreed. Branded as the antidote to "enterprise amnesia." North America 127% YoY 2025. Spellbook Library Firm-precedent corpus memory vector similarity long-term extraction document auditable none specified ↗ Transactional / contract drafting. Ingests entire precedent corpus (OneDrive, Dropbox); ML surfaces and reuses the firm's own prior clauses during live drafting in Word, tuned to specific deal context. — Customer support / voice agent memory
CX, voice, and contact-center AI products. T1 = system writes durable, queryable customer-level state that the AI retrieves autonomously. T2 = well-integrated CRM reader without independent memory layer.

Agent Data Platform links unstructured conversation data to enterprise systems (billing, inventory, policies, transactions); persists across channels and time. — Gaming / NPC character memory
AI products for game NPCs, character chat, interactive fiction, and AI companions. Memory layer encodes character knowledge (what each character knows), personality continuity, and persistent world state.
Inworld AI Long-term character memory + Character Brain vectorgraph similaritygraph-traversal long-term extractionconsolidation episodefact opaque LLM-arbitrate ↗ Leading purpose-built NPC engine. Long-Term Memory (named feature) stores interaction facts across sessions; surfaces them contextually. Character Brain coordinates multiple ML models. Personality defined by trait axes, backstory, goals, emotional state. Unity, Unreal, NVIDIA ACE integrations. $120M raised (Lightspeed, KP, Founders Fund, M12, Samsung NEXT). text ↗ 2022-01 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo 10 jobs 9 HN · 1 press↗ not applicable — not academic $50M total $500M val Series A · 2023-08↗ 51-200 ppl Xbox, Ubisoft (NEO NPC prototype), NVIDIA, Kinetix, Shiseido↗ Free + paid ↗ SOC 2 Type II; GDPR-compliant ↗ searched not found ↗ Both ↗ US ↗ Lightspeed / Kleiner Perkins / Disney Accelerator; co-founders Ilya Gelfenbeyn and Michael Ermo… ↗ no scale claim ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Most adopted NPC AI platform — memory model handles personality, mood, relationship state in addition to conversation history. Game-engine-tied integrations; production deployment requires game studio engineering effort. inworld.ai

Scene awareness layer adds in-engine perception. Unity + Unreal SDKs with real-time voice + lipsync. 15k+ developers. $5M seed. Characters can deliberately lie / misremember and track when players have lied. Latitude / Voyage World Engine — separate state-management AI graphvector graph-traversalsimilarity long-term agent-controlled episodescene-graph opaque none specified ↗ AI Dungeon's maker. Voyage (April 2026) launched as a creator platform for AI RPGs. World Engine (5 years in dev) is dedicated AI tracking characters, objects, backstories, relationships independently from the narrative AI; persistent world state evolves with player choices. Voyage in open beta. AI Dungeon launched 2019 with millions of players. text ↗ 2019 (Latitude founded 2019 by Nick Walton and Alan Walton; Provo UT; AI Dungeon launched 2019) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched not applicable — not academic $3.3M seed (NFX/Album VC/Griffin Gaming 2021) + Voyage backed by NFX/Google AI Futures Fund/Midjourney (2026 round) ↗ AI Dungeon 2M+ users; Voyage launched April 2026 ↗ Free + paid ↗ searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ Managed-only ↗ US ↗ Nick Walton (CEO/co-founder; BYU DL lab) and Alan Walton (co-founder) ↗ no scale claim (millions of AI Dungeon players) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Player-driven generative narrative product — memory is the gameplay; strong demonstration of long-running emergent state. Consumer game product, not a memory infrastructure product; not directly applicable to other agent contexts. latitude.io

Nomi AI Mind Map 2.0 — navigable memory graph graph graph-traversal lifelong extraction factepisode inspectable append (no resolution) ↗ AI companion built around long-term memory as primary differentiator. Mind Map 2.0 gives users a navigable graph of what their Nomi knows about them and how memories connect — the most transparent memory model in the companion segment. Users can edit/delete nodes. Strongest memory architecture in companion segment. text ↗ no data ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic Bootstrapped · no data↗ 1-10 ppl no data (consumer - bootstrapped)↗ Free + paid ↗ GDPR non-compliance: removed from Google Play in EU 2024; privacy policy stale (dated Apr 2023 as of 2025) ↗ User conversation data stored; DPO not confirmed; policy outdated ↗ Managed-only ↗ US ↗ Alex Cardinell (founder/CEO; Carnegie Mellon dropout; Towson MD) ↗ no scale claim ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Multi-companion / group-chat support with memory across multiple AI relationships — distinct from single-companion competitors. — Robotics / autonomous-driving / embodied memory
Memory architectures for embodied AI — robot manipulation, navigation, autonomous driving, humanoids. Distinct paradigm: spatial maps, scene graphs, world-model latents, episodic trajectories. Memory grounded in physical state rather than language.
Figure Helix Temporal vision memory + tactile buffer vectorkv attentioncache-lookup session overwrite episodekv-token opaque overwrite ↗ System 2 (7B VLM) feeds tokens to System 1 (80M visuomotor policy at 200 Hz). Temporal memory module maintains visual-state history; force-feedback buffered as tactile memory proxy. 500 hours of human demos. Deployed in BMW logistics. SOTA on humanoid logistics benchmarks (Scaling Helix paper). vision + sensor + text ↗ 2022 (Figure AI founded May 2022 by Brett Adcock; Sunnyvale CA) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo HN high: multiple top-100 posts ↗ not applicable — not academic $675M total $2.6B val Series B · 2024-02↗ 201-500 ppl BMW (11-month deployment - 30000 cars produced)↗ searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ Onboard GPU inference; robot performance logs feed training data flywheel ↗ Managed hardware deployment (robots at partner sites) ↗ US (Sunnyvale CA) Figure AI; founder Brett Adcock (ex-Vettery CEO acquired by Adecco; ex-Archer Aviation founder)… ↗ no scale claim (500 hours of human demos) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper First major humanoid platform with explicit episodic + semantic memory layers in a published architecture; backed by deep funding. Robot-specific (Figure platform); production deployment is years away; published memory details are still high-level. figure.ai/helix
π0.5 (Physical Intelligence) Multi-Scale Embodied Memory (short + long episodic) vector attentionsimilarity long-term append-onlyconsolidation episodetrajectory opaque append (no resolution) ↗ Extends π0 with MEM layer giving the policy short-term (within-task) + long-term (cross-task, >10-min horizon) memory. Co-trained on robot teleoperation + human video + text. Hierarchical inference: VLA predicts subtasks as text tokens, then executes as action chunks. Open-world generalisation to unseen household environments. Cleans an entirely new kitchen with no environment-specific training. vision + sensor + text ↗ 2024 (Physical Intelligence formally founded 2024; informal work began Q3 2023; Chelsea Finn Sergey Levine Karol Ha… ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic $400M total $2.0B val Series A · 2024-11↗ 51-200 ppl↗ searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ Trained on Open X-Embodiment + proprietary in-house datasets; open-source weights available ↗ Cloud inference + on-robot deployment (websocket streaming) ↗ US (San Francisco) Physical Intelligence; founders Sergey Levine (UC Berkeley robotics professor) Karol Hausman Ch… ↗ no scale claim ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Generalist robot policy with strong simulation-to-real transfer; memory is implicit in the policy network rather than explicit external store. Implicit memory is harder to inspect or edit; embodied agent only — not generalizable to LLM agents. π0.5 blog paper
NVIDIA Isaac GR00T N1.7 Slow VLM planner + fast diffusion action model kv-cache attention session read-only trajectory tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable ↗ Dual-system (VLM "System 2" + Diffusion Transformer "System 1") jointly trained end-to-end; planner outputs persist as conditioning context across action generation. N1.7 adopts relative end-effector action space — incremental spatial memory via positional history. Open foundation model. Generalises across quadrupeds, tabletop arms, mobile manipulators. vision + sensor + text ↗ 2025-03 n1.7-release 2026-04-18 ↗ Apache-2.0 ↗ 6.9k★ +133/mo↗ Python searched not found ↗ 677 cites 677/yr↗ searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial Open-source (Apache-2.0); self-hosted ↗ US (NVIDIA Research) ↗ NVIDIA Research; corporate robotics foundation model team ↗ no scale claim (open foundation model) ↗ no public benchmark scores found Too recent ↗ Code public + weights public ↗ Foundation humanoid robot model from NVIDIA — the closest thing to a 'GPT for robots' so far. Outperforms LLM/VLM baselines on NaVQA across spatial, temporal, descriptive question types. vision + sensor + text ↗ 2024-08 no releases ↗ Custom ↗ 336★↗ Python searched not found ↗ 63 cites 31/yr↗ searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial Open-source (Custom NVIDIA license) ↗ US (NVIDIA Research) ↗ NVIDIA Research; no individual authors confirmed ↗ no scale claim (research; Nova Carter robot) ↗ no public benchmark scores found No reproductions found ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Episodic-memory robot reasoning framework — explicit memory of past attempts in a task.
