Autonomous driving scope only; closed weights; production deployment in vehicles still in pilot. GAIA-3 arxiv 2503.20523
Tesla FSD V13 — Occupancy + 4D World 3D occupancy network + temporal multi-modal fusion vectorkv-cache attention session overwrite episodescene-graph opaque overwrite ↗ Single end-to-end neural network ingesting multi-camera video + navigation + ego-motion. Maintains evolving 3D occupancy volume (voxel-level) + 3D Gaussian rendering as internal world state — spatial memory persisting across frames; tracks objects that temporarily leave the camera FOV. 70k GPU-hours per training cycle. 1.5 PB fleet data from 4M+ vehicles. vision + sensor ↗ 2024 (FSD v13 first released December 2024 to early access HW4 vehicles; wide release mid-December 2024) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic Public company (TSLA) ↗ ~12% FSD conversion rate among Tesla installed base; HW4 customers only for v13 ↗ $99/month subscription (as of Apr 2024); purchase price $8000 (discontinued Feb 2026 — subscription only) ↗ UNECE-neutered in EU; NHTSA-regulated in US; no SOC2/ISO listed ↗ Shadow fleet telemetry used for training; PHI not applicable; European UNECE restrictions limit maneuver scope ↗ HW4/AI4 Tesla vehicles only (on-device) ↗ US (Austin TX) ↗ Elon Musk (CEO/founder) ↗ 1.5 PB fleet data from 4M+ vehicles (vendor claim) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Largest fleet-driven memory in any robotic system — millions of vehicles producing data daily; 4D occupancy is the memory format. Tesla-only; closed system; safety / regulatory track record is contested. occupancy-networks writeup
Sanctuary AI Carbon (Phoenix Gen 7/8) Cognitive architecture with explicit task memory kvvector agenticsimilarity long-term extraction episodeskillfact opaque none specified ↗ Mirrors human cognitive functions: explicit memory, vision, hearing, tactile. Translates natural-language instructions into actions with explainable intermediate reasoning. Memory stores task procedures so learned skills are reusable across sessions without retraining. 110+ tasks in commercial deployment. ~40% of typical retail-store tasks covered. Task-onboarding reduced from weeks (2024) to <24h (Mar 2025). vision + sensor + text ↗ 2018 (Sanctuary AI founded 2018 by Geordie Rose et al.; Vancouver Canada) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic $58M total Strategic · 2024-07↗ 51-200 ppl Accenture, Bell, Magna, SE Health, Verizon Ventures…↗ Est. starting ~$65000 USD (configuration-dependent) ↗ searched not found ↗ High-fidelity teleoperation captures behavioral training data; haptic feedback from tactile sensors ↗ Hardware deployment at customer sites ↗ Canada (Vancouver BC) Geordie Rose, Suzanne Gildert, Ajay Agrawal, Olivia Norton (co-founders; Rose removed as CEO Nov 2024; Gildert depa… ↗ no scale claim (110+ tasks in commercial deployment) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Cognitive-architecture-inspired robot memory with explicit task-procedure store; humanoid platform with growing commercial deployment. Robot-specific (Phoenix platform); commercial deployment is early; generalization across embodiments is unproven. sanctuary.ai
Skild Brain In-context real-time memory + live data collection vector attentionsimilarity session overwrite episode opaque overwrite ↗ Real-time simultaneous inference + data collection — dynamically updates in-context representation as the robot encounters new situations. Lets the system adapt to novel embodiments / unseen environments without explicit fine-tuning. Pre-trained on internet human video + physics sim. ~$30M ARR within months of 2025 commercial launch. $1.4B raised at >$14B (Jan 2026). vision + sensor ↗ 2023 (Skild AI founded 2023 by Deepak Pathak and Abhinav Gupta; Pittsburgh PA) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic $1.7B total $14.0B val Series C · 2026-01↗ 51-200 ppl Samsung, LG, Schneider, CommonSpirit, Salesforce Ventures (strategic investors)↗ No public pricing; $4k–$15k target hardware cost enabled by Skild Brain ↗ Complex multi-jurisdiction compliance landscape (38 state AI laws + EU AI Act) ↗ 4-source data flywheel: simulation + internet video + teleoperation + real deployments ↗ Cloud inference + on-robot edge deployment ↗ US (Pittsburgh PA) Deepak Pathak (CEO) and Abhinav Gupta (President); Carnegie Mellon professors ↗ internal — undisclosed ($30M ARR; $14B valuation) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Real-time in-context adaptation — robot updates representation as it acts, instead of relying on offline fine-tuning between deployments. Robot-specific; rapid in-context updates raise stability concerns under distribution shift; no published failure-mode analysis. Skild brain blog
— Education AI memory
AI tutors, language-learning apps, and teacher productivity tools. Memory shape varies sharply: SRS state (Quizlet, Duolingo), pronunciation profiles (ELSA), curriculum mastery graphs (Khanmigo), per-student session logs (SchoolAI), or no student-facing memory at all (MagicSchool — by design). The architectural divide is whether memory lives in the AI tutor, the teacher dashboard, or a structured curriculum — three answers to "who carries the model of the learner."
ELSA Speak Evolving phoneme-level pronunciation profile relationalvector injectionsimilarity lifelong extractionoverwrite profilefact inspectable overwrite ↗ Persistent pronunciation profile per user, tracking which sounds, words, and patterns the learner consistently mispronounces. Self-evolving model re-evaluates and updates the profile as the learner improves. Competitive moat is a proprietary non-native accent training dataset, not a general LLM. 50M+ users in 200+ countries. $32.5M revenue 2023; $60M raised total. voice ↗ 2015 (ELSA Speak founded 2015 by Vu Van and Dr. Xavier Anguera; San Francisco) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic Series C · 2023-09↗ 51-200 ppl Consumer pronunciation learners, B2B enterprise↗ Free + paid ↗ GDPR-compliant; DPO appointed (Matan Cohen); data processing agreements per Art.28 GDPR; data deleted when purpose … ↗ Retains voice recordings for ASR improvement; DPO-governed; user deletion rights under GDPR/CCPA ↗ Managed-only ↗ US ↗ Vu Van (CEO/co-founder) and Xavier Anguera (CTO/co-founder) ↗ 50M+ users (pronunciation profiles; vendor claim) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Only product in this set with a genuinely domain-specific persistent model — pronunciation-error memory is precise and directly actionable, not fuzzy chat history. Scope is narrow (English pronunciation only); profile does not extend to grammar, vocabulary, or other language dimensions. elsaspeak.com ELSA AI
Duolingo Max SRS state + GPT-4 roleplay continuity relational injection lifelong overwrite profilefact inspectable overwrite ↗ Adds GPT-4-powered Roleplay (character conversations, with cross-call memory for "Lily") and Explain My Answer on top of Duolingo's existing spaced-repetition engine, which tracks per-word and per-skill mastery state across all sessions. ~9% of 12.2M paid subscribers on Max by end-2025 (~1.1M users). 78% of Roleplay users reported better real-world conversation readiness in internal surveys. voice + text ↗ 2011 (Duolingo founded 2011; Duolingo Max tier launched 2023) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo 2 HN · 1 press↗ not applicable — not academic $183M total raised (CapitalG/NEA/General Atlantic); public company (DUOL) ↗ 116.7M MAU / 40.5M DAU / 9.5M paid subscribers (Q3 2024); Max accounted for 5% of paid subs by end 2024 ↗ Free + paid ↗ searched not found ↗ Uses engagement data for personalization; no specific Max-tier training policy published ↗ Managed-only ↗ US ↗ Luis von Ahn (CEO/co-founder) and Severin Hacker (CTO/co-founder) ↗ internal — undisclosed (~1.1M Max subscribers) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Memory grounded in a proven SRS with decades of research behind it; Explain My Answer gives context-specific error feedback rather than generic responses. GPT-4 features sit on top of a lesson-completion model — no holistic learner profile; Roleplay memory is character-scoped, not learning-goal-scoped; $168/yr Max pricing. Max blog TC launch
Quizlet — AI Study Tools / Magic Notes Per-set Memory Score (SRS) + Magic Notes generation relational injection lifelong overwrite profilefact inspectable LLM-arbitrate (SRS scheduling per card) ↗ Tracks per-card answer history within a study set; computes a Memory Score for SRS scheduling. Magic Notes converts uploaded content (notes, PDFs, URLs) into flashcard sets automatically. Q-Chat, the conversational AI layer, was discontinued June 2025. Personalization is scoped to individual study sets, not a unified cross-subject learner model. 600M+ study sets created. Used by ~60% of US high school students. text + image ↗ 2005 (Quizlet founded 2005 by Andrew Sutherland; AI features/Magic Notes added 2023-2024) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched not applicable — not academic $62M total raised (General Atlantic/Icon Ventures) ↗ 50M+ MAU; 1M+ customers; 130+ countries ↗ Free / Plus $4/mo annual ↗ searched not found ↗ Usage data used for predictive models and content recommendations per privacy policy ↗ iOS + Android + Web ↗ US (San Francisco) ↗ Quizlet (Andrew Sutherland, founder; high-school project origin; General Atlantic-bac… ↗ internal — undisclosed (500M+ users; vendor-stated) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper SRS scheduling is algorithmically mature; Magic Notes dramatically reduces friction creating structured study material from unstructured content. Memory is siloed per study set with no cross-set or cross-subject learner model; Q-Chat shutdown left a gap in adaptive tutoring; memory does not carry forward to user-created content. AI Study Tools SRS feature
MagicSchool Teacher tooling — no student-facing memory by design tagging agent could not infer from description injection session read-only tagging agent could not infer from description opaque not applicable ↗ Teacher productivity platform (80+ teacher tools, 50+ student tools) for planning, differentiation, IEP generation, feedback. Does not maintain a cross-session learner model. Teachers input student context manually each session; district deployments can pre-populate fields via RAG over uploaded curriculum/policy documents. ~6M educators. 40% of US public schools. 13,000+ schools/districts in 160+ countries. 77% of teachers report significantly improved quality of life. text ↗ 2023 (launched March-May 2023 by Adeel Khan; Denver CO) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo 3 HN · 1 press↗ not applicable — not academic $15M total Series A · 2024-06↗ 11-50 ppl↗ Free + paid ↗ FERPA; COPPA; GDPR-compliant; SOC 2 Type II ↗ Never trains on student data; data not sold to third parties ↗ Managed-only ↗ US ↗ searched not found ↗ internal — undisclosed (~6M educators; 40% US public sc… ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Largest educator user base in this set; district-level RAG (curriculum + policy docs) is a practical and architecturally honest form of context persistence that does not require a student profile. No student-facing memory or adaptive personalization — context is ephemeral and teacher-supplied each session; deliberate product choice but disqualifies meaningful student-memory classification. magicschool.ai Impact Report
Khanmigo Chat history + Khan Academy mastery state relational injection cross-session append-only episode auditable append (no resolution) ↗ Stores full chat transcripts per student (visible to learner and teacher via dashboard). Layers over Khan Academy's existing mastery graph (exercise completion, skill progress) for cross-session topic awareness; cross-session memory is derived from structured mastery state rather than free-text recall. 2M students/educators/parents in 2024–25 school year. K–12 student usage 40k → 700k YoY (Khan Academy SY24-25 Annual Report). text ↗ 2023 (Khan Academy; Khanmigo launched March 15 2023 with GPT-4) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo 14 HN · 2 press↗ not applicable — not academic Non-profit (Khan Academy); no funding rounds ↗ searched not found ↗ Free + paid ↗ searched not found ↗ Never trains on third-party models on student data (Khan Academy controls data; COPPA/FERPA compliance) ↗ Managed-only ↗ US ↗ Sal Khan (founder; Khan Academy) ↗ internal — undisclosed (700k student users) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Uniquely integrates AI tutoring with a structured curriculum graph — memory is mapped to a known knowledge domain, not just chat history. Adoption is shallow — only ~15% of students with access actually use it; cross-session memory is bounded by mastery taxonomy rather than open-ended learning goals. khanmigo.ai Annual Report CBS 60 Minutes
SchoolAI Teacher-configured Spaces with observable session logs relational injection cross-session append-only episode auditable append (no resolution) ↗ Teacher-created "Spaces" (structured AI workspaces) where Dot, the student-facing assistant, guides learners through agendas. Student interaction data — completion, errors, time-on-task — surfaces to teachers via dashboards. Memory is within-space session context; the teacher holds the persistent profile, not the AI itself. 1M+ classrooms in 80+ countries; 400+ district partnerships. Raised $25M April 2025. text ↗ 2023 (co-founded January 2023 by Caleb Hicks Kevin Morrill Cahlan Sharp; Lehi UT) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic Seed · 2023-01↗ 11-50 ppl K-12 schools↗ Free tier available; paid depends on student count/features ↗ FERPA; COPPA; SOC 2 ↗ Student data FERPA-protected; not used for third-party training ↗ Web (K-12 classroom deployment) ↗ US ↗ Caleb Hicks (founder/CEO; former teacher) with Kevin and Cahlan (co-founders) ↗ internal — undisclosed (1M+ classrooms; 400+ districts) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Strongest teacher-visibility layer in this set — all student AI interactions are logged and observable, directly addressing the oversight gap in consumer AI tools used in classrooms. Persistent "memory" is a teacher-readable log, not a student-facing adaptive model; no mechanism for the AI to carry context across different Spaces or subjects. schoolai.com OpenAI case study Stanford SCALE
Speak Onboarding curriculum + per-session conversation relational injection cross-session overwriteextraction profileepisode inspectable overwrite ↗ Onboarding assessment builds an initial learner profile and generates a personalized curriculum; AI supports open-ended conversational practice with real-time speech recognition. No SRS or long-term vocabulary tracking — saved phrases go to a passive Phrasebook, not systematically reintegrated. Per-session context is strong; cross-session memory is structurally weak. 10M+ users; doubling annually for five years. $78M Series C at $1B (Dec 2024); $100M revenue 2025. voice + text ↗ 2016 (Speak founded by Connor Zwick and Andrew Hsu; Thiel Fellowship; San Francisco; YC backed) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched not applicable — not academic $162M total raised; Series C $78M at $1B valuation (Accel/OpenAI/Khosla/YC) Dec 2024 ↗ 10M+ users; 40+ countries; 1B+ sentences spoken in 2024 ↗ Free trial; Premium plans (multiple tiers); Enterprise (SSO/white-label) ↗ GDPR-compliant (enterprise compliance/procurement discussions available) ↗ Voice data used for ASR improvement under privacy policy; enterprise data handling via BAA-equivalent agreements ↗ iOS + Android + Web + Enterprise embedded ↗ US (San Francisco) ↗ Connor Zwick and Andrew Hsu (co-founders; Thiel Fellowship) ↗ internal — undisclosed (10M+ users; $100M revenue 2025) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Strongest real-time conversational AI of any language app — speech recognition, scenario roleplay, instant feedback are tightly integrated; OpenAI-backed. Cross-session memory acknowledged as a gap by reviewers — no SRS, no systematic error tracking across conversations; Phrasebook is passive rather than adaptive. speak.com OpenAI case study TC Series C
Synthesis Tutor Real-time micro-assessment adaptation kv injection session overwrite profile inspectable not applicable (micro-assessment adaptation; no persistent conflict) ↗ Embeds micro-assessments into every lesson to track understanding in real time, adjusting difficulty and teaching approach within session. Combines AI personalization with human-curated content. No publicly disclosed mechanism for a durable cross-session learner profile beyond progress tracking within its math curriculum scope (ages 5–11). On pace for $10M+ revenue 2025. Subscribed students up 4.5x YoY. text ↗ 2020 (founded 2020 by Josh Dahn and Chrisman Frank; Synthesis Tutor AI product added later) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic $12M Seed · 2024-01↗ 11-50 ppl 21500+ students, SpaceX-origin program↗ $20–$45/month; annual from $99/year (up to 7 kids) ↗ COPPA-compliant ↗ Child privacy-first; COPPA-governed data handling ↗ iOS (iPad) + Web ↗ US ↗ Synthesis (Josh Dahn, co-founder; ex-SpaceX Ad Astra teacher; Josh Hug co-founder; SF… ↗ internal — undisclosed ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Combines AI-driven real-time adaptation with neuroscientist- and educator-designed content; praised for age-appropriate pacing. Limited to math, ages 5–11; memory is effectively session-scoped adaptive state, not a long-term learner model. synthesis.com/tutor Unite.AI review
ChatGPT Study Mode Socratic mode + general ChatGPT Memory vector similarity cross-session extraction fact user-controllable LLM-arbitrate (Socratic mode + general ChatGPT Memory) ↗ Activates a Socratic interaction style — guiding questions, scaffolded concepts, knowledge checks rather than direct answers. With ChatGPT Memory enabled, persists learning goals and facts across sessions. Memory is not study-mode-specific; it is the same general ChatGPT Memory, so personalization depth depends on whether the user has memory enabled and populated. Launched July 30, 2025. Available to all logged-in ChatGPT users (Free, Plus, Pro, Team). Goal-setting and cross-conversation progress tracking marked as future work. text ↗ 2025-07-29 (ChatGPT Study Mode publicly launched July 29 2025; OpenAI) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo 1 press↗ not applicable — not academic Public company (OpenAI) ↗ 300M+ weekly ChatGPT active users ↗ Included in ChatGPT Plus ($20/mo) and Pro ($200/mo); available on Free tier with limits ↗ SOC 2 Type II, ISO/IEC 27001:2022, ISO 27017, ISO 27018, ISO 27701, CSA STAR L1 ↗ Free/Plus: conversations may be used for training (opt-out available); Team/Enterprise: no training on customer dat… ↗ Web + iOS + Android ↗ US (San Francisco) ↗ OpenAI ↗ internal — undisclosed (ChatGPT Memory backend) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Only product in this set built on a general-purpose model with cross-domain breadth — student can study any subject without vendor having pre-scoped curriculum; lowest friction (free tier). Implemented as custom system instructions rather than trained behavior — OpenAI describes it as a temporary iterative approach; educational memory is a thin layer over general ChatGPT Memory, not a purpose-built learner model. OpenAI Study Mode FAQ Axios
Memory benchmarks & evaluation
The infrastructure used to measure every memory system in the table. LongMemEval, LoCoMo, ConvoMem, BABILong, MADial-Bench, and similar. Not memory systems themselves — but essential for reading the benchmark claims in the rest of the table critically. Each benchmark tests a slightly different thing (single-session recall, multi-session continuity, temporal reasoning, multi-hop, adversarial).
ImplicitMemBench Implicit / procedural memory benchmark tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description read-only tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (evaluation benchmark) ↗ 300 items across three paradigms: procedural memory (one-shot skill retention after interference), priming (theme-driven response biases), classical conditioning (CS-US exposure shaping first decisions). Unified learning-interference-test protocol with first-attempt scoring to isolate automatized behavior — distinct from explicit-recall benchmarks. 17 models evaluated; no model exceeds 66% overall. Top: DeepSeek-R1 65.3%, Qwen3-32B 64.1%, GPT-5 63.0%. Reveals dramatic asymmetries (inhibition 17.6% vs preference 75.0%). text ↗ 2026-04 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ 0 (too recent — April 2026 preprint; not yet indexed on Semantic Scholar) ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2604.08064; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ not applicable — not a memory product 64.1% ImplicitMemBench (overall)↗ Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ First benchmark targeting implicit (non-declarative) memory in LLMs; exposes a category of memory failure invisible to existing explicit-memory benchmarks. 300 items is small; "classical conditioning" in LLMs is contested mechanistically — operationalization may reflect prompt-following rather than true conditioning. arxiv 2604.08064
MemEvoBench Adversarial memory-evolution safety benchmark tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description read-only tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (evaluation benchmark) ↗ QA tasks across 7 domains / 36 risk types plus workflow tasks adapted from 20 Agent-SafetyBench environments with noisy tool returns; both use mixed benign-and-misleading memory pools in multi-round interactions. Targets memory-evolution safety, not recall accuracy — first benchmark specifically targeting adversarial degradation of evolving memory. Substantial safety degradation under biased memory updates; static prompt defenses insufficient. April 17, 2026. text ↗ 2026-04 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ 0 (too recent — April 17 2026 preprint; not yet indexed on Semantic Scholar) ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2604.15774; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ not applicable — not a memory product not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ Addresses a largely unexamined attack surface — adversarial memory injection into agents with evolving long-term stores — rather than standard retrieval accuracy. Too new for independent replication; 36 risk types lack a formal threat-model taxonomy; no comparison against known memory-safety defenses. arxiv 2604.15774
ICLR 2026 MemAgents Workshop Workshop venue — RL + cognitive psychology + LLM memory tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (workshop venue) ↗ Full-day hybrid workshop held April 27, 2026, Rio de Janeiro. Scope: episodic and semantic memory, working memory, parametric knowledge, KGs, vector DBs, retrieval pipelines, context management, long-context utilization, temporal credit assignment. Bridges RL, neuroscience, and cognitive psychology alongside the engineering track. Accepted papers include MemoryAgentBench (ICLR 2026 main track), CraniMem, and MemAgent oral. Full / short / tiny paper tracks. text ↗ 2026-04-27 (workshop held April 27 2026 at ICLR 2026 in Rio de Janeiro) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company ICLR 2026 organizing committee; hosted at sites.google.com/view/memagent-iclr26 ↗ not applicable — not a memory product not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Only dedicated memory-for-agents venue at a top-tier ML conference in 2026; neuroscience/RL bridge is a differentiator from purely engineering-oriented workshops. Workshop proceedings not yet indexed in major databases; paper acceptance criteria and review quality vary across workshop tracks. workshop site OpenReview ICLR virtual
LongMemEval Long-term chat-assistant memory across sessions tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description read-only tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable — read-only evaluation dataset 5 core memory abilities — info extraction, multi-session reasoning, temporal reasoning, knowledge updates, abstention — via 500 curated questions in synthetic chat histories. Two scales: LongMemEval-S (115K tokens), LongMemEval-M (up to 1.5M). Commercial models show ~30% accuracy drop vs oracle baselines. Primary agent-memory benchmark in 2025. ICLR 2025. text (synthetic multi-session chat histories) ↗ 2024-10 no releases ↗ MIT ↗ 740★↗ Python 240 citations (Semantic Scholar); most-cited dedicated agent-memory benchmark in 2025; commercial vendors (Mem0 Zep… ↗ 240 cites 120/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — open benchmark; free to use ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — evaluation harness; run locally against model API ↗ not applicable — not a company UCLA + Microsoft + Tencent; Di Wu (UCLA) Hongwei Wang Wenhao Yu Yuwei Zhang Kai-Wei Chang Dong … ↗ not applicable — not a memory product not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks Disputed ⚠ ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Most-cited memory benchmark — ICLR 2025; 5 core memory abilities across 500 curated questions. Synthetic chat histories; commercial-model accuracy drop suggests benchmark may overstate real-world failures. arxiv 2410.10813 GitHub xiaowu0162
LoCoMo Very long-term multi-session dialogue memory tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description read-only tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable — read-only evaluation dataset Conversations spanning up to 35 sessions / 300 turns / ~9K tokens, built from LLM-agent personas grounded on temporal event graphs and verified by human annotators. Tests QA (single-hop, multi-hop, temporal, commonsense, adversarial), event-graph summarisation, multimodal dialogue. ACL 2024. Used by MemoryOS, MemGPT, A-MEM evaluations. Snap Research / UNC origin. text + multimodal dialogue (includes image-grounded turns) ↗ 2025-01 no-release no-release ↗ 39★↗ Jupyter Notebook 395 citations (Semantic Scholar); heavily used as industry benchmark by Mem0 Zep Letta ByteRover etc ↗ 395 cites 197/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — open benchmark; free to use ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — evaluation harness; run locally ↗ not applicable — not a company University of North Carolina Chapel Hill + USC + Snap Inc.; Adyasha Maharana Dong-Ho Lee Sergey… ↗ not applicable — not a memory product not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks Disputed ⚠ ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Long-conversation benchmark — multi-session continuity, temporal reasoning. Limited domain coverage; commercial vendors have publicly disputed certain LoCoMo results. arxiv 2402.17753 project page
NIAH (Needle in a Haystack) Single-fact verbatim retrieval from long context tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description read-only tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (evaluation benchmark) ↗ Places a known sentence ("needle") at varying positions inside a long irrelevant document ("haystack") and prompts the model to retrieve it. Tests context lengths from 2K to model max. Greg Kamradt, 2023, no formal venue. De facto first-pass filter for long-context LLM releases. Now considered necessary-but-insufficient — most frontier models score near-perfect. text ↗ 2023-11 no releases ↗ MIT ↗ 2.3k★↗ Jupyter Notebook no academic citations (informal GitHub project); de-facto first-pass filter for all frontier LLM releases; universa… ↗ not applicable — not academic not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — open source; free to use ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — run locally ↗ not applicable — not a company Greg Kamradt (independent AI researcher); open-source 2023 ↗ not applicable — not a memory product not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Original needle-in-a-haystack benchmark — defined the long-context recall test paradigm. Single-pattern test; commercial models now ace it; less informative for modern memory architectures. GitHub gkamradt
BABILong Distributed multi-fact reasoning across long docs tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description read-only tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable — read-only evaluation dataset Extends 20 bAbI reasoning tasks (fact chaining, induction, deduction, counting, lists) by scattering relevant sentences across PG-19 book text. Scales to 10M tokens. Tests whether models can chain distributed facts, not merely retrieve a single needle. GPT-4 effectively uses only ~10% of its 128K window. NeurIPS 2024 Datasets & Benchmarks. Used to evaluate RAG, RMT, long-context LLMs. text (bAbI synthetic reasoning tasks interleaved with PG-19 book passages) ↗ 2024-02 no releases ↗ Apache-2.0 ↗ 248★↗ Jupyter Notebook 196 citations (Semantic Scholar); standard long-context reasoning eval especially for recurrence models ↗ 196 cites 98/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — open benchmark; free to use ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — run locally ↗ not applicable — not a company Sber AI + St. Petersburg HSE; no individual authors confirmed ↗ not applicable — not a memory product not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks Independently reproduced ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Extends bAbI tasks to very long context — clean methodology heritage. Synthetic; doesn't capture real-world memory complexity. arxiv 2406.10149 GitHub booydar
RULER Configurable multi-task long-context stress test tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description read-only tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable — read-only evaluation dataset NVIDIA. Extends NIAH with multi-needle retrieval, multi-hop entity tracing, aggregation, QA — configurable for arbitrary length / difficulty. Evaluated 17 long-context models on 13 tasks. All models degrade sharply as context grows despite near-perfect NIAH scores. Exposes that advertised context windows aren't fully usable. COLM 2024. text (configurable synthetic tasks) ↗ 2024-04 no releases ↗ Apache-2.0 ↗ 1.5k★↗ Python 820 citations (Semantic Scholar); heavily used in long-context LLM release evaluations by NVIDIA and others ↗ 820 cites 410/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — open benchmark; free to use ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — run locally ↗ not applicable — not a company NVIDIA Research; no lead author confirmed from search ↗ not applicable — not a memory product not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks Independently reproduced ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Rigorous long-context benchmark with multiple needle / multi-hop / aggregation tasks. Synthetic patterns; doesn't fully capture agent-memory dynamics. arxiv 2404.06654 GitHub NVIDIA/RULER
HELMET Holistic downstream-task long-context eval tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description read-only tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable — read-only evaluation dataset Princeton NLP. 7 task categories — RAG with real retrieval, cited generation, summarisation, re-ranking, classification, many-shot ICL, long-document QA — at lengths up to 128K. Model-based evaluation enables open-ended scoring. Study of 51 LCLMs found NIAH scores poorly predict downstream performance. Endorsed by Hugging Face. ICLR 2025. text (7 categories: RAG summarisation re-ranking classification many-shot ICL long-doc QA) ↗ 2024-10 no-release no-release MIT ↗ 214★ +2/mo↗ Jupyter Notebook 96 citations (Semantic Scholar); Hugging Face endorsed; Princeton NLP pedigree ↗ 96 cites 48/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — open benchmark; free to use ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — run locally ↗ not applicable — not a company Princeton NLP + AI2; Howard Yen Tianyu Gao Minmin Chen et al.; no lead confirmed ↗ not applicable — not a memory product not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks Independently reproduced ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Heterogeneous long-context benchmark across many task types. Limited scale; recall-only focus misses agent-memory nuance. arxiv 2410.02694 project page
InfiniteBench (∞Bench) 100K+ token realistic long-context comprehension tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description read-only tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable — read-only evaluation dataset Tsinghua / OpenBMB. First benchmark with average input >100K tokens. 12 tasks across English + Chinese: long novel QA, code debugging, math in long context, summarisation, retrieval. Realistic documents (novels, code) rather than synthetic filler. Adopted as 100K+ capability reference. Referenced in LongBench v2 + HELMET comparisons. ACL 2024. text (English + Chinese: novels code math summarisation retrieval) ↗ 2023-11 no releases ↗ MIT ↗ 382★↗ Python 330 citations (Semantic Scholar); adopted as 100K+ capability reference in LLM releases ↗ 330 cites 165/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — open benchmark; free to use ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — run locally ↗ not applicable — not a company Tsinghua University + ByteDance; no individual authors confirmed ↗ not applicable — not a memory product not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks Independently reproduced ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Extreme-long-context benchmark covering 100K+ tokens. Hard to interpret pass/fail at very long lengths; distributional realism limited. arxiv 2402.13718 GitHub OpenBMB
LongBench Bilingual multitask long-context suite tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description read-only tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable — read-only evaluation dataset Tsinghua THUDM. 21 datasets across 6 categories (single-doc QA, multi-doc QA, summarisation, few-shot ICL, synthetic, code completion) in English + Chinese. ~6.7K words English / 13.4K chars Chinese average. v2 at ACL 2025 with harder, longer instances. One of the most-cited long-context benchmarks. Standard evaluation in Chinese NLP and multilingual long-context papers. ACL 2024. text (bilingual: English + Chinese; 21 datasets) ↗ 2023-07 no releases ↗ MIT ↗ 1.2k★↗ Python 1200 citations (Semantic Scholar); one of most-cited long-context benchmarks; standard in Chinese NLP ↗ 1.2k cites 399/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — open benchmark; free to use ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — run locally ↗ not applicable — not a company Tsinghua KEG Lab; Yushi Bai Xin Lv Jiajie Zhang et al.; AAAI 2024 ↗ not applicable — not a memory product not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks Independently reproduced ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Balanced long-context tasks across domains; widely cited. Limited memory-specific tasks; primarily long-context recall not memory operations. arxiv 2308.14508 GitHub THUDM
MemBench Multi-dim agent memory mechanism comparison tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description read-only tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable — read-only evaluation dataset Renmin University. Evaluates LLM-agent memory mechanisms across three axes — effectiveness (accuracy), efficiency (LLM call count), capacity (degradation as memory grows). Distinguishes factual vs reflective memory, participation vs observation scenarios. Tested 7 mechanisms including GenerativeAgent, MemoryBank, MemGPT, SCMemory. ACL 2025 Findings. text (agent memory mechanisms across 7 evaluated systems) ↗ 2025-06 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched 35 cites 35/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company no data — 2025 arxiv benchmark paper ↗ not applicable — not a memory product not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ Dedicated memory benchmark with multiple memory operation types. Smaller adoption than LongMemEval; coverage less broad. arxiv 2506.21605 ACL Anthology
MemoryAgentBench 4-competency agent memory benchmark tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description read-only tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable — read-only evaluation dataset HUST. Reconstructs existing datasets into incremental multi-turn dialogue chunks fed in time order. Plus EventQA (accurate retrieval) and FactConsolidation (selective forgetting). Targets retrieval, test-time learning, long-range understanding, selective forgetting. Evaluates MIRIX, MemGPT, RAG agents — none master all 4 competencies. ICLR 2026 (accepted). Most recent agent-memory-specific benchmark. text (multi-turn dialogue fed as incremental chunks) ↗ 2025-06 no releases ↗ Unlicensed ↗ 323★↗ Python below detection threshold or not yet researched 66 cites 66/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — open benchmark; free to use (unlicensed GitHub) ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — run locally ↗ not applicable — not a company no data — 2025 arxiv benchmark paper ↗ not applicable — not a memory product not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks Too recent ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Agent-memory-specific benchmark — multi-turn agent tasks with memory. Very recent; limited replication and adoption. arxiv 2507.05257 GitHub HUST-AI-HYZ
MemoryArena Multi-session agentic memory eval gym tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description read-only tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (evaluation benchmark) ↗ Stanford Digital Economy Lab et al. Benchmarks memory in realistic Memory-Agent-Environment loops where tasks span multiple sessions and subtasks are explicitly interdependent — agents must distill prior-session experiences into memory and use that memory to act later. Exposes the gap between conversational memory benchmarks (LoCoMo) and true agentic memory. Average 57 action steps + >40k tokens per task. Agents with near-saturated LoCoMo perf fail badly here. Feb 2026. text ↗ 2026-02 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched 9 cites 9/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Stanford Digital Economy Lab et al.; arxiv 2602.16313 ↗ not applicable — not a memory product not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ Arena-style head-to-head memory model evaluation. Preprint; arena methodology has known biases at small sample sizes. arxiv 2602.16313
MemoryBench (Tsinghua THUIR) Declarative + procedural continual-learning bench tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description read-only tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable (evaluation benchmark) ↗ Tsinghua THUIR. First benchmark covering both declarative and procedural memory for LLM systems. User-feedback simulation framework tests adaptive improvement. Distinct from Renmin's MemBench. Existing memory systems fail badly on procedural knowledge. text ↗ 2025-10 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold ↗ 21 cites 21/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Tsinghua THUIR; arxiv 2510.17281 ↗ not applicable — not a memory product not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks Too recent ↗ Code public + weights closed ↗ Tsinghua's structured memory benchmark — multiple memory operation types in one harness. Smaller adoption than LongMemEval / LoCoMo; tasks oriented toward Chinese language. arxiv 2510.17281 HF dataset
Memory observability & monitoring
Tools for tracing, drift-detection, and debugging memory in production — Langfuse, Arize Phoenix, custom poisoning detectors, memory-quality dashboards. Adjacent to LLMOps but specifically scoped to memory health: who wrote what, when, did it diverge from the corpus, did anyone slip in poisoned facts.
Zep / Graphiti Bi-temporal KG with provenance graph graph-traversal long-term append-only episodefact auditable bi-temporal versioning ↗ Memory and observability in one. Every fact carries valid_at / invalid_at timestamps; bi-temporal queries answer "what did the agent believe at time T?" Diffs incoming facts against the graph for contradiction detection at ingestion. Most purpose-built memory observability of the group. No poisoning detection. Free tier (1k credits/month); Flex $25 / Flex Plus $75. text ↗ 2025-01 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched not applicable — commercial product with a paper (arxiv 2501.13956 has ~50 cites) ↗ $500K pre-seed (YC W24 March 2024); $3M seed (2024); total ~$3.5M raised ↗ searched not found ↗ free tier: 2500 messages/mo + 2.5MB graph data; paid: $1.25/1000 messages + $2.50/MB extra; Enterprise custom with … ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial cloud (Zep managed) or self-hosted (Graphiti OSS + Neo4j); Enterprise: BYOK BYOM BYOC VPC ↗ San Francisco CA (YC W24 company) ↗ Zep AI (YC W24; Daniel Chalef) ↗ no scale claim — local or managed cloud ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — commercial product (Graphiti paper has partial reproducibility) ↗ Graphiti is Apache-2.0 OSS (github.com/getzep/graphiti); Zep cloud is closed ↗ Built-in temporal graph means provenance and history-of-facts queries are first-class — better forensic story than tools that bolt logging on top. Observability is one of multiple Zep concerns; not a dedicated monitoring product. getzep.com paper 2501.13956
Galileo (galileo.ai) Real-time intent / belief drift detection vector similarity cross-session append-only episode auditable not applicable (observability/monitoring platform; no memory write path) ↗ Treats memory as first-class in multi-agent tracing. Luna-2 SLMs (3B / 8B) scan every interaction for intent drift and belief drift; 20+ checks at sub-200ms latency. Catches when agent A's view of the world splits from teammate B's. OpenTelemetry-compatible. Free 5k traces/month; Pro from $100; Enterprise custom. Doesn't scan stored memory files directly. text (LLM trace + agent step monitoring) ↗ 2021 (founded 2021 by Atindriyo Sanyal Vikram Chatterji Yash Sheth; seed May 2022) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched not applicable — not academic $68M total ($10M seed + $13M Series A + $45M Series B Oct 2024); Scale Venture Partners + Premji Invest led Series B ↗ searched not found ↗ free: 5K traces/mo; Pro: $100+/mo (50K traces); Enterprise: custom (VPC on-prem); runtime guardrails on Enterprise only ↗ SOC 2 Type II (enterprise); no public HIPAA statement found ↗ cloud-hosted (SaaS); Enterprise option: VPC or on-premises deployment ↗ SaaS cloud; Enterprise: VPC or on-prem ↗ San Francisco CA ↗ ex-Google AI founders; Vikram Chatterji (Carnegie Mellon; ex-Google AI PM 2018-2021) Atindriyo … ↗ searched not found ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper First-mover in LLM observability; covers retrieval drift, hallucination, factuality alongside generic latency / cost — purpose-built for memory-driven agents. Closed SaaS; pricing scales with traces; less open than Langfuse. galileo.ai drift detection blog
Langfuse Memory ops as named spans + Agent Graphs relational exact-match long-term append-only episode auditable not applicable (tracing/observability platform; no memory write path) ↗ Memory module reads/writes captured as named spans. Trace Log View concatenates every agent step including memory ops. Agent Graphs (GA 2025) infer graph structure from observation nesting; session-level replay tracks how memory state evolves. No automated drift / poisoning detection — structural observability only. Free 50k observations; Pro $59/mo; Team $199/mo. text (LLM trace + memory span instrumentation) ↗ 2023-03 (Langfuse founded March 2023; LLM observability beta summer 2023; GA Feb 2024) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched not applicable — not academic acquired · 2026-01↗ 1-10 ppl 20k+ GitHub stars, 26M SDK installs/month, acquired by ClickHouse↗ free: 50K observations/mo; Pro: $59/mo; Team: $199/mo; Enterprise: custom; acquired by ClickHouse so pricing evolut… ↗ SOC 2 (pre-acquisition); ClickHouse enterprise compliance expected post-acquisition ↗ cloud-hosted SaaS or self-hosted (OSS; MIT licensed); ClickHouse backend post-acquisition ↗ SaaS cloud (Berlin + SF) or self-hosted Docker/K8s; ClickHouse native integration post-acquisition ↗ Germany (Berlin) ↗ Marc Klingen (CEO) + Max Deichmann + Clemens Rawert; Berlin-based; acquired by ClickHouse Jan 2026 ↗ 26 million SDK installs per month (pre-acquisition claim) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper OSS-first LLM observability with a polished cloud option — self-hostable for compliance-sensitive shops; large open-source community. Memory-specific observability is improving but less domain-specialized than Galileo's purpose-built memory views. langfuse.com agent observability blog
Mem0 (with AgentOps) Memory Operation Timeline + Search Analytics vectorgraph similaritygraph-traversal long-term extraction factepisode auditable LLM-arbitrate ↗ When integrated with AgentOps, surfaces Memory Operation Timeline, Search Analytics (query patterns, hit rates), Memory Growth curves, Cost Attribution per memory op. Native TTL/expiration + relevance decay handle staleness. No native poisoning / contradiction detection. Self-reports staleness via TTL; relevance decay for low-signal entries. text ↗ 2024-01 (Mem0 launched January 2024 as open-source; observability features added with AgentOps integration) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched not applicable — not academic $3.9M seed (Kindred Ventures) + $20M Series A (Basis Set Ventures + YC + Peak XV); total $24M raised ↗ 80k+ developers signed up; 186M API calls/mo (Q3 2025); AWS Agent SDK exclusive memory provider ↗ free Hobby (10K memories 1K retrieval/mo); Starter $19/mo; Pro $249/mo; Enterprise custom ↗ SOC 2 Type II + HIPAA BAA (Enterprise tier); BYOK encryption ↗ cloud-managed (US East); Enterprise: on-prem or VPC isolation; OpenMemory for local self-host ↗ SaaS cloud API; Enterprise: dedicated VPC; OpenMemory: local Docker Compose ↗ San Francisco CA (YC S24) ↗ Mem0 (YC S24; Taranjeet Singh & Deshraj Yadav) ↗ cloud-managed; no published per-user cap ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Native integration with the Mem0 layer surfaces memory-specific metrics (extraction quality, retrieval relevance) that generic observability misses. Tightly coupled to Mem0; less useful if your memory is on a different layer. mem0.ai AgentOps integration
Ratine Offline scanner — memory poisoning detector file extraction-pull cross-session read-only fact auditable not applicable (read-only offline scanner) ↗ Only tool found that scans the persistent memory layer on disk rather than runtime tracing. Detects injected instructions, obfuscated payloads (zero-width Unicode, base64, homoglyphs, hex), C2-pattern URLs, credential leakage. ratine diff compares snapshots for belief drift. Free / open source. Auto-detects Mem0 local, AutoGen, custom layouts. Zero deps, zero network calls. text ↗ 2026-04 v0.1.0 2026-04-02 ↗ MIT ↗ 1★↗ Python below detection threshold or not yet researched not applicable — not academic no public funding ↗ unknown ppl No public funding data found↗ free / open source (MIT); no paid tier ↗ searched not found ↗ searched not found ↗ local CLI — Python; pip install; no server required ↗ unknown ↗ goweft (open-source; no institutional affiliation) ↗ not applicable (scanner; no memory storage) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Memory-specific observability with explicit memory-quality dashboards. Newer entrant; small adoption signal; thin documentation. GitHub goweft/ratine
AgentOps Operational metrics for agent memory relational exact-match long-term append-only episode auditable not applicable (observability layer; no memory storage) ↗ When Mem0 is connected, gains Memory Operation Timeline, Search Analytics, Memory Growth tracking, Error Tracking per memory call. Standalone, records context at each step but doesn't analyse memory quality. 400+ LLM integrations. Free tier; Pro/Enterprise pricing not public. text ↗ 2023 (founded 2023 by Alex Reibman Adam Silverman Braelyn Boynton; pre-seed Aug 2024) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched not applicable — not academic $2.6M pre-seed (645 Ventures + Afore Capital; Aug 2024) ↗ searched not found ↗ free tier; Pro/Enterprise pricing not publicly listed; contact sales ↗ searched not found ↗ cloud-hosted SaaS; traces sent to AgentOps servers ↗ SaaS cloud; SDK integration into agent code ↗ San Francisco CA ↗ AgentOps (Alex Reibman, co-founder; YC W24) ↗ internal — undisclosed (SaaS observability) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Agent-loop observability with strong memory-step instrumentation; integrates with Mem0. Agent-orchestration tilted; less broad than Galileo / Langfuse for general LLM observability. agentops.ai
LangSmith Memory mutations as distinct span types relational exact-match long-term append-only episode auditable not applicable (observability/tracing; no memory storage) ↗ Memory reads, vector DB retrievals, state changes are distinct span types in traces. RAG eval separates retrieval quality (context precision) from generation quality (faithfulness). Dataset versioning guards against eval drift. Free 5k traces/month Developer; Plus $39/mo; Enterprise custom. No automated drift / poisoning detection. text ↗ 2023 (LangSmith beta summer 2023; GA Feb 2024 with $25M Series A) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched not applicable — not academic LangChain total ~$35M+ ($3M seed Benchmark 2023; $25M Series A Sequoia Feb 2024; $125M Oct 2025 unicorn round at $1… ↗ searched not found ↗ Developer: free (5K traces/mo); Plus: $39/seat/mo (10K traces); Enterprise: custom (SSO/SAML; dedicated support) ↗ SOC 2 Type II (Enterprise tier); SAML SSO ↗ cloud SaaS; no self-host option publicly announced; Enterprise: dedicated tenant ↗ SaaS cloud (US); Enterprise: dedicated tenant ↗ San Francisco CA ↗ LangChain Inc. (Harrison Chase, co-founder; ex-Robust Intelligence; Sequoia-backed) ↗ internal — undisclosed (SaaS observability) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Best integration with the LangChain / LangGraph stack — debug memory + chain + agent in one trace; the de-facto trace tool for that ecosystem. LangChain-shaped — works less well outside that ecosystem; closed SaaS. langchain.com/langsmith
Memory governance, privacy & safety
Products that secure memory writes and reads — poisoning protection, differential-privacy wrappers, memory access control, audit logging for memory mutations. The compliance and safety tier for systems that retain user data. Emerging category — most production memory systems treat this as someone else's problem.
Lakera Guard / Lakera Red Real-time prompt-injection + poisoning screen tagging agent could not infer from description injection session read-only tagging agent could not infer from description auditable not applicable (security screening; no memory storage) ↗ Screens every prompt, response, and retrieved document for indirect prompt injection — primary vector for memory poisoning. Treats memory as untrusted: anything written to or read from memory is adversarial until proven clean. Lakera Red provides "Agent Breaker" gamified red-teaming. Sub-100ms p99 REST API. Threat model: poisoning, long-horizon goal hijack, cross-session manipulation. text ↗ 2021 (founded 2021; Lakera Guard product launched Aug 2023; $10M seed 2023; $20M Series A Jul 2024) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic $30M total ($10M seed Redalpine/Atomico 2023; $20M Series A Jul 2024 Redpoint Ventures + Dropbox VC + Citi Ventures… ↗ searched not found ↗ Developer: free (10K API calls/mo); Pro: contact sales (higher volume + advanced detection); Enterprise: custom (on… ↗ SOC 2 (enterprise); Swiss data sovereignty options (Zurich HQ) ↗ SaaS cloud API (default) or self-hosted (Enterprise); sub-100ms screening; prompts/responses sent to Lakera servers… ↗ REST API (SaaS cloud) or self-hosted Enterprise deployment ↗ Zurich Switzerland + San Francisco CA ↗ Lakera AI (David Haber, CEO; Zürich-based; Redpoint Ventures-backed) ↗ internal — undisclosed (SaaS; real-time screening) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Memory-aware guardrail product — Guard for runtime + Red for adversarial testing; covers prompt-injection-into-memory specifically. Closed SaaS; rule library is opaque; per-call cost can be meaningful at high volume. lakera.ai/lakera-guard memory poisoning blog
OWASP Agent Memory Guard Declarative YAML policies + SHA-256 baselines file exact-match long-term read-only file auditable not applicable (declarative YAML policy framework) ↗ Open-source runtime defense. Enforces YAML policies on every memory read/write. SHA-256 baselines detect tampering, injection, sensitive-data leakage, protected-key modification, rapid-change anomalies. Forensic snapshots + rollback. Reference impl for OWASP ASI06 (Memory Poisoning). v0.3.0 roadmap (Q2 2026): LlamaIndex + CrewAI integrations, Redis + Postgres backends, Prometheus metrics. text ↗ 2026-Q1 (OWASP project page set up Q1 2026; v0.2.1 with OWASP branding; v0.3.0 roadmap Q2 2026) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic not applicable — OWASP Foundation open-source project; community-maintained ↗ not applicable — open-source policy framework; not a commercial product ↗ free / open source ↗ provides compliance framework for OWASP ASI06 (Memory Poisoning); reference for EU AI Act and NIST AI RMF ↗ searched not found ↗ local deployment; Python; run against local memory stores ↗ not applicable — OWASP Foundation; global community ↗ OWASP Foundation (open-source; community-maintained) ↗ not applicable (policy framework; no memory storage) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Standardized threat taxonomy for memory in agents — closest thing to industry consensus on memory threats. Framework / taxonomy, not a tool; adoption depends on practitioner uptake. OWASP project
Microsoft Agent Governance Toolkit Cross-Model Verification Kernel + Agent OS tagging agent could not infer from description agentic session read-only tagging agent could not infer from description auditable LLM-arbitrate (CMVK majority-vote before memory-influenced action) ↗ Cross-Model Verification Kernel (CMVK) requires majority-voting agreement across multiple model calls before a memory-influenced action. Agent OS package intercepts every action (memory included) at sub-millisecond latency. MIT licensed. p99 < 0.1ms. Integrations for LangChain, CrewAI, Google ADK, Microsoft Agent Framework. Python / TypeScript / Rust / Go / .NET. April 2026. text ↗ 2026-03 v3.4.0 2026-05-05 ↗ MIT ↗ 1.4k★ +50/mo↗ Python 1.4k GitHub stars (+50/mo); growing signal for enterprise governance tooling ↗ not applicable — not academic not applicable — Microsoft Research; no external funding ↗ searched not found ↗ free / open source (MIT) ↗ MIT-licensed tooling; Microsoft Azure enterprise compliance stack available separately ↗ local enforcement (sub-millisecond p99 action interception); no mandatory cloud dependency ↗ library/SDK integration; multi-language (Python TypeScript Rust Go .NET); LangChain/CrewAI/Google ADK integrations ↗ not applicable — Microsoft Corporation (Redmond WA) ↗ Microsoft Research ↗ internal — undisclosed (enterprise toolkit) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Microsoft's governance toolkit for AI agents — covers memory access policies + audit trails as part of broader agent governance. Microsoft-stack-tilted; less applicable outside M365 / Azure deployments. GitHub microsoft/agent-governance-toolkit
Mem0 Security / OpenMemory SOC 2 + HIPAA + zero-trust + BYOK vectorgraph similaritygraph-traversal long-term extraction fact auditable LLM-arbitrate ↗ Commercial Mem0 ships SOC 2 / HIPAA, zero-trust access controls, BYOK encryption, real-time monitoring, audit logs, workspace governance as defaults. OpenMemory is the local self-hosted variant (Docker + FastAPI + Postgres + Qdrant) for privacy-first deployments. Threat model: exfiltration, HIPAA/GDPR right-to-erasure, unauthorised access, cross-session leakage. MCP standard for local serving to Claude/ChatGPT/Perplexity. text ↗ 2024-01 (Mem0 launched Jan 2024; security features and HIPAA BAA added with enterprise tier) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched not applicable — not academic $24M total ($3.9M seed Kindred + $20M Series A Basis Set + YC + Peak XV Oct 2025) ↗ 80k+ developers; AWS Agent SDK exclusive memory provider; 186M API calls/mo Q3 2025 ↗ Hobby free; Starter $19/mo; Pro $249/mo; Enterprise custom with SOC2/HIPAA ↗ SOC 2 Type II + HIPAA BAA (Enterprise); BYOK encryption; zero-trust access controls ↗ cloud-managed US East (default); Enterprise VPC isolation; OpenMemory: local Docker Compose Postgres+Qdrant ↗ SaaS cloud; Enterprise VPC; OpenMemory self-hosted (Docker Compose) ↗ San Francisco CA (YC S24) ↗ Mem0 (YC S24; Taranjeet Singh & Deshraj Yadav) ↗ cloud-managed; no published per-user cap ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Native privacy / governance hooks inside Mem0 — encryption, masking, retention policies as first-class config rather than bolt-ons. Mem0-only; OpenMemory protocol is still emerging and lacks broad ecosystem support. security page security best practices
Enkrypt AI Three-point RAG / memory pipeline guardrails tagging agent could not infer from description injection session read-only tagging agent could not infer from description auditable not applicable (guardrail/security layer; no memory storage) ↗ Applies guardrails at three points: (1) before write to vector DB, (2) before query reaches embedding model, (3) before response. Detects malicious instructions in stored memory before retrieval; scans for PII/PHI in/out. Text + image + voice modalities. Sub-100ms latency. Documented MongoDB Atlas Vector Search integration. text ↗ 2022 (founded 2022 by Sahil Agarwal and Prashanth Harshangi; Yale PhDs) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched not applicable — not academic $2M Seed · 2024-02↗ 1-10 ppl Enterprise AI governance users↗ free starter + pay-as-you-go; Enterprise: contact sales ↗ searched not found ↗ SaaS cloud API; real-time screening of prompts and responses; no self-host confirmed ↗ REST API SaaS (cloud-hosted); no self-host option confirmed ↗ US (Brighton MA) ↗ Enkrypt AI (Sahil Agarwal, CEO; ex-IBM Research; YC-backed) ↗ internal — undisclosed (SaaS guardrails) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Comprehensive AI security product covering training data, model, and runtime — broader than memory but addresses memory-specific threats too. Memory is one of many concerns; less depth on memory-injection attacks than Lakera. enkryptai.com
Acuvity (now Proofpoint) MCP / Shadow-AI runtime enforcement tagging agent could not infer from description agentic session read-only tagging agent could not infer from description auditable not applicable (runtime enforcement; no memory storage) ↗ Runtime enforcement targeting memory poisoning, unauthorised execution, identity spoofing per the OWASP LLM threat list. Visibility/control over MCP servers and locally-installed AI tools — the infrastructure layer where memory is most exposed. Acquired by Proofpoint Feb 2026 for integration into enterprise DLP / governance. text ↗ 2023 (founded by cybersecurity veterans; acquired by Proofpoint Feb 12 2026) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic no public funding data found — pre-acquisition private company; acquired by Proofpoint for undisclosed amount ↗ searched not found ↗ enterprise pricing via Proofpoint (not public); contact sales ↗ Proofpoint enterprise compliance (SOC 2 FedRAMP ISO 27001); Acuvity's own certs not confirmed ↗ enterprise deployment; runtime inspection of AI/MCP traffic; data handling under Proofpoint's enterprise DLP framework ↗ on-prem or cloud (Proofpoint enterprise deployment); agentless approach ↗ Sunnyvale CA (acquired by Proofpoint Sunnyvale) ↗ Acuvity (acquired by Proofpoint 2025; enterprise AI security) ↗ internal — undisclosed (enterprise security) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Enterprise DLP for AI products — protects against data exfiltration via memory writes/reads; backed by Proofpoint's enterprise channel. Enterprise-tier pricing and complexity; smaller mind-share than Lakera or Microsoft Defender for AI. acuvity.ai Proofpoint acquisition
HiddenLayer AISec Platform 2.0 Model Genealogy + AIBOM (supply-chain layer) tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description read-only tagging agent could not infer from description auditable not applicable (supply-chain/lineage; no memory storage) ↗ Targets the supply-chain / lineage layer rather than runtime memory writes. Model Genealogy tracks training/fine-tuning/modification history — catches poisoning baked in during training. AIBOM generates auditable inventory of model components + datasets. Runtime layer also monitors agentic workflows. Model-agnostic and agentless — no access to weights or training data required. text ↗ 2022-03 (founded March 2022; emerged from stealth July 2022 with $6M seed; Series A $50M Sep 2023) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic $56M total ($6M seed Ten Eleven Ventures Jul 2022; $50M Series A M12+Moore Strategic Sep 2023) ↗ searched not found ↗ enterprise pricing; contact sales; no public pricing ↗ FedRAMP path (DoD SBIR contract); SOC 2 enterprise; US Gov compliance focus ↗ SaaS cloud or on-prem (agentless — no access to weights required); model-agnostic ↗ SaaS cloud or on-prem enterprise; agentless; no weights or training data access needed ↗ Austin TX ↗ HiddenLayer (Chris Sestito & Tanner Burns, co-founders; Austin TX; IVP-backed) ↗ internal — undisclosed (enterprise SaaS) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Strong on adversarial ML threats (model evasion, training-data poisoning) extending into memory poisoning at runtime. Broad ML-security positioning means memory specifically isn't the focus; pricing is enterprise-tier. hiddenlayer.com 2.0 announcement
Zep — governance posture Bi-temporal KG = structural provenance graph graph-traversal long-term append-only factepisode auditable bi-temporal versioning ↗ Bi-temporal knowledge graph preserves full lineage of every stored fact — every memory write traceable and auditable by graph schema, not bolted-on logging. SOC 2 Type II; HIPAA BAA available; BYOK + VPC-isolated deployment for data residency. Provenance is structural. Threat model: exfiltration, HIPAA/SOC 2 compliance, audit trail gaps, explainability failures. text ↗ 2023 (Zep AI founded 2023; YC W24; bi-temporal governance posture described in product from 2024) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ not applicable — not academic $500K pre-seed (YC W24 March 2024); total ~$3.5M ↗ searched not found ↗ free: 2500 messages/mo; paid: $1.25/1000 messages; Enterprise: custom HIPAA BAA VPC ↗ SOC 2 Type II + HIPAA BAA (Enterprise); BYOK + VPC-isolated deployment ↗ cloud-managed (default) or self-hosted Graphiti OSS + Neo4j; Enterprise: BYOK BYOM BYOC ↗ SaaS cloud; self-hosted (Graphiti OSS); Enterprise: BYOC VPC ↗ San Francisco CA (Oakland area per some sources) ↗ Zep AI (YC W24; Daniel Chalef) ↗ no scale claim — bi-temporal KG (same backend as Zep/Graphiti) ↗ no public benchmark scores found not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Bi-temporal graph IS the governance story — every memory write traceable by graph schema, not bolted-on logging; SOC 2 Type II + HIPAA + BYOK. Governance is a property of Zep's architecture, not a separate product — you don't get the governance without adopting Zep itself. Zep agent memory
Theoretical / informal — ideas without a paper
Ideas that exist only as tweets, blog posts, talks, or RFCs — no paper, no implementation. The earliest stage of the funnel: pure speculation, but where the next generation of papers is seeded. Populate with named concepts as you encounter them.
From Human Memory to AI Memory (survey) 3D taxonomy: object × form × time tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable — theoretical / not a system Eight-quadrant classification grid across personal/system, parametric/non-parametric, and short-term/long-term axes. Bridges cognitive-science memory taxonomy to LLM architecture choices, less common than purely engineering-oriented surveys. v2 revision April 23, 2025. 26-page survey covering encoding, storage, retrieval; maps open problems and future directions. text (survey paper; no implementation) ↗ 2025-04 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo 73 citations (Semantic Scholar 2025-04 to present); above-average for a 1-year-old survey ↗ 73 cites 73/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company no data — 2025 arxiv survey; multiple Chinese institutions ↗ not applicable — not a memory product not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ 3D taxonomy provides orthogonal categorization axes that cut across existing framework-centric surveys; cognitive-science grounding enables comparison with neuroscience literature. Coverage of very recent (2026) methods is limited in v2; survey-only — no new method or empirical evaluation. arxiv 2504.15965
Externalization in LLM Agents Memory + skills + protocols as coupled externalization tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable — theoretical / not a system Traces the shift from weights-as-capability to harness-as-capability; analyzes memory, skills, and protocols as three coupled forms of externalization and examines how they interact. Memory is defined as the externalization of state across time. Covers self-evolving harnesses and shared agent infrastructure as emerging directions. April 9, 2026. No empirical evaluation; positions as a unifying conceptual review across the memory/skills/protocol triad that most papers treat separately. text ↗ 2026-04 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo 1 citation (too recent — April 2026) ↗ 1 cites 1/yr↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2604.08224; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ not applicable — not a memory product not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ The unified framework makes the coupling between memory, skills, and protocols explicit — absent from most memory-only surveys; the harness-engineering layer is underexamined. Conceptual/review paper only; no new method, no benchmarks; assessment of "open challenges" is speculative given the field's pace. arxiv 2604.08224 html version
Memory as Metabolism Metabolic governance cycle for companion knowledge tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable — theoretical / not a system Argues that companion AI knowledge systems must treat memory as a continuous metabolic process rather than a static store. Proposes a three-tier store (raw buffer, active wiki, cold memory) governed by five operations (TRIAGE, DECAY, CONTEXTUALIZE, CONSOLIDATE, AUDIT), memory gravity (recency/frequency weighting), and explicit minority-hypothesis retention to counter worldview entrenchment. April 13, 2026. Normative framework with testable conformance invariants; positions itself within the 2026 governance-proposals cluster (Context Cartography, MemOS). text ↗ 2026-04 ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo searched not found ↗ 0 (too recent — April 13 2026; Semantic Scholar not yet indexed) ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company no data — searched arxiv.org/abs/2604.12034; author affiliations not found in preview ↗ not applicable — not a memory product not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks Too recent ↗ Paper only ↗ Addresses companion-specific ossification and paradigm-reinforcement — failure modes not modeled by performance-oriented memory benchmarks; includes conformance-testable invariants. Entirely theoretical/design; no implementation, benchmark, or ablation; conformance invariants are not formally specified in the preprint. arxiv 2604.12034 memorypapers.org
LLM as OS Kernel Architectural metaphor vector similarity long-term tagging agent could not infer from description file tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable — theoretical / not a system LLM is "the kernel process of a new Operating System" orchestrating I/O, code interpreter, browser, and an embedding store for files / internal memory. Context window = RAM; embedding store = filesystem. Andrej Karpathy, Sept 2023 tweet. Defined the vocabulary practitioners use for memory-vs-context decisions. not applicable — tweet/blog concept only ↗ 2023-09 (Andrej Karpathy tweet September 28 2023) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo no formal citation count (tweet not indexed); estimated 100k+ impressions based on Karpathy's 1M+ followers; widely… ↗ ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Andrej Karpathy (ex-OpenAI; re-joined OpenAI in 2023 from Tesla AI Director) ↗ not applicable — not a memory product not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Defined the vocabulary practitioners use for memory-vs-context decisions; widely adopted mental model. Tweet-only; metaphor rather than architecture; no concrete implementation guidance. tweet
LLM OS Specs Memory in hardware terms vector similarity long-term tagging agent could not infer from description file tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable — theoretical / not a system Operationalises the kernel analogy: 256-core processor at 20 Hz (tokens/sec), RAM = 128K tokens of context, filesystem = Ada-002 embedding store. Memory capacity becomes a measurable architectural constraint.
