Context Engineering Discipline rename tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable — theoretical / not a system "+1 for 'context engineering' over 'prompt engineering' … the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." The LLM is "a coworker with anterograde amnesia" — cannot consolidate or build long-running knowledge once training ends. Karpathy, June 2025 tweet + YC AI Startup School keynote. not applicable — conceptual naming event ↗ 2025-06 (Karpathy tweet June 2025 + YC AI Startup School keynote) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo no formal citation count; widely adopted term — "context engineering" now standard vocabulary in 2025-2026 agent di… ↗ ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Andrej Karpathy (independent / ex-OpenAI at time of post 2025) ↗ not applicable — not a memory product not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Moved the community vocabulary from 'prompt engineering' to 'context engineering'; widely adopted. LLM Wiki — compiled memory Compilation over RAG file injection long-term overwrite file editable not applicable — theoretical / not a system Three-layer directory: raw/ (immutable sources), wiki/ (LLM-maintained compiled pages), CLAUDE.md (schema). LLM reads sources and integrates them into the wiki — updating pages, resolving contradictions — so knowledge compounds across sessions instead of being retrieved cold. Karpathy, April 2026 GitHub Gist. Challenges RAG orthodoxy; separates ingestion (compilation) from retrieval. text no data no data — repo … no data — no data — repo unreachable ↗ 0★↗ no data — repo unreachable below detection threshold or not yet researched ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — theoretical / informal not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — theoretical / informal not applicable — not a company Andrej Karpathy (independent) ↗ not applicable — not a memory product not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Three-layer compilation paradigm (raw / wiki / schema) directly challenges RAG orthodoxy — knowledge compounds across sessions instead of being retrieved cold. GitHub Gist; conceptual sketch with minimal implementation guidance. gist
Four-Type Agent Memory Taxonomy Cognitive-science → LLM mapping tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable — theoretical / not a system "Agent = LLM + memory + planning + tools." Maps cognitive memory types to LLM machinery: sensory (learned embeddings), short-term / in-context (the context window), long-term (external vector store with fast retrieval). The first widely-cited taxonomy mapping human memory science onto LLM agent architecture. Lilian Weng (VP Research, OpenAI), June 2023 blog. Adopted (often uncited) by LangChain, AutoGPT, and most subsequent agent frameworks. text 2023-09 (CoALA framework arxiv 2309.02427 submitted September 2023 by Wang et al. Princeton; formalises the four-ty… ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Lilian Weng (VP Research at OpenAI at time of post; June 2023) ↗ not applicable — not a memory product not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper First widely-cited taxonomy mapping cognitive memory types to LLM machinery; adopted (often uncited) across the agent-framework field. Blog post; taxonomy is descriptive rather than prescriptive. blog post tweet
Context Expansion Law Parkinson's-Law analogue for context tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable — theoretical / not a system "Application context tends to expand to fill the context limits supported by the model." Treats agent memory as a first-class unresolved design problem rather than a solved component; explicitly defers a memory deep-dive to a future post. Chip Huyen (author, AI Engineering O'Reilly 2025), Jan 2025 blog. text searched not found ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Chip Huyen (independent author/researcher; ex-NVIDIA NeMo; ex-Snorkel AI; Stanford lecturer; pu… ↗ not applicable — not a memory product not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Treats agent memory as a first-class unresolved design problem rather than a solved component. Blog post; states the problem clearly but defers the answer to a future post. agents post
Files Are All You Need Filesystem as memory interface file extraction-pull cross-session append-only file inspectable not applicable — theoretical / not a system Coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor) converge on the filesystem as their primary memory abstraction: conversation histories as searchable files, skills as files, retrieval via file search rather than vector DBs. Argument: LLMs are fluent with filesystem concepts, so the filesystem is the right interface even if storage underneath is a database. Jerry Liu (CEO, LlamaIndex), Jan 2026. Inverts RAG orthodoxy. text 2026-01 (Jerry Liu post in January 2026 declaring 'Files Are All You Need'; sparked broader debate published Jan 2026) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Jerry Liu (CEO and co-founder LlamaIndex; YC-backed) ↗ not applicable — not a memory product not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Names the consensus pattern — coding agents converge on filesystem as memory abstraction; inverts RAG orthodoxy. Blog post; argument by observation rather than benchmark. blog post tweet
RL-Based Continual Learning as Memory Memory-in-weights via on-policy RL parametric parametric-recall parametric-permanent parametric-edit weight tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable — theoretical / not a system On-policy RL is naturally more resistant to catastrophic forgetting than supervised fine-tuning — its online nature biases learning toward low-distribution-shift updates. Proposal: RL post-training as a viable mechanism for agents to accumulate long-term memory in weights, not just in an external store. Cameron R. Wolfe (Deep Learning Focus), Jan 2026 Substack. text ↗ 2025-2026 (concept from multiple informal posts; key anchor paper Just-In-Time RL arxiv 2601.18510 submitted Jan 20… ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Cameron Wolfe (independent researcher / Gradient Flow newsletter; ex-Rice University) ↗ not applicable — not a memory product not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Proposes on-policy RL post-training as a viable mechanism for in-weights memory accumulation; principled grounding in known continual-learning theory. Substack post; speculative; no implementation or benchmark. Substack post
Memory Poisoning as Attack Surface Security framing tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable — theoretical / not a system Agent persistent memory is an attack surface: an adversarial document in the environment can instruct the agent's memory tool to write malicious facts, poisoning the long-term store. The more capable the memory system, the larger the injection surface. Conversation history is memory's first-class form; long-term memory is "an extra set of tools." Simon Willison (creator of Datasette, coined "prompt injection"), Sept 2025 + ongoing series. text 2024 (AgentPoison NeurIPS 2024; Lakera blog Nov 2025; concept crystallised 2024 with AgentPoison paper) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Simon Willison (independent developer and co-creator of Django; founder of Datasette) ↗ not applicable — not a memory product not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Frames agent persistent memory as a first-class attack surface; widely cited by security practitioners; ongoing series. Blog framing; provides analysis but does not specify concrete countermeasures. agents post prompt-injection series
Context Engineering — naming event Discipline crystallisation tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable — theoretical / not a system Endorses "context engineering" as a distinct discipline from prompt engineering — what agents do with their context window (routing, compression, tool output formatting, memory retrieval injection) is engineering, not just prompting. Simon Willison, June 27, 2025 blog. The community's naming event for the term. text 2025-06-25 (Andrej Karpathy coined/popularised 'context engineering' on X on June 25 2025) ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Simon Willison (independent developer; co-creator of Django; Datasette founder) ↗ not applicable — not a memory product not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Crystallized the community's acceptance of 'context engineering' as a distinct discipline from prompt engineering. Blog post; naming event rather than architectural contribution. blog post
JEPA Six-Module World Model (informal) Memory as architecturally peer module tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description tagging agent could not infer from description not applicable — theoretical / not a system Six-module cognitive architecture (perception, world model, cost, memory, action, configurator). Memory is a distinct addressable module with its own update rules, separate from the world model that reads/updates it — not in-context storage, not RAG. Directly challenges the "memory = RAG" consensus. Yann LeCun (Chief AI Scientist, Meta), June 2022 position paper. Widely circulated as a blog-adjacent informal proposal rather than a peer-reviewed paper. text ↗ 2022-02 (Yann LeCun 'A Path Towards Autonomous Intelligence' published February 2022; introduced the six-module JEP… ↗ not applicable — not OSS not applicable — not OSS not applicable — no GitHub repo below detection threshold or not yet researched ↗ not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not commercial not applicable — not a deployable product not applicable — not a company Yann LeCun — Meta Chief AI Scientist; NYU; openreview.net/pdf?id=BZ5a1r-kVsf ↗ not applicable — not a memory product not applicable — does not compete on benchmarks not applicable — not a research paper not applicable — not a research paper Memory as a peer architectural module separate from the world model — directly challenges the memory-as-RAG consensus. Position paper / blog-adjacent; not peer-reviewed; LeCun's broader JEPA programme has critics. OpenReview PDF

Column key

Identity

System
Display name + URL. Tier badge (T1–T5) prefixed in the Memory model cell.
Memory model
One-phrase free-form description of the architecture (kept for narrative).

Taxonomy (controlled vocabulary — see taxonomy/schema.md)

Storage
Where memory physically lives: vector · graph · kv · kv-cache · file · parametric · relational · column · cache · proprietary · hybrid.
Retrieval
How memory is recalled: similarity · graph-traversal · exact-match · attention · cache-lookup · parametric-recall · hybrid-rerank · extraction-pull · injection · agentic.
Persistence
How long memory is designed to live: ephemeral · session · cross-session · long-term · lifelong · parametric-permanent.
Update
How memory contents change: append-only · extraction · overwrite · parametric-edit · consolidation · evict-oldest · read-only · agent-controlled.
Unit
What is stored as a single "thing": fact · document · episode · skill · profile · scene-graph · kv-token · file · weight · trajectory · chunk · summary.
Governance
Inspectability / control: opaque · inspectable · editable · auditable · deterministic · user-controllable.
Conflict res.
How writes that contradict prior writes are resolved: append (no resolution) · overwrite · LLM-arbitrate · bi-temporal versioning · manual · none specified.

Substance

What it does
1–2 sentence neutral architectural description.
Claims
Vendor- or paper-stated claims (revenue, benchmarks, customers, scale). Always vendor-stated unless cited otherwise.
Modalities
Input modalities supported: text · voice · image · video · sensor. Multi = comma-separated.

Activity

Created
Repo creation date (OSS rows) or first-paper date.
Latest release
Most recent versioned release (orthogonal to last-commit — repos can have commits but no releases).
License
OSS license (MIT / Apache 2 / BSD-3 / AGPL / GPL / etc.) or Proprietary / Closed.

Adoption signals

GitHub
Stars + 30-day star velocity + primary language. Source: GitHub REST API.
Mindshare
Inbound integrations (count) + package downloads (npm/PyPI/crates) + LinkedIn job-postings + HN/Reddit/press mentions. Source citations vary by sub-signal.
Citations
Total citations + citations/year via Semantic Scholar.

Commercial signals

Funding
Total disclosed raised + latest valuation + latest round + date. Source: Crunchbase / TechCrunch / official press.
Customers / scale
ARR (where disclosed) + employee-count range + named customer logos (top 5). 58% of commercial rows have no public customer data.
Pricing
Pricing model: Free · OSS · Free + paid · SaaS · Enterprise only · Pay-per-use · Hardware-bundled.
Compliance
Certifications: SOC 2 Type I/II · HIPAA · FedRAMP · ISO 27001 · GDPR · PCI · others. Empty for non-applicable rows.
Data handling
Customer-data policy: Trains on data · Opt-out available · Never trains on customer data · BYOK supported. Sourced from vendor ToS / trust pages.

Operational

Deployment
Mode: Managed-only · Self-hostable · Both · Hybrid · On-prem. From vendor docs.
HQ
Headquarters region: US · EU · UK · China · Korea · Japan · India · Israel · etc. Country-level where it differs from region.
Founders / pedigree
Lab / company / accelerator origin. Examples: "UC Berkeley spin-off"; "ex-Anthropic"; "YC W24"; "NeurIPS 2023 best paper authors".

Architecture-specific

Memory volume / scale
Vendor-claimed scale envelope. Examples: "1B+ memories" · "fits in 4MB file" · "100M+ tokens of context" · "internal — undisclosed".
Performance
Public benchmark scores (LongMemEval-S / LoCoMo / ConvoMem / MemoryAgentBench). ⚠ icon flags disputed claims (see analysis §5).

Research-specific (T3 / T4 papers)

Reproducibility
Independent reproduction status: Independently reproduced · Original team only · Disputed · No reproductions found.
Code/weights release
Build-on-ability: Code public + weights public · Code public, weights closed · Code partial / pseudocode · Paper only.

Judgement

Pros
1–2 sentence summary of where this system wins relative to peers.
Cons
1–2 sentence summary of known weaknesses / where it loses.
Project & sources
URLs to vendor page, GitHub repo, paper, key press articles.

Cell content conventions: — means the cell has not yet been processed. no data ↗ (italic gray with citation) means an agent searched and could not find the data — the citation points to where they searched. Numeric cells use bold for the headline number and gray subline for context. Any cell with a ↗ superscript citation has a clickable source link.

Cross-cutting analyses — rated

Synthesis of nine parallel analyses (2026-05-06) against the 520-row catalog + 6-axis taxonomy + adoption / funding / activity passes. Each finding rated on four dimensions out of five: Conf (data robustness) · Surp (vs. conventional wisdom) · Act (does it dictate a move) · Def (would it survive a hostile reviewer). Source: .agent-results/analysis-synthesis.md.

Rating matrix

# Finding Conf Surp Act Def Overall
18 paradigms cover 83%; 288 singleton fingerprints5344★★★★
2Market rewards inspectable + append-only + kv/file + injection + cross-session3553★★★★
3RAG → KG → world-model is wrong; concurrent not sequential4534★★★★
4Power-law network; Mem0 / LangChain / LangGraph absorb 38% of edges4354★★★★
5Academy diverges from market (only retrieval=similarity shared)5444★★★★★
6Governance gap is real (11% genuine; only Zep/Graphiti AI-native)4355★★★★★
793% of capital outside dedicated memory; 68× valuation gap4434★★★★
8T3 repos rot (59% stalled); live edge is T2 Claude Code / dedicated / framework3233★★★
983% text-only; multimodal forced by use case, not chosen5544★★★★★
10Deployment bifurcated; browser-agents 100% SaaS-only4344★★★★

The three strongest (★★★★★)

#5 — Academy / market divergence

Cleanest data signal in the entire pass. T1 (production) and T4 (preprints) share one dominant axis value out of six (retrieval=similarity). Translation gaps (hybrid storage, parametric memory family, trajectory unit) and pragmatic gaps (relational, opaque governance, column storage) are both crisp. Survives any hostile reviewer because the percentage-point deltas are 30+.

#6 — Governance gap

Started as a hypothesis, came back confirmed at multiple resolutions: 11% genuine governance overall, 0/30 in agent frameworks, 1/36 in dedicated memory layers. Highest actionability because it names a specific defensible white-space. Only AI-native system combining provenance audit + structured conflict resolution: Zep / Graphiti.

#9 — Multimodal forced, not chosen

The "pre-2020 24% non-text vs. 2026-to-date 0%" inversion is the single most counterintuitive finding in the pass. Bracketed by hard time-series data, not a single-snapshot artifact. Multimodal is bimodally concentrated (voice-wearable + vertical), and dedicated memory layers — the architectural core — are 94% text-only.

The weakest (★★★)

#8 — Activity / abandonment

Only 77 of 520 rows had a usable maintenance signal. The "research rots, T2 lives" pattern is real but unsurprising, and the small scored sample makes the section-level percentages noisy. Useful as confirmation, not load-bearing.

Confidence caveats worth flagging

#2 — Market rewards composite architecture (Conf 3 / 5)

Tier does most of the work. The architecture-level claim is real within T1+T2 but cross-tier comparisons conflate "what does it do" with "what is this trying to be." Paired-sample n is 12–40 per axis value. The "vector + similarity is below median" headline is a baseline statement (n=62 paired with stars), not a claim about every system. Would tighten with a regression rather than median-ranking if pushed.

#7 — Capital concentration (Conf 4 / 5)

Confidence 4 not 5 because parent-company funding distorts attribution (LangChain's $160M attributed to its memory submodule, etc.). The 68× valuation gap (Devin $10.2B vs. Mem0 $150M peak equity) is rock-solid; the section-total figures are slightly inflated by parent-attribution.

Most actionable finding

The clearest "if you're building memory, do this" signal is #4 (network) — power-law structure tells you exactly which three integration shells to plug into: Mem0 plug-in, LangChain-native node, or vector-DB substrate. Highest actionability score paired with solid data. No fourth shape exists with comparable in-degree.
